To prevent this in future attempts, add `gasPrice: 2000000000000n` (2000 gwei) to the network config in hardhat.config.ts.

error_patterns.cause: Blockscout verification failed. Common causes: wrong constructor arguments, contract not yet indexed by Blockscout, or Solidity version mismatch between local compilation and the verification request.
error_patterns.resolution: 1. Verify the constructor argument matches what was used during deployment (check ignition/deployments/chain-420420417/journal.jsonl for the actual value). 2. Wait 30-60 seconds after deployment for Blockscout to index the contract, then retry. 3. Ensure the Solidity version in hardhat.config.ts matches the contract's pragma.

supplementary_context.description: These documentation pages provide background context for understanding the concepts behind each step. Consult them if you need to understand WHY something works, not HOW to do it. Note: resolved AI page versions (/ai/pages/) do not yet exist for the smart contracts section — these URLs point to the published docs pages which can still be fetched and read.

pages.relevance: The full tutorial this task is based on. Covers Hardhat initialization, configuration, and contract verification on both Blockscout and Routescan.
Network details for Polkadot Hub TestNet and MainNet including RPC endpoints, chain IDs, and wallet configuration. Consult if network connectivity or chain ID issues arise.

skills.title: Deploy Uniswap V2 on Polkadot Hub
skills.objective: Clone the Uniswap V2 Hardhat examples, configure Core and Periphery projects for Polkadot Hub TestNet, compile and test both locally and on-chain, then deploy the full Uniswap V2 protocol (Factory, WETH, and Router) to TestNet.

network: RPC endpoint: https://services.polkadothub-rpc.com/testnet (Polkadot Hub TestNet, chain ID 420420417)
Base fee: 1000 gwei — significantly higher than Ethereum. When troubleshooting stuck transactions or setting custom gas parameters, use this as the minimum base fee
tokens: Testnet PAS tokens from https://faucet.polkadot.io/ — the deploying account needs substantial PAS to cover gas for compiling, testing on-chain (tests deploy contracts), and deploying the full protocol (multiple contract deployments)
wallet: MetaMask or compatible EVM wallet with an exported private key (0x-prefixed) funded with testnet PAS tokens

env_vars.name: PRIVATE_KEY
env_vars.description: The 0x-prefixed private key of a funded Polkadot Hub TestNet account. Export from MetaMask (Account Details > Show Private Key) or another EVM wallet. Fund the corresponding address via https://faucet.polkadot.io/. This key is used for both Core and Periphery projects.

steps.description: Clone the full reference repository. This contains two Uniswap V2 projects: uniswap-v2-core-hardhat/ (Factory and Pair contracts) and uniswap-v2-periphery-hardhat/ (Router contracts). Unlike the simpler tasks which fetch individual files, this task clones the repo because it involves 25+ contract files across two interdependent projects.

Install dependencies for the Uniswap V2 Core project (Factory, Pair, and ERC20 contracts). This includes Hardhat v2, hardhat-toolbox (ethers.js v6, Mocha, Chai, TypeChain), and TypeScript. The dotenv package is needed for the config modification in step 3.
steps.expected_output: npm install completes without errors.

steps.description:
Open hardhat.config.ts (already present from the clone) and make the following substitutions:

1. Add `import "dotenv/config";` as the FIRST import line in the file (before any other imports).
2. Remove `vars` from the import statement (keep `HardhatUserConfig` and any other imports from "hardhat/config").
3. Replace the accounts conditional that uses `vars.has("TESTNET_PRIVATE_KEY")` / `vars.get("TESTNET_PRIVATE_KEY")` with: `accounts: process.env.PRIVATE_KEY ? [process.env.PRIVATE_KEY] : []`
4. The RPC URL for polkadotTestnet should already be `https://services.polkadothub-rpc.com/testnet`. If it is different, replace it.
5. Add `gasPrice: 5000000000000` (5000 gwei) to the polkadotTestnet network config. This is a PROACTIVE measure — Polkadot Hub TestNet has a 1000 gwei base fee, and the default gas estimation from ethers.js often falls below the node's minimum acceptance threshold. Setting an explicit gasPrice of 5x the base fee prevents 'Priority is too low' and 'Transaction Already Imported' errors that occur when replacement transactions don't meet the minimum price bump. The network config should include: `url, chainId, accounts, gasPrice: 5000000000000`.

Preserve all other config settings (solidity version, optimizer, other network configs, Mocha timeout).

Create a .env file with an empty PRIVATE_KEY= placeholder. Append .env to the existing .gitignore (use >> to preserve existing entries).

IMPORTANT: After creating these files, stop and ask the user to edit the .env file and paste their secret value. Do NOT ask the user to type or paste secrets in the chat — they should edit the .env file directly in their editor or file system. The value must be their 0x-prefixed private key. Wait for the user to confirm before proceeding.
Compile the Uniswap V2 Core contracts (UniswapV2Factory, UniswapV2Pair, UniswapV2ERC20, and supporting libraries). The contracts use Solidity 0.5.16.
steps.expected_output: Compilation succeeds. Expect multiple Solidity files compiled (the core includes ~12 contract files including interfaces and libraries).

steps.description: Run the Core test suite against Hardhat's built-in local network. This verifies the Factory, Pair, and ERC20 contract logic without needing a funded account or TestNet access.
steps.expected_output: All 29 tests pass, covering ERC20 operations (approve, transfer, permit), Factory operations (pair creation, fee management), and Pair operations (mint, burn, swap, price tracking).

steps.description: Install dependencies for the Uniswap V2 Periphery project (Router01, Router02, and WETH contracts). Same stack as Core: Hardhat v2, hardhat-toolbox, TypeScript.
steps.expected_output: npm install completes without errors.

steps.description:
Open hardhat.config.ts (already present from the clone) and make the same substitutions as step 3:

1. Add `import "dotenv/config";` as the FIRST import line.
2. Remove `vars` from the import statement.
3. Replace the accounts conditional with: `accounts: process.env.PRIVATE_KEY ? [process.env.PRIVATE_KEY] : []`
4. Verify the RPC URL is `https://services.polkadothub-rpc.com/testnet`.
5. Add `gasPrice: 5000000000000` (5000 gwei) to the polkadotTestnet network config, same as step 3. This prevents 'Priority is too low' errors during the gas-intensive Periphery deployment and testing.

Preserve all other config settings. Note: the Periphery config uses a multi-compiler setup (Solidity 0.5.16 and 0.6.6) and has `allowUnlimitedContractSize: true` on the hardhat network — both are required and must be preserved.
Copy the .env file from the Core project (which the user already filled in at step 4) so both projects use the same private key. Append .env to the Periphery's .gitignore.

Compile the Uniswap V2 Periphery contracts (UniswapV2Router01, UniswapV2Router02, WETH9, and supporting libraries/test helpers). Uses multi-compiler: Solidity 0.5.16 for legacy contracts and 0.6.6 for the routers.
steps.expected_output: Compilation succeeds. Expect multiple Solidity files compiled across two compiler versions (~13 contract files).

steps.description: Run the Periphery test suite against Hardhat's built-in local network. This verifies Router01 (38 tests covering liquidity management and swap types) and Router02 (12 tests covering fee-on-transfer token support).
steps.expected_output: All 50 tests pass.

steps.action: Run Core tests on Polkadot Hub TestNet
steps.description:
Run the Core test suite against the live Polkadot Hub TestNet. This validates that the contracts behave correctly on the actual chain.

Before running this command, confirm the user has added their private key to the .env file (step 4). The test account must be funded with PAS tokens since tests deploy contracts and execute transactions on-chain.

Note: TestNet tests are slower than local tests and consume gas. If the TestNet is unstable, the local test results from step 6 confirm the contract logic is sound. If tests time out, wait and retry.
steps.expected_output: All 29 Core tests pass against the live TestNet. Execution will be slower than the local run.

steps.action: Run Periphery tests on Polkadot Hub TestNet
steps.description:
Run the Periphery test suite against the live Polkadot Hub TestNet.

Before running, confirm the .env file contains the private key (copied in step 9). The Periphery tests are more extensive (50 tests) and deploy multiple contracts per test, so they consume more gas than Core tests.

Note: If tests time out due to TestNet congestion, the local test results from step 11 confirm the contract logic is sound.
steps.expected_output: All 50 Periphery tests pass against the live TestNet.

steps.action: Deploy Uniswap V2 Core (Factory) to TestNet
commands: npx hardhat ignition deploy ignition/modules/UniswapV2Factory.ts --network polkadotTestnet
steps.description:
Deploy the UniswapV2Factory contract using Hardhat Ignition. The Factory is the central registry that creates and manages trading pairs.

This command is INTERACTIVE — it will prompt for deployment confirmation. To run non-interactively: echo "y" | npx hardhat ignition deploy ignition/modules/UniswapV2Factory.ts --network polkadotTestnet

After successful deployment, save the Factory contract address — it is printed to the console and saved in ignition/deployments/chain-420420417/deployed_addresses.json.

DEPLOYMENT RECOVERY: Polkadot Hub TestNet can be unstable. If Ignition reports the transaction as 'dropped' or you encounter 'Transaction Already Imported' errors on retry, the original transaction may still be pending in the mempool. Do NOT immediately retry. Instead:
1. Wait at least 60-90 seconds for the transaction to be mined. The TestNet base fee is 1000 gwei — this is normal, not a gas pricing error.
2. Check if a contract address was written to ignition/deployments/chain-420420417/deployed_addresses.json.
3. If the deployment genuinely failed, delete the ignition/deployments/ directory entirely and re-run the deploy command.
steps.expected_output: Deployment completes and prints the UniswapV2Factory contract address.

steps.action: Deploy Uniswap V2 Periphery (WETH + Factory + Router) to TestNet
commands: npx hardhat ignition deploy ignition/modules/UniswapV2Router02.ts --network polkadotTestnet
steps.description:
Deploy the Periphery stack using Hardhat Ignition. The Periphery Ignition module deploys three contracts in sequence: WETH9 (wrapped native token), a UniswapV2Factory instance, and UniswapV2Router02 (the user-facing router that handles swaps and liquidity).

Note: The Periphery deploys its own Factory instance. In a production scenario, you would configure the Router to use the Core Factory from step 14. For this tutorial, each project is self-contained.

This command is INTERACTIVE — pipe confirmation: echo "y" | npx hardhat ignition deploy ignition/modules/UniswapV2Router02.ts --network polkadotTestnet

The Ignition module deploys multiple contracts, so this step involves multiple on-chain transactions. If any transaction fails mid-deployment, Ignition's journal tracks progress — you can re-run the same command to resume from where it left off.

DEPLOYMENT RECOVERY: Same guidance as step 14. If transactions are stuck, wait 60-90 seconds, check deployed_addresses.json, and delete ignition/deployments/ only if genuinely failed.
steps.expected_output: Deployment completes and prints addresses for WETH9, UniswapV2Factory, and UniswapV2Router02. All addresses are saved in ignition/deployments/chain-420420417/deployed_addresses.json.

files.description: Core project manifest with Hardhat v2, hardhat-toolbox, and TypeScript (CommonJS)
Core Hardhat v2 config with Solidity 0.5.16, optimizer, polkadotTestnet network, and Mocha timeout
Ignition module deploying UniswapV2Factory with deployer as fee setter
Periphery project manifest with Hardhat v2, hardhat-toolbox, and TypeScript (CommonJS)
Periphery Hardhat v2 config with multi-compiler (Solidity 0.5.16 + 0.6.6), allowUnlimitedContractSize, polkadotTestnet network
Ignition module deploying WETH9 → UniswapV2Factory → UniswapV2Router02 with dependency chain

error_patterns.cause: The account does not have enough PAS tokens. Uniswap V2 deployment and testing is gas-intensive — TestNet tests alone deploy multiple contracts per test run across 79 total tests.
error_patterns.resolution: Fund the account generously using the faucet at https://faucet.polkadot.io/. You may need to request tokens multiple times. Verify the correct address is funded.

error_patterns.cause: The RPC endpoint URL is incorrect, unreachable, or the TestNet is experiencing downtime.
error_patterns.resolution: Verify the RPC URL is exactly https://services.polkadothub-rpc.com/testnet in BOTH hardhat.config.ts files (core and periphery). Check the Polkadot status page or Discord for outage reports.

error_patterns.cause: The dotenv package was not installed in one of the projects.
error_patterns.resolution: Run npm install dotenv from the project directory that's throwing the error (uniswap-v2-core-hardhat/ or uniswap-v2-periphery-hardhat/).

error_patterns.cause: The hardhat.config.ts still uses Hardhat's Configuration Variables system instead of the dotenv pattern.
error_patterns.resolution: Verify that the substitutions from step 3 (core) or step 8 (periphery) were applied correctly: remove vars from the import, replace vars.has()/vars.get() with process.env.PRIVATE_KEY, and ensure import "dotenv/config" is the first line.

error_patterns.cause: A test timed out waiting for a transaction to be mined on the TestNet. Uniswap V2 tests are especially heavy — each test deploys multiple contracts.
error_patterns.resolution: Wait and retry. If timeouts persist, increase the Mocha timeout in the relevant hardhat.config.ts (e.g., from 40000 to 120000). Local tests passing (steps 6 and 11) confirm the contract logic is sound.

error_patterns.cause: The transaction's gas price or priority fee is below the network's minimum acceptance threshold. On Polkadot Hub TestNet (base fee 1000 gwei), the default gas estimation from ethers.js often produces values too low for the node to accept. This also occurs when retrying a failed transaction at the same nonce without sufficiently increasing the gas price (nodes typically require a 10%+ bump for replacement transactions).
error_patterns.resolution: Add an explicit gasPrice to the polkadotTestnet network config in hardhat.config.ts: `gasPrice: 5000000000000` (5000 gwei, 5x the base fee). This ensures all transactions clear with comfortable margin. Do NOT simply retry at the same gas price — that triggers 'Transaction Already Imported'. After updating the config, delete the ignition/deployments/ directory and redeploy.

error_patterns.cause: Hardhat Ignition reported a transaction as dropped and attempted to resubmit it, but the node's mempool still holds the original transaction.
error_patterns.resolution: Wait at least 60-90 seconds for the original transaction to be mined. Then delete the ignition/deployments/ directory in the relevant project and re-run the deployment command.

error_patterns.pattern: IgnitionError: You are trying to deploy to an in-process deployment
error_patterns.cause: A previous Hardhat Ignition deployment was interrupted and left an incomplete deployment journal.
error_patterns.resolution: Delete the ignition/deployments/ directory in the relevant project and re-run the deployment command.

error_patterns.pattern: IGN411: An error occurred while trying to send a transaction
error_patterns.cause: Hardhat Ignition encountered a transaction-level failure. The error message includes the nonce and sender address.
error_patterns.resolution: The error output includes the nonce and sender address. To find the transaction hash, run a Hardhat script: `await ethers.provider.getTransaction(await ethers.provider.getTransactionCount('SENDER_ADDRESS') - 1)`. Once you have the tx hash, run: `npx hardhat ignition track-tx <txHash> <deploymentId> --network polkadotTestnet`. If recovery fails, delete ignition/deployments/ and redeploy.

error_patterns.pattern: Transaction stuck: deployed_addresses.json is empty and eth_getCode returns 0x
error_patterns.cause: The deployment transaction is genuinely stuck in the mempool with insufficient gas price (base fee is 1000 gwei on Polkadot Hub TestNet).
error_patterns.resolution: Replace the stuck transaction by sending a zero-value transaction to your own address with the SAME nonce and a HIGHER gas price. Create and run a Hardhat script: `const [signer] = await ethers.getSigners(); const tx = await signer.sendTransaction({ to: signer.address, value: 0, nonce: STUCK_NONCE, gasPrice: ethers.parseUnits('2000', 'gwei') }); await tx.wait();` — replace STUCK_NONCE with the nonce from the error. After the replacement is mined, delete ignition/deployments/ and redeploy. To prevent recurrence, add `gasPrice: 2000000000000n` to the network config.

error_patterns.cause: Some Uniswap V2 contracts are complex and may hit Solidity's stack depth limit, especially with optimizer settings that don't match the original.
error_patterns.resolution: Ensure the optimizer is enabled with 200 runs (matching the reference config). Do not change the Solidity versions (0.5.16 for core, 0.5.16/0.6.6 for periphery) as the contracts are written for these specific versions.

supplementary_context.description: These documentation pages provide background context for understanding the Uniswap V2 protocol and its deployment on Polkadot. Consult them if you need to understand WHY something works, not HOW to do it.

pages.relevance: The full tutorial this task is based on. Covers AMM concepts, Uniswap V2 architecture (Factory/Pair/Router), and the porting rationale.
Hardhat setup and configuration reference for Polkadot Hub. Notes that Hardhat's standard test helpers may not work against Polkadot nodes.
Network details for Polkadot Hub TestNet including RPC endpoints, chain IDs, and wallet configuration.

skills.title: Set Up a Local Development Node
skills.objective: Clone the Polkadot SDK, compile the revive-dev-node and eth-rpc binaries, and run a local blockchain environment with an ETH-RPC adapter accessible at http://localhost:8545 for smart contract development without network fees or confirmation delays.

runtime: Rust (stable toolchain) — install via rustup at https://rustup.rs/
Polkadot SDK build dependencies — complete the Install Polkadot SDK Dependencies guide before proceeding

steps.description: Clone the Polkadot SDK monorepo from GitHub. It contains both the revive-dev-node and the pallet-revive-eth-rpc adapter used in subsequent steps.

Compile the revive-dev-node binary in release mode. This node runs a local blockchain with pallet-revive for smart contract support and pre-funded development accounts. The binary is output to polkadot-sdk/target/release/revive-dev-node. Release builds optimize for performance over compilation speed.
steps.expected_output: Compilation completes without errors. This step can take up to 30 minutes on first run.

steps.description: Compile the eth-rpc adapter binary in release mode. This adapter translates standard Ethereum JSON-RPC calls into Substrate-compatible requests, enabling familiar Ethereum tooling (MetaMask, Hardhat, Ethers.js) to work against the local node. The binary is output to polkadot-sdk/target/release/eth-rpc.
steps.expected_output: Compilation completes without errors. This step can also take up to 30 minutes on first run.

steps.description: Start the local blockchain node in development mode. The --dev flag initializes a single-node chain with pallet-revive enabled and pre-funded development accounts. Keep this terminal open — the node must remain running for the ETH-RPC adapter and development tools to connect.
steps.expected_output: Node starts and begins producing blocks. Log output confirms the node is running.

steps.description: Open a new terminal and start the ETH-RPC adapter in development mode. The adapter connects to the running local node and exposes a standard Ethereum JSON-RPC interface at http://localhost:8545. Keep this terminal open alongside the node terminal. Once running, tools like MetaMask, Hardhat, and Ethers.js can be pointed at http://localhost:8545 to interact with the local chain. Note: the local node exposes a subset of the Ethereum JSON-RPC API — some methods used by tools like Foundry or Hardhat may be unavailable or behave differently than on native Ethereum networks.
steps.expected_output: Adapter starts and listens on http://localhost:8545. Log output confirms the adapter is connected to the local node.

error_patterns.cause: A C/C++ linker is not installed, which is required to compile Rust projects with native dependencies.
error_patterns.resolution: Install build tools. On Ubuntu/Debian: sudo apt-get install build-essential. On macOS: xcode-select --install. Then retry the cargo build command.

error_patterns.cause: The Rust standard library target is missing for the active toolchain.
error_patterns.resolution: Run rustup target add wasm32-unknown-unknown and ensure all Polkadot SDK build dependencies from the prerequisite guide are fully installed. Then retry the cargo build command.

error_patterns.cause: Port 8545 is already occupied by another process — either a previous eth-rpc instance or another Ethereum node.
error_patterns.resolution: Stop the conflicting process. On Linux/macOS: lsof -ti:8545 | xargs kill -9. Then restart the eth-rpc adapter.

error_patterns.cause: The ETH-RPC adapter cannot connect to the local node. Either the node is not running or it has not finished starting.
error_patterns.resolution: Ensure revive-dev-node is running (step 4) and producing blocks before starting the eth-rpc adapter (step 5). Both must remain running simultaneously in separate terminals.

supplementary_context.description: These documentation pages provide background context for understanding the local development environment. Consult them if you need to understand WHY something works, not HOW to do it.

pages.relevance: The full guide this skill is based on. Consult for narrative context, optional debug logging configuration for both binaries, and details about the ETH-RPC API subset limitations.
Network details for Polkadot Hub TestNet and MainNet. Consult when transitioning from local development to deploying on a live network.

skills.title: Create a Polkadot Account Programmatically
skills.objective: Create a new Polkadot account with a 12-word mnemonic, SR25519 keypair, and SS58-encoded address using the Polkadot.js keyring library in a Node.js project.

steps.action: Create the project directory and initialize it
commands: cd account-creator && npm init -y && npm pkg set type=module
steps.description: Create a new directory called `account-creator` and initialize it as an ESM Node.js project. The `type=module` setting is required because the Polkadot.js libraries use ES module imports.

Install the Polkadot cryptographic utilities and keyring library. The `@polkadot/util-crypto` package provides mnemonic generation and WASM crypto initialization. The `@polkadot/keyring` package manages keypairs and address encoding. TypeScript and tsx are dev dependencies for running the script.
steps.expected_output: npm install completes without errors. Peer dependency warnings can be ignored.

The script prints an SS58-encoded address (starting with `1`) and a 12-word mnemonic phrase. Example:

Address: 15oF4uVJwmo4TdGW7VfQxNLavjCXviqWrztPu5CAcNTHmPvH
Mnemonic: word1 word2 word3 word4 word5 word6 word7 word8 word9 word10 word11 word12

The address and mnemonic will be different each run since a new keypair is generated.

error_patterns.cause: The `cryptoWaitReady()` call failed, likely due to a missing or incompatible WASM binary in the @polkadot/wasm-crypto package.
error_patterns.resolution: Ensure Node.js v18+ is installed. Delete `node_modules` and `package-lock.json`, then run `npm install` again. If the issue persists, check that the @polkadot/util-crypto version is compatible with your Node.js version.

error_patterns.pattern: SyntaxError: Cannot use import statement outside a module
error_patterns.cause: The project is not configured as an ES module. The `type` field in package.json is missing or set to `commonjs`.
error_patterns.resolution: Run `npm pkg set type=module` in the project directory to set `"type": "module"` in package.json.

error_patterns.cause: Dependencies are not installed or a package name is misspelled.
error_patterns.resolution: Run `npm install` in the project directory. Verify that `@polkadot/util-crypto` and `@polkadot/keyring` appear in `node_modules/`.

supplementary_context.description: Consult these pages for background on Polkadot account concepts, not for procedural steps.

pages.relevance: The full tutorial this skill is based on. Includes Python and Rust alternatives for account creation.
Reference on account data structures, balance types, SS58 address formats, and reference counters.

skills.title: Query Account Information with SDKs
skills.objective: Query System.Account storage on Polkadot Hub to retrieve nonce, consumers, providers, sufficients, free balance, reserved balance, and frozen balance for any account address using PAPI, Polkadot.js, Dedot, Python Substrate Interface, or Subxt.

runtime: Node.js v18+ and npm (for PAPI, Polkadot.js, Dedot)
Python 3.8+ and pip (for Python Substrate Interface)
Rust toolchain latest stable and Cargo (for Subxt)
network: WebSocket RPC endpoint for Polkadot Hub (e.g., wss://asset-hub-paseo.dotters.network for testnet)
wallet: A Polkadot account address in SS58 format to query — no funds required for read-only queries

steps.action: Choose your SDK and create the project
steps.description:
Select one of the five supported SDKs and create a new project directory. Each SDK variant is shown below.

**PAPI (TypeScript):**
```bash
mkdir papi-query-account-example && cd papi-query-account-example && npm init -y && npm pkg set type=module
npm install polkadot-api && npm install --save-dev @types/node tsx typescript
npx papi add polkadotTestNet -w wss://asset-hub-paseo.dotters.network
```

**Polkadot.js (JavaScript):**
```bash
mkdir pjs-query-account-example && cd pjs-query-account-example && npm init -y && npm pkg set type=module
npm install @polkadot/api
```

**Dedot (TypeScript):**
```bash
mkdir dedot-query-account-example && cd dedot-query-account-example && npm init -y && npm pkg set type=module
npm install dedot && npm install --save-dev @dedot/chaintypes @types/node tsx typescript
```

**Python Substrate Interface (Python):**
```bash
mkdir psi-query-account-example && cd psi-query-account-example && python3 -m venv venv && source venv/bin/activate
pip install substrate-interface
```

**Subxt (Rust):**
```bash
cargo new subxt-query-account-example && cd subxt-query-account-example
cargo install subxt-cli@0.50.0
subxt metadata --url INSERT_WS_ENDPOINT -o polkadot_testnet_metadata.scale
```
Replace INSERT_WS_ENDPOINT with your WebSocket endpoint (e.g., wss://asset-hub-paseo.dotters.network).
Then update Cargo.toml:
```toml
[package]
name = "subxt-query-account-example"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2021"

[[bin]]
name = "query_account"
path = "src/bin/query_account.rs"

[dependencies]
subxt = { version = "0.50.0" }
tokio = { version = "1.36.0", features = ["macros", "rt"] }
```
steps.expected_output: Project directory created with dependencies installed.
Script created successfully.

steps.action: Run the query script and verify output
steps.description: Run the script for your chosen SDK using the command shown in step 2. If querying a funded account, you should see non-zero free balance values. For accounts with no on-chain activity, Subxt returns None (not found) — use an account that has had at least one transaction.
steps.expected_output:
Output showing account information including nonce, consumers, providers, sufficients, and balance details (free, reserved, frozen balances in both raw units and PAS). Example:
Nonce: 5
Consumers: 0
Providers: 1
Sufficients: 0
Free Balance: 50000000000000 (5000 PAS)
Reserved Balance: 0 (0 PAS)
Frozen Balance: 0 (0 PAS)

error_patterns.cause: The WebSocket endpoint is unreachable, incorrect, or the testnet is temporarily down.
error_patterns.resolution: Verify the WebSocket URL is correct. Use wss://asset-hub-paseo.dotters.network for Polkadot Hub TestNet. Check the Polkadot Discord status channel if the endpoint is unresponsive.

error_patterns.pattern: Account not found / fetch returns None (Subxt)
error_patterns.cause: The queried account has no on-chain storage entry — it has never had a balance or sent a transaction.
error_patterns.resolution: Use an account that has had on-chain activity. Accounts with zero balance that have never transacted will not have a storage entry in the System.Account map.

error_patterns.cause: ESM/CommonJS module conflict or missing dependency.
error_patterns.resolution: Ensure package.json has '"type": "module"' set (run `npm pkg set type=module`). Reinstall dependencies with npm install.

error_patterns.pattern: Cannot find type definition / type descriptor not generated
error_patterns.cause: PAPI type descriptors not generated, or Dedot chaintypes not installed.
error_patterns.resolution: For PAPI: run `npx papi add polkadotTestNet -w wss://asset-hub-paseo.dotters.network` to generate descriptors. For Dedot: ensure @dedot/chaintypes is installed (`npm install --save-dev @dedot/chaintypes`).

error_patterns.cause: The metadata file path in the #[subxt::subxt(runtime_metadata_path = ...)] macro does not match the actual file location.
error_patterns.resolution: Ensure polkadot_testnet_metadata.scale was downloaded to the project root (same directory as Cargo.toml) and the path in the macro matches exactly.

supplementary_context.description: Consult these pages for background on Polkadot account data structures and the fields returned by System.Account queries.

pages.relevance: Documents account data structures, balance types (free, reserved, frozen), reference counters (consumers, providers, sufficients), and SS58 address formats — essential background for interpreting query results.
Explains runtime metadata, storage map queries, and how System.Account is structured in the chain's storage.

skills.title: Deploy a Basic Contract with Remix IDE
skills.objective: Deploy the default Storage.sol smart contract to Polkadot Hub TestNet using Remix IDE and MetaMask, verifying the deployment transaction on-chain.

network: MetaMask wallet connected to Polkadot Hub TestNet (RPC: https://services.polkadothub-rpc.com/testnet, Chain ID: 420420417)
tokens: PAS test tokens from https://faucet.polkadot.io/
wallet: MetaMask or any EVM-compatible browser wallet

steps.description: Open your browser and navigate to https://remix.ethereum.org/. Wait for the default workspace to load. You should see a file explorer on the left with a 'contracts' folder containing sample contracts.
steps.expected_output: Remix IDE loads with a default workspace containing a 'contracts' folder.

steps.description: In the Remix file explorer (left panel), expand the 'contracts' folder. Click on 'Storage.sol' to open it in the editor. This is a simple contract that stores and retrieves a uint256 value. Review the code to understand its store() and retrieve() functions.
steps.expected_output: Storage.sol is open in the Remix editor showing a contract with store(uint256) and retrieve() functions.

steps.description:
1. Click the 'Solidity Compiler' plugin icon in the left panel (it looks like a document with an 'S').
2. Ensure the compiler version is 0.8.x (matching the pragma in Storage.sol).
3. Click the 'Compile Storage.sol' button.
4. Wait for compilation to complete — a green checkmark will appear on the Solidity Compiler icon when successful.

If you see errors, check the terminal panel at the bottom of the screen for details.
steps.expected_output: Green checkmark appears on the Solidity Compiler icon. No errors in the terminal.

steps.action: Connect MetaMask to Polkadot Hub TestNet
steps.description:
Before deploying, ensure MetaMask is connected to Polkadot Hub TestNet:
1. Open MetaMask in your browser.
2. Click the network dropdown at the top.
3. Add a custom network with these settings:
   - Network Name: Polkadot Hub TestNet
   - RPC URL: https://services.polkadothub-rpc.com/testnet
   - Chain ID: 420420417
   - Currency Symbol: PAS
4. Switch to this network.
5. Ensure your account has PAS test tokens (get them from https://faucet.polkadot.io/ if needed).
steps.expected_output: MetaMask shows 'Polkadot Hub TestNet' as the active network with a PAS balance.

steps.description:
1. Click 'Deploy & Run Transactions' in the left panel (icon with an arrow and document).
2. In the 'Environment' dropdown, select 'Injected Provider - MetaMask'. Remix will connect to your MetaMask wallet.
3. Verify the Account field shows your MetaMask address and a PAS balance.
4. Ensure 'Storage' is selected in the Contract dropdown.
5. Click the 'Deploy' button.
6. MetaMask will pop up asking you to confirm the transaction. Review the gas fee and click 'Confirm'.
7. Wait for the transaction to be mined.

After deployment succeeds, the terminal at the bottom will display the transaction hash and contract address. Your contract will also appear under 'Deployed Contracts' in the left panel.
steps.expected_output: Terminal shows a green checkmark with transaction details including contract address and transaction hash. The contract appears under 'Deployed Contracts'.

steps.description:
1. Copy the deployed contract address from the 'Deployed Contracts' section or from the terminal output.
2. Open https://blockscout-testnet.polkadot.io/ in a new browser tab.
3. Paste the contract address in the search bar and press Enter.
4. You should see your contract's deployment transaction and details.

Optionally, back in Remix, expand the deployed contract to see its functions. Try calling 'store' with a value like 42 (confirm in MetaMask), then call 'retrieve' to read it back.
steps.expected_output: Blockscout shows the deployed contract. Calling store(42) then retrieve() returns 42.

error_patterns.cause: Insufficient PAS tokens for gas or contract constructor failure.
error_patterns.resolution: Ensure your MetaMask wallet has PAS test tokens from https://faucet.polkadot.io/. Polkadot Hub TestNet has a base fee of 1000 gwei which is much higher than Ethereum.

error_patterns.cause: MetaMask is not installed or the browser extension is disabled.
error_patterns.resolution: Install MetaMask from https://metamask.io/ and ensure the extension is enabled. Refresh Remix IDE after installation.

error_patterns.cause: MetaMask is connected to a different network than Polkadot Hub TestNet.
error_patterns.resolution: Switch MetaMask to Polkadot Hub TestNet (Chain ID: 420420417, RPC: https://services.polkadothub-rpc.com/testnet).

supplementary_context.description: Additional Polkadot smart contract deployment resources

General Remix IDE setup and usage with Polkadot Hub

skills.title: Deploy an ERC-20 Token Using Remix IDE
skills.objective: Create and deploy an ERC-20 fungible token contract to Polkadot Hub TestNet using Remix IDE, then mint tokens and verify balances.

network: MetaMask wallet connected to Polkadot Hub TestNet (RPC: https://services.polkadothub-rpc.com/testnet, Chain ID: 420420417)
tokens: PAS test tokens from https://faucet.polkadot.io/
wallet: MetaMask or any EVM-compatible browser wallet

steps.description: Open your browser and navigate to https://remix.ethereum.org/. Wait for the default workspace to load.
steps.expected_output: Remix IDE loads with the default workspace.
MyToken.sol file is created in the contracts folder with the ERC-20 contract code.

steps.description:
1. Click the 'Solidity Compiler' plugin icon in the left panel.
2. Ensure the compiler version is 0.8.22 or later (matching the pragma).
3. Click 'Compile MyToken.sol'.
4. Wait for the green checkmark on the compiler icon.

Remix will automatically resolve the OpenZeppelin imports from npm. If you see import errors, try clicking 'Compile' again — Remix sometimes needs a moment to fetch dependencies.
steps.expected_output: Green checkmark on the Solidity Compiler icon. No errors in the terminal.

steps.action: Connect MetaMask to Polkadot Hub TestNet
steps.description:
Ensure MetaMask is connected to Polkadot Hub TestNet:
1. Open MetaMask and click the network dropdown.
2. Add or switch to the custom network:
   - Network Name: Polkadot Hub TestNet
   - RPC URL: https://services.polkadothub-rpc.com/testnet
   - Chain ID: 420420417
   - Currency Symbol: PAS
3. Ensure your account has PAS test tokens (get them from https://faucet.polkadot.io/).
steps.expected_output: MetaMask shows 'Polkadot Hub TestNet' as the active network with a PAS balance.

steps.description:
1. Click 'Deploy & Run Transactions' in the left panel.
2. In the 'Environment' dropdown, select 'Injected Provider - MetaMask'.
3. Ensure 'MyToken' is selected in the Contract dropdown.
4. In the deployment parameters field (next to the Deploy button), enter your MetaMask wallet address as the 'initialOwner' parameter. This address will have permission to mint tokens.
5. Click 'Deploy'.
6. Confirm the transaction in MetaMask.
7. Wait for the transaction to be mined.

Save the deployed contract address from the terminal output — you will need it for minting tokens.
steps.expected_output: Terminal shows successful deployment with contract address and transaction hash. Contract appears under 'Deployed Contracts'.

steps.action: Mint tokens using the deployed contract
steps.description:
1. Under 'Deployed/Unpinned Contracts', click your MyToken contract to expand its functions.
2. Find and expand the 'mint' function.
3. Enter two parameters:
   - 'to': your MetaMask wallet address (the recipient)
   - 'amount': the number of tokens to mint in wei (for 1 whole token with 18 decimals, enter 1000000000000000000)
4. Click 'transact'.
5. Confirm the transaction in MetaMask.
6. Wait for the green checkmark in the terminal.

To verify the mint worked:
7. Expand the 'balanceOf' function.
8. Enter the same address you minted to.
9. Click 'call'.
10. The result should show the minted balance.
steps.expected_output: balanceOf returns the minted amount (e.g., 1000000000000000000 for 1 token).

files.description: ERC-20 token contract using OpenZeppelin with mintable and permit extensions

error_patterns.cause: Insufficient PAS tokens or invalid constructor argument.
error_patterns.resolution: Ensure your wallet has PAS tokens and that you provided a valid Ethereum address as the initialOwner constructor parameter.

error_patterns.pattern: Ownable: caller is not the owner
error_patterns.cause: Trying to mint from an address that is not the initialOwner.
error_patterns.resolution: Only the address passed as initialOwner during deployment can call the mint function. Connect with the correct account in MetaMask.

error_patterns.pattern: Import error or file not found
error_patterns.cause: OpenZeppelin imports cannot be resolved by Remix.
error_patterns.resolution: Remix should auto-resolve npm imports. Try recompiling. If the issue persists, ensure you are connected to the internet and Remix can fetch packages.

skills.title: Deploy an ERC-721 NFT Using Hardhat
skills.objective: Deploy an ERC-721 NFT contract to Polkadot Hub TestNet using Hardhat and OpenZeppelin, with Hardhat Ignition for deployment.

network: Polkadot Hub TestNet (RPC: https://services.polkadothub-rpc.com/testnet, Chain ID: 420420417)
tokens: PAS test tokens from https://faucet.polkadot.io/
wallet: A wallet private key for signing deployment transactions

env_vars.name: PRIVATE_KEY
env_vars.description: Private key of the deployer wallet (without 0x prefix). Used to sign the deployment transaction. Fund this wallet with PAS tokens first.

steps.action: Create and initialize the Hardhat project
steps.description: Create a new directory and initialize a Hardhat project. When prompted by `npx hardhat init`, select 'Create a TypeScript project', accept the default project root, and say yes to installing dependencies.
steps.expected_output: Hardhat project initialized with contracts/, ignition/, test/, and hardhat.config.ts files.

steps.description: Install the OpenZeppelin contracts library which provides the battle-tested ERC-721 implementation.
steps.expected_output: OpenZeppelin contracts package installed successfully.

steps.description:
Create a .env file to store your private key securely. Do NOT ask the user to type or paste their private key in the chat — they should edit the .env file directly.

Create the file with this content:

```
PRIVATE_KEY=INSERT_YOUR_PRIVATE_KEY_HERE
```

Also install dotenv:

```bash
npm install dotenv
```

And add .env to .gitignore to prevent committing secrets:

```bash
echo '.env' >> .gitignore
```
steps.expected_output: dotenv installed and .env added to .gitignore.

steps.action: Configure Hardhat for Polkadot Hub TestNet
steps.expected_output: hardhat.config.ts is configured with Polkadot Hub TestNet network and dotenv-based private key.
contracts/MyNFT.sol created with the ERC-721 NFT contract.

steps.description: Compile the MyNFT.sol contract using Hardhat.
steps.expected_output: Compiled 1 Solidity file successfully (or similar message confirming compilation).

steps.action: Create the Hardhat Ignition deployment module
steps.expected_output: ignition/modules/MyNFT.ts created with the deployment module.

steps.action: Deploy the NFT contract to Polkadot Hub TestNet
steps.description:
Deploy the contract using Hardhat Ignition. When prompted to confirm the deployment network and chain ID, type 'yes'.

Note: The requiredConfirmations for Polkadot Hub TestNet must be 1 or higher (not 0).
commands: npx hardhat ignition deploy ignition/modules/MyNFT.ts --network polkadotTestnet
steps.expected_output: Deployment succeeds showing the deployed contract address, e.g., 'MyNFTModule#MyNFT - 0x...'.

files.description: ERC-721 NFT contract with auto-incrementing token ID and owner-only minting
Hardhat configuration with Polkadot Hub TestNet network. Substitutions: replace vars.get('PRIVATE_KEY') with process.env.PRIVATE_KEY, add dotenv/config import, add evmVersion cancun
Hardhat Ignition deployment module for MyNFT contract

error_patterns.cause: The deployer wallet does not have enough PAS tokens to pay for gas.
error_patterns.resolution: Fund your wallet with PAS test tokens from https://faucet.polkadot.io/. Note that Polkadot Hub TestNet has a base fee of 1000 gwei.

error_patterns.cause: dotenv package is not installed.
error_patterns.resolution: Run: npm install dotenv

error_patterns.pattern: InvalidArgumentError: Expected a hex-encoded private key
error_patterns.cause: PRIVATE_KEY in .env is empty or malformed.
error_patterns.resolution: Edit .env directly (do not paste keys in chat) and ensure PRIVATE_KEY contains a valid hex private key without the 0x prefix.

error_patterns.pattern: Error HH12: Trying to use a non-local installation of Hardhat
error_patterns.cause: Hardhat is not installed locally in the project.
error_patterns.resolution: Run: npm install --save-dev hardhat

error_patterns.cause: OpenZeppelin v5.4.0+ requires evmVersion cancun but it is not set.
error_patterns.resolution: Add evmVersion: 'cancun' to the solidity.settings in hardhat.config.ts.

skills.title: Set Up Foundry and Deploy a Contract on Polkadot Hub
skills.objective: Install Foundry nightly, initialize a project, configure it for Polkadot Hub TestNet, deploy the default Counter contract, and interact with it using Cast.

runtime: Unix-based OS (Linux/macOS) or Windows with WSL
network: Polkadot Hub TestNet (RPC: https://services.polkadothub-rpc.com/testnet, Chain ID: 420420417)
tokens: PAS test tokens from https://faucet.polkadot.io/
wallet: A wallet private key for signing deployment transactions

env_vars.name: PRIVATE_KEY
env_vars.description: Private key of the deployer wallet (hex, with or without 0x prefix). Used by forge create and cast send to sign transactions.

steps.description: Install foundryup (the Foundry toolchain installer) and then install the nightly build, which is required for Polkadot Hub support including native chain flags and contract verification.
steps.expected_output: foundryup installed, then nightly versions of forge, cast, anvil, and chisel are installed. Verify with: forge --version (should show nightly build).

steps.description: Create a new Foundry project with the default Counter contract.
steps.expected_output: Project created with src/, script/, test/, lib/ directories, foundry.toml config, and a Counter.sol contract in src/.

steps.description: Compile all contracts in the src/ directory.
steps.expected_output: Compiler run successful! Artifacts output to the out/ directory.

steps.action: Configure Foundry for Polkadot Hub TestNet
steps.description:
Replace the contents of foundry.toml with the following configuration. This sets the Solidity compiler version and configures Blockscout for contract verification:

```toml
[profile.default]
src = "src"
out = "out"
libs = ["lib"]
solc_version = "0.8.28"

[etherscan]
polkadot-testnet = { key = "", url = "https://blockscout-testnet.polkadot.io/api?" }
```

Foundry's nightly build has native support for --chain polkadot-testnet, so you do not need to specify the RPC URL in the config.
steps.expected_output: foundry.toml configured with Blockscout verification endpoint.

steps.description:
Create a .env file to store your private key. Do NOT ask the user to type or paste their private key in the chat — they should edit the .env file directly.

Create the file with this content:

```
PRIVATE_KEY=INSERT_PRIVATE_KEY_HERE
```

Add .env to .gitignore:

```bash
echo '.env' >> .gitignore
```

Load the environment variables before deploying:

```bash
source .env
```
steps.expected_output: .env file created with PRIVATE_KEY placeholder, added to .gitignore, and sourced.

steps.action: Deploy the Counter contract to Polkadot Hub TestNet
steps.description: Deploy using forge create with the native --chain polkadot-testnet flag. Save the 'Deployed to' address from the output — you will use it in the next step to interact with the contract.
steps.expected_output: Deployer address, deployed contract address, and transaction hash are printed. Example: 'Deployed to: 0x...'

steps.action: Interact with the deployed contract using Cast
steps.description:
Use cast to read and write the deployed Counter contract. Replace INSERT_CONTRACT_ADDRESS with the address from step 6.

Read the current counter value:
```bash
cast call INSERT_CONTRACT_ADDRESS "number()(uint256)" --chain polkadot-testnet
```

Set the counter to 42:
```bash
cast send INSERT_CONTRACT_ADDRESS "setNumber(uint256)" 42 --chain polkadot-testnet --private-key $PRIVATE_KEY
```

Increment the counter:
```bash
cast send INSERT_CONTRACT_ADDRESS "increment()" --chain polkadot-testnet --private-key $PRIVATE_KEY
```

Read the value again to confirm:
```bash
cast call INSERT_CONTRACT_ADDRESS "number()(uint256)" --chain polkadot-testnet
```
steps.expected_output: First read returns 0, after setNumber(42) the read returns 42.

steps.description:
Verify the deployed contract on Blockscout so its source code is publicly viewable. Replace INSERT_CONTRACT_ADDRESS with your deployed address.

```bash
forge verify-contract INSERT_CONTRACT_ADDRESS src/Counter.sol:Counter --chain polkadot-testnet
```

Blockscout does not require an API key. Foundry's native Polkadot support handles the verifier URL automatically.
steps.expected_output: Verification submitted successfully with a Blockscout URL to view the verified contract.

error_patterns.cause: Using stable Foundry release instead of the nightly build.
error_patterns.resolution: Install the nightly build: foundryup --version nightly

error_patterns.cause: Deployer wallet does not have enough PAS tokens.
error_patterns.resolution: Fund your wallet from https://faucet.polkadot.io/. Polkadot Hub TestNet base fee is 1000 gwei.

error_patterns.cause: PRIVATE_KEY environment variable is not set or malformed.
error_patterns.resolution: Ensure .env contains a valid hex private key and run: source .env

error_patterns.cause: Cannot connect to the Polkadot Hub TestNet RPC endpoint.
error_patterns.resolution: Verify the RPC URL: https://services.polkadothub-rpc.com/testnet. Check your internet connection.

skills.title: Query On-Chain State with SDKs
skills.objective: Query on-chain storage data (account balances and asset information) on Polkadot Hub using PAPI, Polkadot.js, Dedot, Python Substrate Interface, or Subxt.

runtime: Node.js v18+ and npm (for PAPI, Polkadot.js, Dedot)
Python 3.8+ and pip (for Py Substrate Interface)
Rust toolchain latest stable and Cargo (for Subxt)
network: WebSocket RPC endpoint for Polkadot Hub (e.g., wss://polkadot-asset-hub-rpc.polkadot.io for mainnet or wss://asset-hub-paseo.dotters.network for testnet)
wallet: A Polkadot account address (SS58 format) to query — no funds required for read-only queries

steps.action: Choose your SDK and create the project
steps.description:
Select one of the five supported SDKs and create a new project directory. Each SDK variant is shown below.

**PAPI (TypeScript):**
```bash
mkdir papi-query-example && cd papi-query-example && npm init -y && npm pkg set type=module
npm install polkadot-api && npm install --save-dev @types/node tsx typescript
npx papi add pah -n polkadot_asset_hub
```

**Polkadot.js (JavaScript):**
```bash
mkdir pjs-query-example && cd pjs-query-example && npm init -y && npm pkg set type=module
npm install @polkadot/api
```

**Dedot (TypeScript):**
```bash
mkdir dedot-query-example && cd dedot-query-example && npm init -y && npm pkg set type=module
npm install dedot && npm install --save-dev @dedot/chaintypes @types/node tsx typescript
```

**Py Substrate Interface (Python):**
```bash
mkdir psi-query-example && cd psi-query-example && python3 -m venv venv && source venv/bin/activate
pip install substrate-interface
```

**Subxt (Rust):**
```bash
cargo new subxt-query-example && cd subxt-query-example
cargo install subxt-cli
subxt metadata --url INSERT_WS_ENDPOINT -o asset_hub_metadata.scale
```
Then update Cargo.toml with subxt, subxt-signer, and tokio dependencies.
steps.expected_output: Project directory created with dependencies installed.

steps.description:
Create the balance query script for your chosen SDK. The script connects to the chain via WebSocket, queries System.Account storage for a given address, and prints the free, reserved, and frozen balances.

Replace INSERT_WS_ENDPOINT with your WebSocket endpoint (e.g., wss://polkadot-asset-hub-rpc.polkadot.io) and INSERT_ADDRESS with the SS58 account address to query.

**PAPI:** Create query-balance.ts, run with `npx tsx query-balance.ts`
**Polkadot.js:** Create query-balance.js, run with `node query-balance.js`
**Dedot:** Create query-balance.ts, run with `npx tsx query-balance.ts`
**Python:** Create query_balance.py, run with `python query_balance.py`
**Subxt:** Create src/bin/query_balance.rs, run with `cargo run --bin query_balance`

See the source documentation for the complete code for each SDK variant.
steps.expected_output: Output showing account balance information: free balance, reserved balance, frozen balance, and flags.

steps.description:
Create the asset query script for your chosen SDK. The script queries the Assets pallet for metadata and balance information for USDT (asset ID 1984).

Replace INSERT_WS_ENDPOINT with your WebSocket endpoint and INSERT_ADDRESS with the SS58 account address.

**PAPI:** Create query-asset.ts, run with `npx tsx query-asset.ts`
**Polkadot.js:** Create query-asset.js, run with `node query-asset.js`
**Dedot:** Create query-asset.ts, run with `npx tsx query-asset.ts`
**Python:** Create query_asset.py, run with `python query_asset.py`
**Subxt:** Create src/bin/query_asset.rs, run with `cargo run --bin query_asset`

See the source documentation for the complete code for each SDK variant.

skills.title: Calculate Transaction Fees on Polkadot
skills.objective: Estimate transaction fees for a balance transfer on Polkadot using Polkadot-API (PAPI) or Polkadot.js API before submitting the transaction.

network: WebSocket RPC endpoint (e.g., wss://pas-rpc.stakeworld.io/assethub for Polkadot TestNet)
wallet: Two Polkadot account addresses (sender and recipient) in SS58 format — no funds required for fee estimation as a dummy signature is used

steps.description:
Create a new project directory and install dependencies for your chosen SDK.

```bash
mkdir fee-calculator && cd fee-calculator
npm init -y && npm pkg set type=module
```

**For PAPI:**
```bash
npm install polkadot-api
npm install --save-dev typescript tsx
npx papi add polkadotTestNet -w INSERT_WS_ENDPOINT
```
Replace INSERT_WS_ENDPOINT with your WebSocket endpoint (e.g., wss://pas-rpc.stakeworld.io/assethub).

**For Polkadot.js:**
```bash
npm install @polkadot/api
```
commands: mkdir fee-calculator && cd fee-calculator && npm init -y && npm pkg set type=module
steps.expected_output: Project initialized with package.json created.

steps.action: Create the fee calculation script (PAPI variant)
steps.description:
Create a file named papi-fee-calculator.ts. The script:
1. Connects to the chain via WebSocket using createClient and getWsProvider.
2. Creates a Balances.transfer_keep_alive transaction with a MultiAddress (Id variant) dest and a value amount.
3. Calls getEstimatedFees(senderAddress) which internally applies a dummy signature to simulate the transaction.
4. Returns the estimated fee in plancks (1 DOT = 10^10 plancks).

Replace INSERT_WS_ENDPOINT with your WebSocket endpoint, INSERT_ALICE_ADDRESS with the sender's address, and INSERT_BOB_ADDRESS with the recipient's address.

Run with:
```bash
npx tsx papi-fee-calculator.ts
```

See the source documentation for complete code.
steps.expected_output: Output showing estimated fee in plancks, e.g., 'Estimated fee: 1234567890 plancks'.

steps.action: Create the fee calculation script (Polkadot.js variant)
steps.description:
Create a file named polkadotjs-fee-calculator.ts. The script:
1. Connects using WsProvider and ApiPromise.
2. Creates a balances.transferKeepAlive transaction.
3. Calls paymentInfo(senderAddress) which applies a dummy signature and queries the RPC for fee estimation.
4. Returns the partialFee property containing the estimated fee in plancks.

Replace INSERT_WS_ENDPOINT with your WebSocket endpoint, INSERT_ALICE_ADDRESS with the sender's address, and INSERT_BOB_ADDRESS with the recipient's address.

Run with:
```bash
npx tsx polkadotjs-fee-calculator.ts
```

See the source documentation for complete code.
steps.expected_output: Output showing estimated fee details including partialFee, weight, and class.

error_patterns.cause: The WebSocket endpoint is unreachable or incorrect.
error_patterns.resolution: Verify the WebSocket URL. Use wss://pas-rpc.stakeworld.io/assethub for Polkadot TestNet.

error_patterns.cause: The PAPI descriptors were not generated or the chain name is incorrect.
error_patterns.resolution: Re-run 'npx papi add polkadotTestNet -w <WS_ENDPOINT>' to regenerate type descriptors.

error_patterns.cause: The provided SS58 address is malformed or for the wrong network.
error_patterns.resolution: Ensure the address is a valid SS58-encoded Polkadot address. Use a known valid address for testing.

supplementary_context.description: Reference material on how transaction fees are calculated (base fee + length fee + weight fee + optional tip).

pages.relevance: Explains the transaction format, extrinsic structure, and how transactions are processed — context for understanding what affects fee calculation.
Detailed reference on the fee model: base fee, length fee, weight fee, tip, and how they are computed on Polkadot SDK chains.

skills.objective: Construct, sign, and submit a balance transfer transaction on Polkadot Hub using PAPI, Polkadot.js, Dedot, Python Substrate Interface, or Subxt.

runtime: Node.js v18+ and npm (for PAPI, Polkadot.js, Dedot)
Python 3.8+ and pip (for Py Substrate Interface)

network: WebSocket RPC endpoint for Polkadot Hub (e.g., wss://polkadot-asset-hub-rpc.polkadot.io for mainnet or wss://asset-hub-paseo.dotters.network for testnet)

env_vars.description: The mnemonic (seed phrase) for the sending account. Must have sufficient balance to cover the transfer amount and fees.

steps.action: Create and run the balance transfer script

Create the send-transfer script for your chosen SDK. The script:

2. Creates a keypair/signer from the mnemonic.
3. Connects to the chain via WebSocket.
4. Constructs a Balances.transfer_keep_alive transaction with the destination address and amount.
5. Signs and submits the transaction.
6. Watches for finalization and reports the result.

Replace INSERT_WS_ENDPOINT with your WebSocket endpoint, INSERT_SENDER_MNEMONIC with the env var reference (e.g., process.env.SENDER_MNEMONIC), and INSERT_DEST_ADDRESS with the recipient's SS58 address.

**PAPI:** Create send-transfer.ts, run with `npx tsx send-transfer.ts`
**Polkadot.js:** Create send-transfer.js, run with `node send-transfer.js`
**Dedot:** Create send-transfer.ts, run with `npx tsx send-transfer.ts`
**Python:** Create send_transfer.py, run with `python send_transfer.py`
**Subxt:** Create src/bin/send_transfer.rs, run with `cargo run --bin send_transfer`

See the source documentation for the complete code for each SDK variant.

error_patterns.pattern: Error: 1010: Invalid Transaction: Inability to pay some fees
error_patterns.cause: The sending account does not have enough balance to cover the transfer amount plus fees.
error_patterns.resolution: Fund the sender account using the faucet at https://faucet.polkadot.io/?parachain=1111 or reduce the transfer amount.

error_patterns.pattern: Error: 1010: Invalid Transaction: Transaction is outdated
error_patterns.cause: The transaction's mortality period has expired or the nonce is stale.
error_patterns.resolution: Recreate and resubmit the transaction. Ensure the nonce is current.

error_patterns.cause: The mnemonic phrase is malformed or contains incorrect words.
error_patterns.resolution: Verify the mnemonic is a valid BIP39 phrase (12 or 24 words). Check for typos.

error_patterns.cause: WASM cryptographic libraries not initialized before creating keyring (Polkadot.js/Dedot).
error_patterns.resolution: Call await cryptoWaitReady() before creating any keyring or signer instances.

error_patterns.cause: ESM/CommonJS module conflict.
error_patterns.resolution: Ensure package.json has '"type": "module"' set and all imports use ES module syntax.

skills.title: Pay Transaction Fees with Alternative Tokens
skills.objective: Send a DOT transfer transaction on Polkadot Hub while paying transaction fees in USDT (or another alternative token) using PAPI, Polkadot.js, or Subxt with a local Chopsticks fork.

runtime: Node.js v18+ and npm (for PAPI, Polkadot.js)
Rust toolchain latest stable and Cargo (for Subxt)
network: Local Chopsticks fork of Polkadot Hub at ws://localhost:8000
tokens: Alice's dev account (pre-funded with DOT and USDT on Chopsticks fork)
wallet: Alice's dev account (available by default on Chopsticks fork with the polkadot-asset-hub config)

steps.action: Start a local Polkadot Hub fork with Chopsticks
steps.description:
Fork the Polkadot Hub chain locally using Chopsticks. This creates a local chain replica with Alice's pre-funded account (DOT + USDT).

```bash
npx @nicepkg/chopsticks -c polkadot-asset-hub
```

Alternatively, if Chopsticks is installed globally:
```bash
chopsticks -c polkadot-asset-hub
```

This makes the chain available at ws://localhost:8000. The polkadot-asset-hub.yml configuration pre-configures Alice's account with USDT assets. Keep this terminal running.
steps.expected_output: Chopsticks outputs a message like 'Listening on ws://localhost:8000' indicating the fork is running.

steps.action: Set up the project for your chosen SDK
steps.description:
In a new terminal, create the project.

**PAPI (TypeScript):**
```bash
mkdir fee-payment-tutorial && cd fee-payment-tutorial
npm init -y
npm install --save-dev @types/node ts-node typescript
npm install @polkadot-labs/hdkd @polkadot-labs/hdkd-helpers polkadot-api
npx tsc --init && npm pkg set type=module
npx papi add assetHub -n polkadot_asset_hub
```

**Polkadot.js (JavaScript):**
```bash
mkdir fee-payment-tutorial && cd fee-payment-tutorial
npm init -y && npm pkg set type=module
npm install @polkadot/api
```

**Subxt (Rust):**
```bash
cargo new subxt-fee-payment-example && cd subxt-fee-payment-example
cargo install subxt-cli
mkdir metadata && subxt metadata --url ws://localhost:8000 -o metadata/asset_hub.scale
```
Update Cargo.toml with subxt, subxt-signer, and tokio dependencies.

Note for Subxt: ensure the Chopsticks fork is running at ws://localhost:8000 before downloading metadata.
steps.expected_output: Project created with dependencies installed.

steps.action: Create the fee payment transaction script
steps.description:
Create the transaction script that sends a DOT transfer while paying fees in USDT. The implementation has four key parts:

1. **Import dependencies and define constants** — set up the target address (Bob's), transfer amount (3 DOT), and USDT asset ID (1984).

2. **Create a signer and connect** — use Alice's dev account mnemonic and connect to ws://localhost:8000.
   - For Polkadot.js: call cryptoWaitReady() first to initialize WASM crypto libraries.

3. **Create the transaction** — construct a Balances.transfer_keep_alive call.

4. **Sign and submit with alternative fee payment** — the critical step specifying USDT as the fee asset using XCM location format:
   - PalletInstance(50) refers to the Assets pallet
   - GeneralIndex(1984) identifies USDT
   - In PAPI: pass the `asset` parameter in signAndSubmit options
   - In Polkadot.js: define an XCM multi-location object as `assetId` option to signAndSend
   - In Subxt: use DefaultExtrinsicParamsBuilder with tip_of(0, asset_location)

**PAPI:** Create fee-payment-transaction.ts, run with `npx ts-node fee-payment-transaction.ts`
**Polkadot.js:** Create fee-payment-transaction.js, run with `node fee-payment-transaction.js`
**Subxt:** Create src/bin/fee_payment_transaction.rs, run with `cargo run --bin fee_payment_transaction`

See the source documentation for the complete code for each SDK variant.
steps.expected_output: Output showing transaction events: AssetTxPayment (confirming fees paid in USDT), AssetConversion (alternative asset swapped for native fees), Balances (transfer executed), and System (transaction succeeded).

error_patterns.cause: Chopsticks fork is not running.
error_patterns.resolution: Start the Chopsticks fork first: npx @nicepkg/chopsticks -c polkadot-asset-hub

error_patterns.pattern: 1010: Invalid Transaction: Inability to pay some fees
error_patterns.cause: The account does not have enough USDT to cover the fees, or the USDT asset ID is incorrect.
error_patterns.resolution: Verify Alice's account has USDT on the Chopsticks fork. The polkadot-asset-hub config should pre-fund Alice with USDT. Use asset ID 1984 for USDT.

error_patterns.cause: No liquidity pool exists for the USDT-to-native token swap on the forked chain.
error_patterns.resolution: The Polkadot Hub mainnet fork should have existing USDT/DOT liquidity pools. If using a testnet fork, ensure liquidity pools are available.

error_patterns.cause: WASM cryptographic libraries not initialized (Polkadot.js).
error_patterns.resolution: Call await cryptoWaitReady() before creating any keyring instances.

supplementary_context.description: Background on transaction fees and how alternative fee payment works on Polkadot Hub.

pages.relevance: Explains the fee model and AssetTxPayment signed extension that enables paying fees in alternative tokens.

skills.title: Transfer Assets from Ethereum into Polkadot via Snowbridge
skills.objective: Bridge WETH (or other ERC-20 tokens) from Ethereum to Polkadot Hub using the ParaSpell XCM SDK and the Snowbridge decentralized bridge.

network: Ethereum mainnet RPC endpoint (e.g., via Infura or Alchemy)
Polkadot Hub (receives bridged assets automatically via Snowbridge relayers)
tokens: WETH tokens on Ethereum (wrap ETH via the WETH contract at 0xC02aaA39b223FE8D0A0e5C4F27eAD9083C756Cc2)
ETH for Ethereum gas fees
wallet: An Ethereum wallet with a private key holding WETH and ETH for gas
A Polkadot account address (SS58 format) to receive bridged tokens

env_vars.name: ETHEREUM_PRIVATE_KEY
env_vars.description: The private key of the Ethereum wallet holding WETH and ETH for gas. 0x-prefixed hex string.

env_vars.name: ETHEREUM_RPC_URL
env_vars.description: Ethereum mainnet RPC endpoint URL (e.g., https://mainnet.infura.io/v3/YOUR_KEY).

steps.description:
Snowbridge only supports ERC-20 tokens — it cannot bridge native ETH directly. You must first wrap ETH into WETH.

The WETH contract on Ethereum mainnet: 0xC02aaA39b223FE8D0A0e5C4F27eAD9083C756Cc2

To wrap ETH:
1. Visit the WETH contract on Etherscan: https://etherscan.io/address/0xC02aaA39b223FE8D0A0e5C4F27eAD9083C756Cc2#writeContract
2. Connect your wallet.
3. Call the deposit() function with the amount of ETH you want to wrap.

Skip this step if you already have WETH.
steps.expected_output: WETH balance available in your Ethereum wallet.

steps.action: Initialize the project and install dependencies
steps.description:
Create the project directory and install dependencies. This tutorial uses the PJS version of the ParaSpell SDK (required for Ethereum-to-Polkadot transfers).

```bash
mkdir eth-to-polkadot-bridge && cd eth-to-polkadot-bridge
npm init -y && npm pkg set type=module
npm install --save-dev @types/node tsx typescript
npm install @paraspell/sdk-pjs @polkadot/api @polkadot/types ethers dotenv
```
commands: mkdir eth-to-polkadot-bridge && cd eth-to-polkadot-bridge && npm init -y && npm pkg set type=module
steps.expected_output: Project created and all dependencies installed.

steps.description:
Create a .env file with placeholders for your Ethereum private key and RPC URL.

```
ETHEREUM_PRIVATE_KEY=
ETHEREUM_RPC_URL=
```

Do NOT ask the user to type or paste their private key/seed phrase in the chat — they should edit the .env file directly.

Also create a .gitignore:
```
.env
node_modules/
```
steps.expected_output: .env file created with placeholders.

steps.description:
Create an index.ts file with four sections:

1. **Setup and imports**: Import EvmBuilder from @paraspell/sdk-pjs, ethers, and dotenv/config. Define constants: recipientAddress (your Polkadot SS58 address), WETH contract address (0xC02aaA39b223FE8D0A0e5C4F27eAD9083C756Cc2), and transfer amount. Set up ethers provider and signer from env vars.

2. **approveTokens() function**: Approves the Snowbridge Gateway contract to spend your WETH. Uses ParaSpell's helper to get the Gateway address, then calls ERC-20 approve via ethers.

3. **bridgeTransfer() function**: Builds and executes the bridge transfer:
```typescript
await EvmBuilder(provider)
  .to('AssetHubPolkadot')
  .currency({ symbol: 'WETH', amount: amount })
  .address(recipientAddress)
  .signer(signer)
  .build();
```

4. **Monitoring**: Bridge transfers take ~30 minutes. Monitor via Snowbridge App (https://app.snowbridge.network/), Etherscan, or Polkadot.js Apps.

Replace INSERT_YOUR_POLKADOT_ADDRESS with the recipient's SS58 address.

Workflow:
- First run: execute approveTokens() — `npx tsx index.ts`
- Second run: comment out approveTokens(), uncomment bridgeTransfer() — `npx tsx index.ts`

See the source documentation for the complete code.
steps.expected_output: Approval: token balance and approval confirmation. Transfer: Ethereum transaction hash for the bridge transaction.

error_patterns.cause: The Ethereum wallet does not have enough ETH to pay gas for the approval or bridge transaction.
error_patterns.resolution: Ensure the wallet has enough ETH for gas fees. Bridge transactions can cost significant gas.

error_patterns.cause: The Snowbridge Gateway contract has not been approved to spend the token amount.
error_patterns.resolution: Run the approveTokens() function first before executing the bridge transfer.

error_patterns.cause: The wallet does not hold WETH tokens.
error_patterns.resolution: Wrap ETH to WETH by calling the deposit() function on the WETH contract at 0xC02aaA39b223FE8D0A0e5C4F27eAD9083C756Cc2.

error_patterns.cause: The destination parachain does not have the bridged token registered as a foreign asset.
error_patterns.resolution: Verify the foreign asset is registered on the destination chain. Foreign asset creation on Polkadot Hub is permissionless but must be done before bridging. Check the asset's multilocation matches the legitimate Ethereum token contract.

supplementary_context.description: Background on Polkadot bridging infrastructure and ParaSpell SDK.

pages.relevance: Explains the bridging architecture on Polkadot Hub including Snowbridge, bridge hub, and how cross-ecosystem transfers work.
Reference documentation for the ParaSpell XCM SDK including EvmBuilder, supported assets, and cross-chain transfer patterns.

steps.action: Create the index.ts script with setup code
steps.description:
Create index.ts with the initial setup:
1. Import dependencies: Builder, verifyEdOnDestination, getTransferInfo from @paraspell/sdk; createClient from polkadot-api; hdkd and hdkd-helpers for key derivation; dotenv/config for env loading.
2. Load the seed phrase from .env using process.env.SENDER_SEED_PHRASE.
3. Create a signer from the seed phrase using sr25519CreateDerive and DEV_PHRASE derivation.
4. Define the transfer amount (e.g., 10_000_000_000 for 10 PAS with 10 decimals).
5. Set up the polkadot-api client connecting to the Paseo Asset Hub WebSocket endpoint.

See the source documentation for the exact import structure and setup code.
steps.expected_output: Setup code created in index.ts.

steps.action: Build the XCM transfer transaction and add helper functions
steps.description:
Add the following functions to index.ts:

1. **transfer() function**: Builds and submits the XCM transfer:
```typescript
const tx = await Builder(api)
  .from('AssetHubPaseo')
  .to('PaseoPeople')
  .currency({ symbol: 'PAS', amount: transferAmount })
  .address(recipientAddress)
  .build();
```
Then signs and submits using the signer.

2. **dryRunTransfer() function**: Simulates the transfer without broadcasting to validate it will succeed.

3. **verifyED() function**: Checks the recipient meets the Existential Deposit requirement on People Chain using verifyEdOnDestination.

4. **XcmTransferInfo() function**: Calls getTransferInfo() to estimate XCM fees and show account balance impact.

The workflow is: dry run first, then verify ED, then check fees, then execute transfer. Each function should be called individually (comment out others) via:
```bash
npx tsx index.ts
```
steps.expected_output: All functions defined in index.ts.

steps.action: Perform a dry run to validate the transfer
steps.description:
Call dryRunTransfer() in index.ts (ensure other function calls are commented out).

Run:
```bash
npx tsx index.ts
```

The dry run simulates the XCM transfer without broadcasting, confirming origin validation and destination processing.
steps.expected_output: Dry run output showing transfer validation result with success status.

steps.action: Verify the Existential Deposit on the destination chain
steps.description:
Comment out dryRunTransfer(), uncomment verifyED() call.

Run:
```bash
npx tsx index.ts
```

This checks if the recipient account on People Chain meets the minimum ED threshold.
steps.expected_output: ED verification output confirming whether the account meets the existential deposit requirement.

steps.action: Get transfer info and fee estimates
steps.description:
Comment out verifyED(), uncomment XcmTransferInfo() call.

Run:
```bash
npx tsx index.ts
```

This returns estimated XCM fees and the sender's balance before/after fees.
steps.expected_output: Transfer info with XCM fee estimates and account balance details.

steps.description:
Comment out XcmTransferInfo(), uncomment the transfer() call.

Run:
```bash
npx tsx index.ts
```

The transaction is signed and submitted. Once included in a block, PAS tokens transfer from Paseo Asset Hub to Paseo People Chain.

Verify by checking account balances:
- Paseo Asset Hub: https://polkadot.js.org/apps/?rpc=wss%3A%2F%2Fsys.turboflakes.io%2Fasset-hub-paseo#/accounts
- Paseo People Chain: https://polkadot.js.org/apps/?rpc=wss%3A%2F%2Fsys.ibp.network%2Fpeople-paseo#/accounts

Expected: recipient has 10 more PAS on People Chain, sender has transfer amount + fees deducted on Asset Hub.
steps.expected_output: Transaction submission confirmation with block inclusion. Recipient's People Chain balance increases by 10 PAS.

error_patterns.cause: The recipient account on the destination chain does not meet the minimum existential deposit.
error_patterns.resolution: Send a larger amount that exceeds the destination chain's existential deposit, or pre-fund the recipient account on the destination chain.

error_patterns.cause: The sender does not have enough PAS to cover the transfer amount plus XCM fees.
error_patterns.resolution: Fund the sender account using the Paseo faucet at https://faucet.polkadot.io/?parachain=1000

error_patterns.cause: The seed phrase in .env is malformed or empty.
error_patterns.resolution: Verify the SENDER_SEED_PHRASE in .env is a valid BIP39 mnemonic (12 or 24 words).

error_patterns.cause: ESM/CommonJS module conflict or missing dependency.
error_patterns.resolution: Ensure package.json has '"type": "module"' set. Run npm install to ensure all dependencies are present.

error_patterns.cause: The dry run simulation failed, indicating the transfer would not succeed.
error_patterns.resolution: Check the dry run error details. Common causes: insufficient balance, unsupported asset, or destination chain not reachable.

supplementary_context.description: Background on ParaSpell SDK and cross-chain transfers in Polkadot.

pages.relevance: Reference documentation for the ParaSpell XCM SDK including Builder pattern, dry run, ED verification, transfer info, and supported chains/assets.

skills.title: Create a Custom Pallet from Scratch
skills.objective: Build a custom counter pallet with storage items, events, errors, genesis configuration, and dispatchable functions, then integrate it into a parachain runtime and verify it locally.

runtime: Polkadot SDK Parachain Template cloned and set up locally
rust-src component added: rustup component add rust-src

steps.description: Navigate to the pallets directory and create a new Rust library project for the custom pallet.
steps.expected_output: A new directory pallets/pallet-custom/ is created with Cargo.toml and src/lib.rs.

steps.description: Replace the contents of pallets/pallet-custom/Cargo.toml with the required dependencies. The pallet needs codec (parity-scale-codec with derive feature), scale-info (with derive feature), and frame (with experimental and runtime features). All use workspace = true for version inheritance from the root Cargo.toml. Define features: default = ["std"], std enabling std for codec, scale-info, and frame, plus runtime-benchmarks enabling frame/runtime-benchmarks. The [package] section should set name = "pallet-custom", edition = "2021". The parachain template already includes pallets/* in workspace members, so the new pallet is auto-recognized.
steps.expected_output: Cargo.toml is configured with workspace-inherited dependencies for codec, scale-info, and frame.

steps.description: Replace all contents of src/lib.rs with the initial scaffold. Start with #![cfg_attr(not(feature = "std"), no_std)], then extern crate alloc;, pub use pallet::*;, and the #[frame::pallet] module containing: use frame::prelude::*;, a #[pallet::pallet] struct, a #[pallet::config] trait extending frame_system::Config (initially without RuntimeEvent), a #[pallet::storage] item CounterValue as StorageValue<_, u32>, and a #[pallet::call] impl block. Verify compilation with cargo build --package pallet-custom.
steps.expected_output: Pallet compiles successfully with the minimal scaffold.

steps.action: Add Config trait with RuntimeEvent and CounterMaxValue
steps.description: Update the #[pallet::config] section in src/lib.rs to add: type RuntimeEvent: From<Event<Self>> + IsType<<Self as frame_system::Config>::RuntimeEvent>; and #[pallet::constant] type CounterMaxValue: Get<u32>;. RuntimeEvent is required for the pallet to emit events. CounterMaxValue sets a configurable upper limit on counter values.
steps.expected_output: Config trait now requires RuntimeEvent and CounterMaxValue types.

steps.description: Add a #[pallet::event] section with #[pallet::generate_deposit(pub(super) fn deposit_event)] after the Config trait. Define three events: CounterValueSet { new_value: u32 }, CounterIncremented { new_value: u32, who: T::AccountId, amount: u32 }, and CounterDecremented { new_value: u32, who: T::AccountId, amount: u32 }. The generate_deposit macro creates a deposit_event helper function.
steps.expected_output: Three event variants defined for counter operations.

steps.description: Add a #[pallet::error] section after events. Define three error variants: CounterMaxValueExceeded (counter exceeds configured max), Overflow (arithmetic overflow on increment), and Underflow (arithmetic underflow on decrement).
steps.expected_output: Three error variants defined for validation failures.

steps.description: After the existing CounterValue StorageValue, add a #[pallet::storage] item UserInteractions as StorageMap<_, Blake2_128Concat, T::AccountId, u32>. This tracks the number of interactions per user account.
steps.expected_output: Two storage items: CounterValue (StorageValue) and UserInteractions (StorageMap).

steps.description: Add use alloc::vec::Vec; after use frame::prelude::*; inside the pallet module. Add #[pallet::genesis_config] with a #[derive(DefaultNoBound)] struct GenesisConfig<T: Config> containing pub initial_counter_value: u32 and pub initial_user_interactions: Vec<(T::AccountId, u32)>. Add #[pallet::genesis_build] impl BuildGenesisConfig that sets CounterValue to initial_counter_value and iterates initial_user_interactions to populate UserInteractions storage map.
steps.expected_output: Genesis configuration allows setting initial counter value and user interaction counts.

steps.description: Replace the #[pallet::call] section with three dispatchables: (1) set_counter_value(origin, new_value: u32) with #[pallet::call_index(0)] and #[pallet::weight(Weight::default())] -- requires root origin, validates new_value <= CounterMaxValue, sets CounterValue, emits CounterValueSet. (2) increment(origin, amount: u32) with call_index(1) -- requires signed origin, reads current value, checks overflow with checked_add, validates <= CounterMaxValue, updates CounterValue and UserInteractions (saturating_add 1), emits CounterIncremented. (3) decrement(origin, amount: u32) with call_index(2) -- requires signed origin, reads current, checks underflow with checked_sub, updates CounterValue and UserInteractions, emits CounterDecremented. All return DispatchResult.
