steps.expected_output: Three dispatchable functions: set_counter_value (root only), increment (signed), decrement (signed).

steps.description: Build the pallet in isolation to verify all code compiles correctly before runtime integration.
steps.expected_output: Compilation succeeds with no errors. This may take 1-3 minutes on first build due to Rust dependency compilation.

steps.action: Add pallet dependency to runtime Cargo.toml
steps.description: In runtime/Cargo.toml, add pallet-custom = { path = "../pallets/pallet-custom", default-features = false } to [dependencies]. In the [features] section, add "pallet-custom/std" to the std array.
steps.expected_output: Runtime Cargo.toml references the custom pallet with std feature enabled.

steps.description: At the end of runtime/src/configs/mod.rs, add: impl pallet_custom::Config for Runtime { type RuntimeEvent = RuntimeEvent; type CounterMaxValue = ConstU32<1000>; }. This sets the max counter value to 1000 using ConstU32.
steps.expected_output: Pallet Config trait implemented for the Runtime with max value of 1000.

steps.description: In runtime/src/lib.rs, locate the #[frame_support::runtime] section and add: #[runtime::pallet_index(51)] pub type CustomPallet = pallet_custom;. Choose a unique pallet_index that does not conflict with existing pallets.
steps.expected_output: CustomPallet registered at pallet_index 51 in the runtime.

steps.description: Compile the entire runtime with the new pallet included. This validates all configurations and produces the WASM binary.
steps.expected_output: Full runtime compiles successfully. Expect 5-15 minutes for a release build depending on hardware. The WASM binary is generated at target/release/wbuild/parachain-template-runtime/parachain_template_runtime.compact.compressed.wasm.

steps.action: Generate chain spec and run locally
steps.description: Generate a chain specification file with the updated runtime, then launch the node locally for testing.
commands: chain-spec-builder create -t development --relay-chain paseo --para-id 1000 --runtime ./target/release/wbuild/parachain-template-runtime/parachain_template_runtime.compact.compressed.wasm named-preset development
steps.expected_output: Node starts producing blocks. Connect to ws://127.0.0.1:9944 via Polkadot.js Apps to see customPallet extrinsics: increment, decrement, setCounterValue.

error_patterns.pattern: error[E0433]: failed to resolve: use of undeclared crate or module `alloc`
error_patterns.cause: Missing extern crate alloc; at the top of lib.rs. The pallet uses no_std and needs alloc for Vec.
error_patterns.resolution: Add extern crate alloc; after the #![cfg_attr(not(feature = "std"), no_std)] line.

error_patterns.pattern: error: duplicate lang item in crate `std`
error_patterns.cause: The pallet or a dependency has std feature enabled in a no_std context.
error_patterns.resolution: Ensure all dependencies in Cargo.toml use default-features = false and the std feature is properly gated.

error_patterns.cause: Missing #[pallet::generate_deposit(pub(super) fn deposit_event)] on the Event enum.
error_patterns.resolution: Add the #[pallet::generate_deposit(pub(super) fn deposit_event)] attribute above the Event enum.

error_patterns.cause: The pallet_index chosen for CustomPallet conflicts with an existing pallet.
error_patterns.resolution: Choose a unique pallet_index that does not conflict with any existing pallets in the runtime construct.

error_patterns.pattern: error: the wasm32-unknown-unknown target is not supported
error_patterns.cause: Missing rust-src component needed for WASM compilation.
error_patterns.resolution: Run rustup component add rust-src to install the required component.

supplementary_context.description: Background on SCALE data encoding used throughout pallet storage and types.

pages.relevance: Understanding SCALE codec is essential for working with pallet storage items, events, and dispatchable function parameters.

skills.title: Create a Mock Runtime for Pallet Testing
skills.objective: Create a mock runtime environment (mock.rs) for the custom counter pallet that enables isolated unit testing with configurable genesis states.

runtime: Completed the create-custom-pallet skill -- the custom counter pallet must exist at pallets/pallet-custom/

steps.description: Create a new file src/mock.rs to house the mock runtime code.
steps.expected_output: Empty src/mock.rs file created.

steps.description: Open src/lib.rs and add the mock module declaration after the pub use pallet::*; line: #[cfg(test)] mod mock;. The #[cfg(test)] attribute ensures this module is only compiled during testing.
steps.expected_output: lib.rs includes conditional mock module declaration.

steps.action: Set up basic mock runtime with construct_runtime
steps.description: In src/mock.rs, add the foundational imports and type definitions. Import crate as pallet_custom, frame::deps::frame_support::{derive_impl, runtime}, frame::deps::sp_runtime::BuildStorage, frame::prelude::*, and frame::testing_prelude::*. Define type Block = frame_system::mocking::MockBlock<Test>;. Use the #[runtime] macro to build a minimal test runtime named Test with #[runtime::derive(RuntimeCall, RuntimeEvent, RuntimeError, RuntimeOrigin, RuntimeTask)]. Register frame_system at pallet_index(0) as System, and pallet_custom at pallet_index(1) as CustomPallet.
steps.expected_output: Mock runtime defined with System and CustomPallet.

steps.description: Add the frame_system configuration for the test runtime in src/mock.rs. Use #[derive_impl(frame_system::config_preludes::TestDefaultConfig)] to get sensible test defaults. Override: AccountId = u64 (simple integers instead of cryptographic IDs), Lookup = IdentityLookup<Self::AccountId> (direct account mapping), Block = Block (mock block type).
steps.expected_output: frame_system::Config implemented with test-friendly defaults.

steps.action: Implement custom pallet Config for Test
steps.description: Add the custom pallet configuration in src/mock.rs: impl pallet_custom::Config for Test { type RuntimeEvent = RuntimeEvent; type CounterMaxValue = ConstU32<1000>; }. This mirrors the production configuration with max counter value of 1000.
steps.expected_output: pallet_custom::Config implemented for Test runtime.

steps.description: Add a new_test_ext() function in src/mock.rs that creates a default test environment. It builds frame_system::GenesisConfig::<Test>::default().build_storage().unwrap(), creates TestExternalities from it, and executes System::set_block_number(1) so events can be emitted (events are not emitted on block 0).
steps.expected_output: Basic new_test_ext() helper function available for tests.

steps.description: Add two additional genesis helpers in src/mock.rs: (1) new_test_ext_with_counter(initial_value: u32) that builds default system genesis, then assimilates pallet_custom::GenesisConfig with the given initial_counter_value and default interactions. (2) new_test_ext_with_interactions(initial_value: u32, interactions: Vec<(u64, u32)>) that sets both initial_counter_value and initial_user_interactions. Both helpers use assimilate_storage to merge pallet genesis into existing storage and set block number to 1.
steps.expected_output: Three genesis helper functions available: new_test_ext, new_test_ext_with_counter, new_test_ext_with_interactions.

steps.description: Compile the test code (including the mock and genesis configuration) without running tests to verify correctness.
steps.expected_output: Test compilation succeeds without errors. Two auto-generated tests will be present: mock::__construct_runtime_integrity_test::runtime_integrity_tests and mock::test_genesis_config_builds.

error_patterns.pattern: error[E0433]: failed to resolve: use of undeclared crate or module `pallet_custom`
error_patterns.cause: Missing use crate as pallet_custom; import at the top of mock.rs.
error_patterns.resolution: Add use crate as pallet_custom; at the top of mock.rs to alias the crate.

error_patterns.cause: IdentityLookup not imported in mock.rs.
error_patterns.resolution: Ensure use frame::testing_prelude::*; is included, which re-exports IdentityLookup.

error_patterns.cause: Missing import for BuildStorage trait.
error_patterns.resolution: Add use frame::deps::sp_runtime::BuildStorage; to mock.rs imports.

supplementary_context.description: The mock runtime depends on the pallet built in the create-custom-pallet skill.

pages.relevance: The custom counter pallet source code that the mock runtime tests against.

skills.title: Write Unit Tests for a Custom Pallet
skills.objective: Write a comprehensive suite of 15 unit tests covering dispatchable functions, storage changes, event emission, error conditions, access control, and genesis configuration for the custom counter pallet.

runtime: Completed the create-custom-pallet skill -- the custom counter pallet must exist at pallets/pallet-custom/
Completed the create-pallet-mock-runtime skill -- mock.rs must exist with new_test_ext, new_test_ext_with_counter, and new_test_ext_with_interactions helpers

steps.description: Create a new file src/tests.rs for the test suite.
steps.expected_output: Empty src/tests.rs file created.

steps.description: Open src/lib.rs and add the tests module declaration after the mock module declaration: #[cfg(test)] mod mock; followed by #[cfg(test)] mod tests;. The #[cfg(test)] attribute ensures tests are only compiled during cargo test.
steps.expected_output: lib.rs includes both mock and tests module declarations.

steps.description: In src/tests.rs, add the required imports: use crate::{mock::*, Error, Event}; to import mock runtime helpers and pallet types, use frame::deps::frame_support::{assert_noop, assert_ok}; for FRAME assertion macros (assert_ok! for success, assert_noop! for failure without state change), and use frame::deps::sp_runtime::DispatchError; for origin check testing.
steps.expected_output: Test module has all necessary imports.

steps.description: Add three tests for the set_counter_value dispatchable in src/tests.rs: (1) set_counter_value_works -- Sets block number to 1 (required for event emission), calls set_counter_value via root origin with value 100, asserts CounterValue storage equals 100, asserts last event is CounterValueSet { new_value: 100 }. (2) set_counter_value_requires_root -- Asserts that calling with RuntimeOrigin::signed(1) fails with DispatchError::BadOrigin using assert_noop!. (3) set_counter_value_respects_max_value -- Asserts setting to 1001 fails with Error::<Test>::CounterMaxValueExceeded, and setting to exactly 1000 succeeds.
steps.expected_output: Three tests covering basic functionality, access control, and bounds validation for set_counter_value.

steps.description: Add four tests for the increment dispatchable: (1) increment_works -- Increments by 50 from account 1, asserts CounterValue is 50, checks CounterIncremented event, verifies UserInteractions for account is 1. (2) increment_tracks_multiple_interactions -- Increments 3 times (10, 20, 30) from same account, asserts CounterValue is 60 and UserInteractions is 3. (3) increment_fails_on_overflow -- Uses new_test_ext_with_counter(u32::MAX), asserts incrementing by 1 fails with Error::<Test>::Overflow. (4) increment_respects_max_value -- Uses new_test_ext_with_counter(950), asserts incrementing by 51 fails with CounterMaxValueExceeded, but incrementing by 50 (to exactly 1000) succeeds.
steps.expected_output: Four tests covering increment logic, interaction tracking, overflow, and max value enforcement.

steps.description: Add three tests for the decrement dispatchable: (1) decrement_works -- Uses new_test_ext_with_counter(100), decrements by 30 from account 2, asserts CounterValue is 70, checks CounterDecremented event, verifies UserInteractions is 1. (2) decrement_fails_on_underflow -- Uses new_test_ext_with_counter(10), asserts decrementing by 11 fails with Error::<Test>::Underflow. (3) decrement_tracks_multiple_interactions -- Uses new_test_ext_with_counter(100), decrements twice (10 and 20), asserts CounterValue is 70 and UserInteractions is 2.
steps.expected_output: Three tests covering decrement logic, underflow protection, and interaction tracking.

steps.action: Write integration and genesis tests (3 tests)
steps.description: Add three more tests: (1) mixed_increment_and_decrement_works -- Uses new_test_ext_with_counter(50), performs increment(25), decrement(15), increment(10) from same account, asserts CounterValue is 70 and UserInteractions is 3. (2) different_users_tracked_separately -- Two accounts perform operations, asserts each has independent UserInteractions counts (account1 has 2, account2 has 1, CounterValue is 15). (3) genesis_config_works -- Uses new_test_ext_with_interactions(42, vec![(1, 5), (2, 10)]), asserts initial CounterValue is 42, UserInteractions for account 1 is 5 and account 2 is 10.
steps.expected_output: Three tests covering mixed operations, multi-user tracking, and genesis configuration.

steps.description: Execute the full test suite to verify all 15 tests pass (13 in tests.rs + 2 auto-generated from mock runtime).
steps.expected_output: All 15 tests pass: mock::__construct_runtime_integrity_test::runtime_integrity_tests, mock::test_genesis_config_builds, tests::set_counter_value_works, tests::set_counter_value_requires_root, tests::set_counter_value_respects_max_value, tests::increment_works, tests::increment_tracks_multiple_interactions, tests::increment_fails_on_overflow, tests::increment_respects_max_value, tests::decrement_works, tests::decrement_fails_on_underflow, tests::decrement_tracks_multiple_interactions, tests::mixed_increment_and_decrement_works, tests::different_users_tracked_separately, tests::genesis_config_works. test result: ok. 15 passed; 0 failed

error_patterns.cause: Events are not emitted on block 0 (genesis block). System::set_block_number(1) was not called.
error_patterns.resolution: Always call System::set_block_number(1) at the start of tests that check events. The new_test_ext helpers should already do this.

error_patterns.cause: The error type returned by the dispatchable does not match the expected error.
error_patterns.resolution: Check the exact error variant. For origin failures use DispatchError::BadOrigin, for pallet errors use Error::<Test>::VariantName.

error_patterns.pattern: error[E0433]: failed to resolve: could not find `CounterValue` in `crate`
error_patterns.cause: Storage items need to be accessed through the full crate path in tests.
error_patterns.resolution: Use crate::CounterValue::<Test>::get() instead of CounterValue::<Test>::get() in test files.

supplementary_context.description: This skill depends on both the custom pallet and mock runtime from previous skills.

pages.relevance: The complete pallet implementation being tested.
The mock runtime and genesis helpers used by all tests.

skills.title: Benchmark a Custom Pallet
skills.objective: Add benchmarking to the custom counter pallet, generate accurate weight calculations using frame-omni-bencher, and integrate production weights into the runtime.

runtime: Rust toolchain installed with rustup and rust-src component added
Completed the create-custom-pallet skill -- the custom counter pallet at pallets/pallet-custom/
Completed the create-pallet-mock-runtime skill -- mock.rs with test helpers
Polkadot SDK Parachain Template with the pallet integrated into the runtime

steps.description: Create src/benchmarking.rs with benchmark definitions for all three dispatchables. The file starts with #![cfg(feature = "runtime-benchmarks")] to gate compilation. Import super::*, frame::deps::frame_benchmarking::v2::*, and frame::benchmarking::prelude::RawOrigin. Define a #[benchmarks] mod benchmarks containing: (1) #[benchmark] fn set_counter_value() -- sets new_value = 100, calls via RawOrigin::Root, asserts CounterValue equals new_value. (2) #[benchmark] fn increment() -- creates whitelisted_caller, sets amount = 50, calls via RawOrigin::Signed, asserts CounterValue and UserInteractions. (3) #[benchmark] fn decrement() -- pre-sets CounterValue to 100 via put, creates caller, sets amount = 30, calls, asserts CounterValue is 70. End with impl_benchmark_test_suite!(Pallet, crate::mock::new_test_ext(), crate::mock::Test);
steps.expected_output: benchmarking.rs created with benchmarks for set_counter_value, increment, and decrement.

steps.action: Create weights module with WeightInfo trait
steps.description: Create src/weights.rs with placeholder weights. Define pub trait WeightInfo with fn set_counter_value() -> Weight, fn increment() -> Weight, fn decrement() -> Weight. Implement WeightInfo for () with placeholder values: set_counter_value returns Weight::from_parts(10_000, 0), increment and decrement return Weight::from_parts(15_000, 0). This file will later be overwritten by generated benchmarks.
steps.expected_output: weights.rs created with WeightInfo trait and placeholder implementation.

steps.action: Add WeightInfo to pallet Config trait
steps.description: Update src/lib.rs to add type WeightInfo: crate::weights::WeightInfo; to the #[pallet::config] trait. This makes weights configurable per runtime.
steps.expected_output: Config trait now includes WeightInfo associated type.

steps.description: In src/lib.rs, replace the #[pallet::weight(Weight::default())] annotations on each dispatchable with dynamic weight calls: #[pallet::weight(T::WeightInfo::set_counter_value())] for set_counter_value, #[pallet::weight(T::WeightInfo::increment())] for increment, and #[pallet::weight(T::WeightInfo::decrement())] for decrement. Also add use crate::weights::WeightInfo; inside the pallet module.
steps.expected_output: All three dispatchables use T::WeightInfo:: for dynamic weight calculation.

steps.action: Include benchmarking and weights modules in lib.rs
steps.description: At the top of src/lib.rs, ensure the module declarations are: #![cfg_attr(not(feature = "std"), no_std)] followed by extern crate alloc;, use alloc::vec::Vec;, pub use pallet::*;, #[cfg(test)] mod mock;, #[cfg(test)] mod tests;, #[cfg(feature = "runtime-benchmarks")] mod benchmarking;, and pub mod weights;. Note: benchmarking is only compiled with runtime-benchmarks feature, but weights is always available since the runtime needs it for fee calculation.
steps.expected_output: lib.rs declares benchmarking (feature-gated) and weights (always available) modules.

steps.action: Update pallet Cargo.toml for benchmarking feature
steps.description: Update Cargo.toml features section to include: runtime-benchmarks = ["frame/runtime-benchmarks"]. The full features section should be: default = ["std"], runtime-benchmarks = ["frame/runtime-benchmarks"], std = ["codec/std", "scale-info/std", "frame/std"].
steps.expected_output: Cargo.toml has runtime-benchmarks feature that cascades to frame.

steps.description: In src/mock.rs, update the pallet_custom::Config implementation to include the WeightInfo type using the placeholder: type WeightInfo = ();. Unit tests use () for weights since they test functional correctness, not performance.
steps.expected_output: Mock runtime uses placeholder WeightInfo for testing.

steps.description: Verify benchmarks compile and run as tests with the runtime-benchmarks feature.
steps.expected_output: Benchmark tests pass: benchmarking::benchmarks::bench_set_counter_value ... ok, benchmarking::benchmarks::bench_increment ... ok, benchmarking::benchmarks::bench_decrement ... ok

steps.description: Three updates needed in the runtime: (1) In runtime/Cargo.toml, add "pallet-custom/runtime-benchmarks" to the runtime-benchmarks feature array. (2) In runtime/src/configs/mod.rs, update the Config implementation to include WeightInfo: type WeightInfo = (); (placeholder for now). (3) In runtime/src/benchmarks.rs, add [pallet_custom, CustomPallet] to the define_benchmarks! macro.
steps.expected_output: Runtime configured to include pallet-custom in benchmarking pipeline.

steps.description: Compile the runtime with benchmarking enabled to generate the WASM binary needed for benchmarking.
steps.expected_output: Runtime WASM binary generated at target/release/wbuild/parachain-template-runtime/parachain_template_runtime.wasm. Expect 10-20 minutes for release build with benchmarks.

steps.description: Install the official FRAME benchmarking tool. Download the pre-built binary from the Polkadot SDK release page, or install from source with cargo install frame-omni-bencher --locked. For macOS (Apple Silicon): curl -L -o frame-omni-bencher the appropriate release URL, chmod +x, sudo mv to /usr/local/bin/. For Ubuntu: similar process with the Linux binary.
steps.expected_output: frame-omni-bencher binary available in PATH.

steps.description: Download the official Polkadot SDK weight template file that transforms benchmark data into formatted Rust source.
steps.expected_output: frame-weight-template.hbs downloaded to pallets/pallet-custom/.

steps.description: Run benchmarks against the compiled WASM runtime to generate production weight values. Benchmarks execute against WASM (not native) because WASM is what runs in production.
steps.expected_output: weights.rs is regenerated with actual measured weights including SubstrateWeight<T> struct, DbWeight reads/writes, and hardware-specific timing values. The file contains pub trait WeightInfo, pub struct SubstrateWeight<T>, and impl WeightInfo for SubstrateWeight<T> with real measurements.

steps.description: Update runtime/src/configs/mod.rs to use the generated weights instead of the placeholder: impl pallet_custom::Config for Runtime { type RuntimeEvent = RuntimeEvent; type CounterMaxValue = ConstU32<1000>; type WeightInfo = pallet_custom::weights::SubstrateWeight<Runtime>; }. This activates benchmarked weights in the production runtime for accurate fee calculation and block limits.
steps.expected_output: Runtime uses production-benchmarked weights from SubstrateWeight<Runtime>.

error_patterns.cause: The runtime-benchmarks feature is not enabled or frame_benchmarking is not properly imported.
error_patterns.resolution: Ensure runtime-benchmarks = ["frame/runtime-benchmarks"] is in Cargo.toml features, and the file has #![cfg(feature = "runtime-benchmarks")] at the top.

error_patterns.pattern: Pallet `pallet_custom` not found in the runtime
error_patterns.cause: The pallet is not registered in runtime/src/benchmarks.rs define_benchmarks! macro.
error_patterns.resolution: Add [pallet_custom, CustomPallet] to the define_benchmarks! macro in runtime/src/benchmarks.rs.

error_patterns.cause: The weights module is not declared as public in lib.rs.
error_patterns.resolution: Ensure pub mod weights; (not just mod weights;) is declared in lib.rs so the runtime can access it.

error_patterns.pattern: Runtime does not have benchmarking enabled
error_patterns.cause: The runtime was not compiled with the runtime-benchmarks feature.
error_patterns.resolution: Rebuild with cargo build --release --features runtime-benchmarks.

supplementary_context.description: Benchmarking builds on the custom pallet and mock runtime from previous skills.

pages.relevance: The pallet code being benchmarked.
The mock runtime required by impl_benchmark_test_suite! for running benchmark tests.

skills.title: Add an Existing Pallet to the Runtime
skills.objective: Add pallet-utility to a Polkadot SDK parachain runtime by configuring dependencies, implementing the Config trait, registering in the runtime construct, and verifying locally with batch transactions.

runtime: Polkadot SDK Parachain Template cloned and set up locally
rust-src component added: rustup component add rust-src

steps.description: Open runtime/Cargo.toml and locate the polkadot-sdk dependency in the [dependencies] section. Add "pallet-utility" to the features array. Since pallet-utility is part of the polkadot-sdk monorepo, it is added as a feature rather than a separate dependency.
steps.expected_output: runtime/Cargo.toml includes pallet-utility in polkadot-sdk features.

steps.description: In runtime/Cargo.toml, verify the [features] section has polkadot-sdk/std in the std array. Since pallet-utility is part of polkadot-sdk, its std feature is automatically included when polkadot-sdk/std is enabled. No additional changes are needed for std features.
steps.expected_output: The std feature array already includes polkadot-sdk/std, covering pallet-utility.

steps.description: Open runtime/src/configs/mod.rs and add the Config implementation at the end of the file: impl pallet_utility::Config for Runtime { type RuntimeEvent = RuntimeEvent; type RuntimeCall = RuntimeCall; type PalletsOrigin = OriginCaller; type WeightInfo = pallet_utility::weights::SubstrateWeight<Runtime>; }. RuntimeEvent links pallet events to the runtime. RuntimeCall allows the utility pallet to dispatch calls from other pallets (needed for batch operations). PalletsOrigin enables origin manipulation. WeightInfo uses the pallet's built-in benchmarked weights.
steps.expected_output: pallet_utility::Config implemented for Runtime with all required types.

steps.description: Open runtime/src/lib.rs, locate the #[frame_support::runtime] section, and add pallet-utility with a unique pallet_index: #[runtime::pallet_index(50)] pub type Utility = pallet_utility;. Choose an index that does not conflict with existing pallets.
steps.expected_output: Utility pallet registered at pallet_index 50.

steps.description: Compile the full runtime with the new pallet to validate all configurations.
steps.expected_output: Runtime compiles successfully with pallet-utility included. Expect 5-15 minutes for release build. WASM binary at target/release/wbuild/parachain-template-runtime/parachain_template_runtime.compact.compressed.wasm.

steps.description: Create a chain specification file with the updated runtime.
commands: chain-spec-builder create -t development --relay-chain paseo --para-id 1000 --runtime ./target/release/wbuild/parachain-template-runtime/parachain_template_runtime.compact.compressed.wasm named-preset development
steps.expected_output: chain_spec.json generated with the updated runtime.

steps.description: Launch the parachain locally for testing.
steps.expected_output: Node starts producing blocks. Connect to ws://127.0.0.1:9944.

steps.description: Navigate to Polkadot.js Apps (https://polkadot.js.org/apps/?rpc=ws%3A%2F%2F127.0.0.1%3A9944#/extrinsics) and verify the utility pallet appears in the extrinsic dropdown. Available extrinsics should include: batch(calls) for dispatching multiple calls in a single transaction, batchAll(calls) for dispatching multiple calls stopping on first error, and asDerivative(index, call) for dispatching as a derivative account.
steps.expected_output: utility pallet visible in Polkadot.js Apps with batch, batchAll, and asDerivative extrinsics.

error_patterns.cause: pallet-utility not added to the polkadot-sdk features in runtime/Cargo.toml.
error_patterns.resolution: Add "pallet-utility" to the features array of the polkadot-sdk dependency in runtime/Cargo.toml.

error_patterns.cause: The pallet_index chosen for Utility conflicts with an existing pallet.
error_patterns.resolution: Choose a unique pallet_index that does not conflict with any existing pallets. Check the runtime construct for used indices.

error_patterns.cause: OriginCaller is auto-generated by the runtime macro and must be used within the correct scope.
error_patterns.resolution: Ensure OriginCaller is accessible in configs/mod.rs. It is generated by #[frame_support::runtime] and should be available via the runtime module.

error_patterns.pattern: error: the wasm32-unknown-unknown target is not supported
error_patterns.cause: Missing rust-src component needed for WASM compilation.
error_patterns.resolution: Run rustup component add rust-src.

supplementary_context.description: Additional context on smart contract pallets that can also be added to runtimes.

pages.relevance: Shows how other existing pallets (pallet-revive, pallet-evm, pallet-contracts) can be added to runtimes for smart contract functionality.

skills.title: Add Multiple Pallet Instances to Runtime
skills.objective: Add two independent instances of pallet-collective (TechnicalCommittee and Council) to a parachain runtime with separate configurations and verify they maintain independent state.

runtime: Polkadot SDK Parachain Template cloned and set up locally
rust-src component added: rustup component add rust-src
Basic understanding of adding pallets to a runtime (see add-existing-pallet-to-runtime skill)

steps.description: Open runtime/Cargo.toml and add "pallet-collective" to the features array of the polkadot-sdk dependency.
steps.expected_output: runtime/Cargo.toml includes pallet-collective in polkadot-sdk features.

steps.description: Since pallet-collective is part of polkadot-sdk, verify polkadot-sdk/std is in the std feature array in runtime/Cargo.toml. No additional std entries are needed.
steps.expected_output: std features properly configured via polkadot-sdk/std.

steps.action: Define parameter types for collective instances
steps.description: In runtime/src/configs/mod.rs, add parameter type definitions using the parameter_types! macro: MotionDuration set to 24 * HOURS (in blocks), MaxProposals = 100, MaxMembers = 100, and MaxProposalWeight = Perbill::from_percent(50) * RuntimeBlockWeights::get().max_block. These define motion duration (24 hours), max active proposals, max members per collective, and max weight per proposal (50% of block). You can customize these per instance if needed.
steps.expected_output: Parameter types defined for collective configuration.

steps.description: In runtime/src/configs/mod.rs, add the import: use frame_support::instances::{Instance1, Instance2};. These create distinct type identities for each pallet instance. Instance1 will be TechnicalCommittee and Instance2 will be Council.
steps.expected_output: Instance1 and Instance2 imported from frame_support.

steps.description: In runtime/src/configs/mod.rs, add the Config implementation for Instance1: impl pallet_collective::Config<Instance1> for Runtime with RuntimeOrigin = RuntimeOrigin, Proposal = RuntimeCall, RuntimeEvent = RuntimeEvent, MotionDuration = MotionDuration, MaxProposals = MaxProposals, MaxMembers = MaxMembers, DefaultVote = pallet_collective::MoreThanMajorityThenPrimeDefaultVote, SetMembersOrigin = EnsureRoot<AccountId>, WeightInfo = pallet_collective::weights::SubstrateWeight<Runtime>, MaxProposalWeight = MaxProposalWeight, DisapproveOrigin = EnsureRoot<Self::AccountId>, KillOrigin = EnsureRoot<Self::AccountId>, Consideration = (). Note the <Instance1> generic parameter on Config.
steps.expected_output: Config<Instance1> implemented for TechnicalCommittee.

steps.description: In runtime/src/configs/mod.rs, add the Config implementation for Instance2: impl pallet_collective::Config<Instance2> for Runtime with the same configuration as Instance1. You can customize parameters per instance if needed (e.g., shorter MotionDuration for technical committee by defining separate parameter_types).
steps.expected_output: Config<Instance2> implemented for Council.

steps.action: Register both instances in runtime construct
steps.description: In runtime/src/lib.rs, locate the #[frame_support::runtime] section and add both instances with unique pallet indices: #[runtime::pallet_index(50)] pub type TechnicalCommittee = pallet_collective<Instance1>; and #[runtime::pallet_index(51)] pub type Council = pallet_collective<Instance2>;. Each instance must have a unique pallet_index. The instance type in angle brackets links each registration to its Config implementation. Use descriptive names that reflect each instance's governance purpose.
steps.expected_output: TechnicalCommittee (index 50) and Council (index 51) registered in the runtime.

steps.description: Compile the full runtime to verify both instances are properly configured.
steps.expected_output: Runtime compiles successfully with both pallet-collective instances. Expect 5-15 minutes for release build.

steps.description: Create a chain specification with the updated runtime containing both collective instances.
commands: chain-spec-builder create -t development --relay-chain paseo --para-id 1000 --runtime ./target/release/wbuild/parachain-template-runtime/parachain_template_runtime.compact.compressed.wasm named-preset development
steps.expected_output: chain_spec.json generated with both collective instances.

steps.description: Launch the parachain locally to test both pallet instances.
steps.expected_output: Node starts producing blocks with both collective instances initialized.

steps.action: Verify both instances in Polkadot.js Apps
steps.description: Navigate to Polkadot.js Apps (https://polkadot.js.org/apps/?rpc=ws%3A%2F%2F127.0.0.1%3A9944#/extrinsics) and verify: (1) In the extrinsics dropdown, both technicalCommittee and council appear as separate pallets. (2) Each instance shows extrinsics: close, propose, setMembers, vote. (3) In Developer > Chain state, query technicalCommittee > members() and council > members() to verify separate storage. (4) Changes to one instance's members do not affect the other, confirming independent state.
steps.expected_output: Both technicalCommittee and council appear as independent pallets with separate storage and extrinsics.

error_patterns.cause: pallet-collective not added to the polkadot-sdk features in runtime/Cargo.toml.
error_patterns.resolution: Add "pallet-collective" to the features array of the polkadot-sdk dependency.

error_patterns.cause: Both instances share the same pallet_index, or one conflicts with an existing pallet.
error_patterns.resolution: Assign unique pallet_index values to each instance (e.g., 50 for TechnicalCommittee, 51 for Council).

error_patterns.cause: Instance types not imported from frame_support.
error_patterns.resolution: Add use frame_support::instances::{Instance1, Instance2}; to runtime/src/configs/mod.rs.

error_patterns.pattern: the trait bound `Runtime: pallet_collective::Config<Instance1>` is not satisfied
error_patterns.cause: Config trait not implemented for the specific instance type.
error_patterns.resolution: Ensure you implement pallet_collective::Config<Instance1> (not just pallet_collective::Config) for Runtime.

error_patterns.pattern: error: the wasm32-unknown-unknown target is not supported
error_patterns.cause: Missing rust-src component needed for WASM compilation.
error_patterns.resolution: Run rustup component add rust-src.

supplementary_context.description: Background on adding individual pallets, which is the foundation for multi-instance configuration.

pages.relevance: Foundational guide on adding a single pallet to the runtime, covering dependency management, Config trait implementation, and runtime registration.

skills.title: Set Up and Run a Polkadot Full Node
skills.objective: Install, configure, and run a Polkadot relay chain full node that syncs with the network and provides RPC access for querying chain data.

runtime: Rust toolchain (stable + nightly) installed via rustup, OR Docker, OR snap package manager
wasm32-unknown-unknown target added to nightly toolchain (if building from source)
network: Public or local network access
Port 9944 open for WebSocket RPC
Port 9615 open for Prometheus metrics (optional)
Port 30333 open for P2P networking

steps.action: Choose an installation method and install the Polkadot binary
steps.description:
Select one of the following methods to obtain the polkadot binary:

**Option A - Linux pre-built binary:**
```bash
wget https://github.com/paritytech/polkadot-sdk/releases/download/polkadot-INSERT_VERSION/polkadot
sudo chmod +x polkadot
sudo mv polkadot /usr/local/bin/
```

**Option B - Build from source (macOS/Linux):**
```bash
source ~/.cargo/env
rustup default stable && rustup update
rustup update nightly
rustup target add wasm32-unknown-unknown --toolchain nightly
git clone https://github.com/paritytech/polkadot-sdk polkadot-sdk
cd polkadot-sdk
cargo build --release
```
The binary will be at `./target/release/polkadot`. Building may take 1+ hours.

**Option C - Docker:**
```bash
docker pull parity/polkadot:v1.16.2
```

**Option D - Snap (Linux):**
```bash
sudo apt update && sudo apt install snapd
sudo snap install polkadot
```

NOTE: The developer must execute these commands on their target machine.
steps.expected_output: The polkadot binary is available and executable on the system.

steps.action: Configure and start the full node
steps.description:
Choose the node type and start the node. The developer must run this command on their server as it starts a long-running process.

**Default pruned node (last 256 blocks):**
```bash
polkadot --chain polkadot --name "INSERT_NODE_NAME"
```

**Custom pruned node (last 1000 blocks with RPC):**
```bash
polkadot --chain polkadot \
  --name INSERT_YOUR_NODE_NAME \
  --state-pruning 1000 \
  --blocks-pruning archive \
  --rpc-cors all \
  --rpc-methods safe
```

**Archive node (complete history):**
```bash
polkadot --chain polkadot \
  --name INSERT_YOUR_NODE_NAME \
  --state-pruning archive \
  --blocks-pruning archive
```

**Docker:**
```bash
docker run -p 9944:9944 -p 9615:9615 parity/polkadot:v1.16.2 \
  --name "my-polkadot-node" --rpc-external --prometheus-external
```
For Apple Silicon add `--platform linux/amd64`.

**Snap:**
```bash
sudo snap set polkadot service-args="--name=MyName --chain=polkadot"
sudo snap start polkadot
```

RPC configuration flags:
- `--rpc-methods safe` - Only allow safe RPC methods
- `--rpc-max-connections 200` - Set max RPC connections
- `--rpc-cors all` - Allow access from all origins

NOTE: This starts a long-running process. The developer must execute on their server and keep it running.
steps.expected_output: Node starts syncing and outputs log lines showing block import progress.

steps.description:
The syncing process can take approximately 36 hours depending on hardware. Monitor progress by watching log output for lines like:
```
Syncing 150.0 bps, target=#XXXXXXX ... peers=25
```

The node name will appear gray on Polkadot Telemetry (https://telemetry.polkadot.io/#list/Polkadot) while syncing, and white once fully synced.

For snap: `snap logs polkadot -f`
For Docker: `docker logs -f CONTAINER_NAME`

NOTE: The developer should check Telemetry periodically to verify sync status.
steps.expected_output: Node appears on Polkadot Telemetry and transitions from gray (syncing) to white (synced).

steps.action: Connect to the node via Polkadot.js Apps
steps.description:
Open Polkadot.js Apps at https://polkadot.js.org/apps/?rpc=ws%3A%2F%2F127.0.0.1%3A9944#/explorer and click the logo in the top left to switch node. Activate the Development toggle and enter your node's WebSocket endpoint:
```
ws://127.0.0.1:9944
```
This confirms the node is reachable and serving RPC requests.
steps.expected_output: Polkadot.js Apps connects to the local node and displays current chain state and block explorer.

error_patterns.pattern: cannot execute binary file: Exec format error
error_patterns.cause: The pre-built binary is incompatible with your system architecture.
error_patterns.resolution: Compile the binary from source or use Docker instead.

error_patterns.cause: Database corruption or incompatible state.
error_patterns.resolution: Wipe the chain data directory and re-sync from scratch, or use a fresh snapshot.

error_patterns.cause: No peers discovered on the network.
error_patterns.resolution: Ensure port 30333 is open and accessible. Check firewall rules.

supplementary_context.description: Additional context for running Polkadot full nodes including Docker images and telemetry.

pages.relevance: Primary source page for full node setup

skills.title: Run a Parachain RPC Node on Polkadot
skills.objective: Deploy a production-ready parachain RPC node (using People Chain as example) with Docker or systemd, including snapshot-based fast sync and monitoring.

runtime: Ubuntu 22.04 LTS or similar Linux distribution
Docker installed (for Docker deployment or obtaining binaries)
network: Public IP address
Stable internet connection (1 Gbps for high traffic)
Port 30333 open for parachain P2P
Port 30334 open for relay chain P2P
Port 9944 open for WebSocket RPC
Port 9933 open for HTTP RPC
Port 9615 open for Prometheus metrics
hardware: Memory: 64 GB RAM minimum (128 GB for high traffic)
Storage: Archive node ~1.2 TB NVMe SSD (Asset Hub + relay chain), Pruned ~200 GB
Fast NVMe I/O critical for RPC query performance

env_vars.name: SNAPSHOT_URL_PARACHAIN
env_vars.description: URL to the parachain snapshot from https://snapshots.polkadot.io/

env_vars.name: SNAPSHOT_URL_RELAY
env_vars.description: URL to the pruned relay chain snapshot from https://snapshots.polkadot.io/

steps.description:
Download the chain specification for your target parachain.

**System parachains** (recommended source - Chainspec Collection):
```bash
# Example for People Chain
curl -L https://raw.githubusercontent.com/paritytech/polkadot-sdk/master/cumulus/parachains/chain-specs/people-polkadot.json -o chain-spec.json
```

Available system parachain chain specs:
| Parachain | Para ID | Chain Spec File |
|-----------|---------|----------------|
| Bridge Hub | 1002 | bridge-hub-polkadot.json |
| People Chain | 1004 | people-polkadot.json |
| Coretime Chain | 1005 | coretime-polkadot.json |

For non-system parachains, check the parachain's own documentation for the chain spec file.
steps.expected_output: Chain specification JSON file downloaded to the working directory.

steps.description:
Using snapshots reduces initial sync time from days to hours. The developer must run these commands on their server.

```bash
# Create directories
mkdir -p my-node-data/chains/people-polkadot/db
mkdir -p my-node-data/chains/polkadot/db

# Download parachain snapshot (check https://snapshots.polkadot.io/ for latest URL)
export SNAPSHOT_URL_PARACHAIN="https://snapshots.polkadot.io/polkadot-people-rocksdb-archive/INSERT_LATEST"
rclone copyurl $SNAPSHOT_URL_PARACHAIN/files.txt files.txt
rclone copy --progress --transfers 20 \
  --http-url $SNAPSHOT_URL_PARACHAIN \
  --no-traverse --http-no-head --disable-http2 \
  --inplace --no-gzip-encoding --size-only \
  --retries 6 --retries-sleep 10s \
  --files-from files.txt :http: my-node-data/chains/people-polkadot/db/
rm files.txt

# Download relay chain pruned snapshot
export SNAPSHOT_URL_RELAY="https://snapshots.polkadot.io/polkadot-rocksdb-prune/INSERT_LATEST"
rclone copyurl $SNAPSHOT_URL_RELAY/files.txt files.txt
rclone copy --progress --transfers 20 \
  --http-url $SNAPSHOT_URL_RELAY \
  --no-traverse --http-no-head --disable-http2 \
  --inplace --no-gzip-encoding --size-only \
  --retries 6 --retries-sleep 10s \
  --files-from files.txt :http: my-node-data/chains/polkadot/db/
rm files.txt
```

Snapshot paths by parachain:
- People Chain: polkadot-people-rocksdb-archive
- Bridge Hub: polkadot-bridge-hub-paritydb-archive
- Coretime: polkadot-coretime-rocksdb-archive

NOTE: Snapshot downloads can take significant time depending on bandwidth. The developer must execute these on their server.
steps.expected_output: Snapshot data populated in the node data directories.

steps.description:
Choose Docker or systemd deployment. The developer must execute these on their server as they start long-running services.

**Docker (Archive):**
```bash
docker run -d --name people-chain-rpc --restart unless-stopped \
  -p 9944:9944 -p 9933:9933 -p 9615:9615 -p 30334:30334 -p 30333:30333 \
  -v $(pwd)/chain-spec.json:/people-polkadot.json \
  -v $(pwd)/my-node-data:/data \
  parity/polkadot-parachain:latest \
  --name=PeopleChainRPC \
  --base-path=/data \
  --chain=/people-polkadot.json \
  --prometheus-external --prometheus-port 9615 \
  --unsafe-rpc-external --rpc-port=9944 \
  --rpc-cors=all --rpc-methods=safe --rpc-max-connections=1000 \
  --state-pruning=archive --blocks-pruning=archive \
  -- \
  --base-path=/data --chain=polkadot \
  --state-pruning=256 --blocks-pruning=256 --rpc-port=0
```

**systemd setup:**
1. Download and install the binary:
```bash
wget https://github.com/paritytech/polkadot-sdk/releases/download/INSERT_VERSION/polkadot-parachain
chmod +x polkadot-parachain
sudo mv polkadot-parachain /usr/local/bin/
```

2. Create user and directories:
```bash
sudo useradd -r -s /bin/bash polkadot
sudo mkdir -p /var/lib/people-chain-rpc
sudo cp chain-spec.json /var/lib/people-chain-rpc/people-polkadot.json
sudo chown -R polkadot:polkadot /var/lib/people-chain-rpc
```

3. Create systemd service file at `/etc/systemd/system/people-chain-rpc.service`:
```ini
[Unit]
Description=People Chain RPC Node
After=network.target

[Service]
Type=simple
User=polkadot
Group=polkadot
WorkingDirectory=/var/lib/people-chain-rpc
ExecStart=/usr/local/bin/polkadot-parachain \
  --name=PeopleChainRPC \
  --chain=/var/lib/people-chain-rpc/people-polkadot.json \
  --base-path=/var/lib/people-chain-rpc \
  --port=30333 --rpc-port=9944 \
  --rpc-external --rpc-cors=all --rpc-methods=safe --rpc-max-connections=1000 \
  --prometheus-port=9615 --prometheus-external \
  --state-pruning=archive --blocks-pruning=archive \
  -- \
  --chain=polkadot --base-path=/var/lib/people-chain-rpc \
  --port=30334 --state-pruning=256 --blocks-pruning=256 --rpc-port=0
Restart=always
RestartSec=10
LimitNOFILE=65536

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
```

4. Start the service:
```bash
sudo systemctl daemon-reload
sudo systemctl enable people-chain-rpc
sudo systemctl start people-chain-rpc
```

NOTE: The developer must create the service file and run these commands on their server.
steps.expected_output: Node starts and begins syncing both the parachain and embedded relay chain.

steps.description:
Check sync status with the RPC endpoint:
```bash
curl -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"id":1, "jsonrpc":"2.0", "method": "system_syncState", "params":[]}' \
  http://localhost:9944
```

When sync is complete, `currentBlock` equals `highestBlock`.

Check node health:
```bash
curl -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"id":1, "jsonrpc":"2.0", "method": "system_health", "params":[]}' \
  http://localhost:9944
```

View logs:
- Docker: `docker logs -f people-chain-rpc`
- systemd: `sudo journalctl -u people-chain-rpc -f`

Manage the node:
- Docker stop: `docker stop people-chain-rpc`
- systemd stop: `sudo systemctl stop people-chain-rpc`
- systemd status: `sudo systemctl status people-chain-rpc`
steps.expected_output: JSON response showing currentBlock equal to highestBlock when fully synced.

error_patterns.pattern: Bootnode with peer id .* is on a different chain
error_patterns.cause: Wrong chain specification file used for the target parachain.
error_patterns.resolution: Verify you downloaded the correct chain spec for your target parachain.

error_patterns.cause: Another node instance is already using the same data directory.
error_patterns.resolution: Stop the existing node process before starting a new one.

error_patterns.cause: Corrupted database or mismatched pruning configuration with existing data.
error_patterns.resolution: Wipe the database directory and re-sync, or download fresh snapshots.

supplementary_context.description: Parachain RPC node setup including Docker and systemd deployment options with snapshot-based sync.

pages.relevance: Primary source page for parachain RPC node setup

skills.title: Run an RPC Node for Polkadot Hub with Ethereum RPC Compatibility
skills.objective: Deploy a Polkadot Hub (Asset Hub) RPC node with both Polkadot SDK and optional Ethereum JSON-RPC endpoints for EVM tool compatibility.

runtime: Ubuntu 22.04 LTS or similar Linux distribution
Rust toolchain (if building eth-rpc from source for systemd)
network: Public IP address
Stable internet connection (1 Gbps for high traffic)
Port 30333 open for parachain P2P
Port 30334 open for relay chain P2P
Port 9944 open for Polkadot SDK WebSocket RPC
Port 9933 open for Polkadot SDK HTTP RPC
Port 8545 open for Ethereum JSON-RPC (if running eth-rpc adapter)
hardware: Memory: 64 GB RAM minimum (128 GB for high traffic)
Storage: Archive ~1.2 TB NVMe SSD, Pruned ~200 GB NVMe SSD
Fast disk I/O critical for query performance

env_vars.name: SNAPSHOT_URL_ASSET_HUB
env_vars.description: URL to the Asset Hub archive snapshot from https://snapshots.polkadot.io/

env_vars.name: SNAPSHOT_URL_RELAY
env_vars.description: URL to the pruned relay chain snapshot from https://snapshots.polkadot.io/

steps.action: Download the Polkadot Hub chain specification
steps.description:
Download the official Asset Hub chain spec:
```bash
curl -L https://raw.githubusercontent.com/paritytech/polkadot-sdk/master/cumulus/parachains/chain-specs/asset-hub-polkadot.json -o asset-hub-polkadot.json
```
steps.expected_output: asset-hub-polkadot.json downloaded to the working directory.

steps.description:
Reduces sync time from days to hours. The developer must run these on their server.

```bash
mkdir -p my-node-data/chains/asset-hub-polkadot/db
mkdir -p my-node-data/chains/polkadot/db

# Asset Hub archive snapshot
export SNAPSHOT_URL_ASSET_HUB="https://snapshots.polkadot.io/polkadot-asset-hub-rocksdb-archive/INSERT_LATEST"
rclone copyurl $SNAPSHOT_URL_ASSET_HUB/files.txt files.txt
rclone copy --progress --transfers 20 \
  --http-url $SNAPSHOT_URL_ASSET_HUB \
  --no-traverse --http-no-head --disable-http2 \
  --inplace --no-gzip-encoding --size-only \
  --retries 6 --retries-sleep 10s \
  --files-from files.txt :http: my-node-data/chains/asset-hub-polkadot/db/
rm files.txt

# Pruned relay chain snapshot
export SNAPSHOT_URL_RELAY="https://snapshots.polkadot.io/polkadot-rocksdb-prune/INSERT_LATEST"
rclone copyurl $SNAPSHOT_URL_RELAY/files.txt files.txt
rclone copy --progress --transfers 20 \
  --http-url $SNAPSHOT_URL_RELAY \
  --no-traverse --http-no-head --disable-http2 \
  --inplace --no-gzip-encoding --size-only \
  --retries 6 --retries-sleep 10s \
  --files-from files.txt :http: my-node-data/chains/polkadot/db/
rm files.txt
```

NOTE: Check https://snapshots.polkadot.io/ for the latest snapshot URLs.
steps.expected_output: Snapshot data populated in node data directories.

steps.action: Launch the Polkadot Hub RPC node
steps.description:
Choose Docker or systemd. The developer must execute on their server.

**Docker (Archive):**
```bash
docker run -d --name polkadot-hub-rpc --restart unless-stopped \
  -p 9944:9944 -p 9933:9933 -p 9615:9615 -p 30334:30334 -p 30333:30333 \
  -v $(pwd)/asset-hub-polkadot.json:/asset-hub-polkadot.json \
  -v $(pwd)/my-node-data:/data \
  parity/polkadot-parachain:latest \
  --name=PolkadotHubRPC --base-path=/data \
  --chain=/asset-hub-polkadot.json \
  --prometheus-external --prometheus-port 9615 \
  --unsafe-rpc-external --rpc-port=9944 \
  --rpc-cors=all --rpc-methods=safe --rpc-max-connections=1000 \
  --state-pruning=archive --blocks-pruning=archive \
  -- \
  --base-path=/data --chain=polkadot \
  --state-pruning=256 --blocks-pruning=256 --rpc-port=0
```

**systemd setup:**
1. Download binary:
```bash
wget https://github.com/paritytech/polkadot-sdk/releases/download/INSERT_VERSION/polkadot-parachain
chmod +x polkadot-parachain
sudo mv polkadot-parachain /usr/local/bin/
```

2. Create user and directories:
```bash
sudo useradd -r -s /bin/bash polkadot
sudo mkdir -p /var/lib/polkadot-hub-rpc
sudo cp asset-hub-polkadot.json /var/lib/polkadot-hub-rpc/
sudo chown -R polkadot:polkadot /var/lib/polkadot-hub-rpc
```

3. Create `/etc/systemd/system/polkadot-hub-rpc.service`:
```ini
[Unit]
Description=Polkadot Hub RPC Node
After=network.target

[Service]
Type=simple
User=polkadot
Group=polkadot
WorkingDirectory=/var/lib/polkadot-hub-rpc
ExecStart=/usr/local/bin/polkadot-parachain \
  --name=PolkadotHubRPC \
  --chain=/var/lib/polkadot-hub-rpc/asset-hub-polkadot.json \
  --base-path=/var/lib/polkadot-hub-rpc \
  --port=30333 --rpc-port=9944 \
  --rpc-external --rpc-cors=all --rpc-methods=safe --rpc-max-connections=1000 \
  --prometheus-port=9615 --prometheus-external \
  --state-pruning=archive --blocks-pruning=archive \
  -- \
  --chain=polkadot --base-path=/var/lib/polkadot-hub-rpc \
  --port=30334 --state-pruning=256 --blocks-pruning=256 --rpc-port=0
Restart=always
RestartSec=10
LimitNOFILE=65536

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
```

4. Start:
```bash
sudo systemctl daemon-reload
sudo systemctl enable polkadot-hub-rpc
sudo systemctl start polkadot-hub-rpc
```

NOTE: This starts a long-running service. The developer must execute on their server.
steps.expected_output: Node starts syncing the Asset Hub parachain and embedded relay chain.

steps.action: Wait for node sync and verify
steps.description:
Monitor sync status:
```bash
curl -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"id":1, "jsonrpc":"2.0", "method": "system_syncState", "params":[]}' \
  http://localhost:9944
```

When `currentBlock` equals `highestBlock`, the node is fully synced.

The node MUST be fully synced before starting the Ethereum RPC adapter.
steps.expected_output: JSON response with currentBlock equal to highestBlock.

steps.description:
The eth-rpc adapter translates Ethereum JSON-RPC calls into Polkadot SDK requests, enabling MetaMask, Hardhat, and Ethers.js compatibility. An archive node is recommended.

**Docker:**
```bash
docker run -d --name eth-rpc --restart unless-stopped \
  --network=host \
  -v /var/lib/eth-rpc:/data \
  paritypr/eth-rpc:master-1ea05e17 \
  --node-rpc-url=ws://127.0.0.1:9944 \
  --rpc-port=8545 \
  --base-path=/data \
  --unsafe-rpc-external \
  --rpc-cors=all
```
Check https://hub.docker.com/r/paritypr/eth-rpc/tags for the latest image tag.

**systemd:**
1. Build from source:
```bash
git clone https://github.com/paritytech/polkadot-sdk.git
cd polkadot-sdk
cargo build -p pallet-revive-eth-rpc --bin eth-rpc --release
sudo mv target/release/eth-rpc /usr/local/bin/
```

2. Create `/etc/systemd/system/eth-rpc.service`:
```ini
[Unit]
Description=Ethereum RPC Adapter for Polkadot Hub
After=network.target polkadot-hub-rpc.service

[Service]
Type=simple
User=polkadot
Group=polkadot
ExecStart=/usr/local/bin/eth-rpc \
  --node-rpc-url=ws://127.0.0.1:9944 \
  --rpc-port=8545 \
  --base-path=/var/lib/eth-rpc \
  --unsafe-rpc-external \
  --rpc-cors=all
Restart=always
RestartSec=10

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
```

3. Start:
```bash
sudo systemctl daemon-reload
sudo systemctl enable eth-rpc
sudo systemctl start eth-rpc
```

Key configuration parameters:
- `--node-rpc-url`: Polkadot SDK node WebSocket URL (default ws://127.0.0.1:9944)
- `--rpc-port`: Ethereum RPC port (default 8545)
- `--eth-pruning`: `archive` for full history, or `<N>` for in-memory last N blocks

API endpoints after setup:
- Polkadot SDK RPC: port 9944 (WebSocket/HTTP)
- Ethereum RPC: port 8545 (HTTP)

NOTE: The developer must execute on their server. Use a reverse proxy for production.
steps.expected_output: Ethereum RPC adapter running and responding to eth_chainId, eth_blockNumber queries on port 8545.

error_patterns.cause: The Polkadot SDK RPC node is not accessible at the configured WebSocket URL.
error_patterns.resolution: Ensure the Polkadot Hub node is running and the RPC port 9944 is accessible. Check --node-rpc-url configuration.

error_patterns.cause: The eth-rpc adapter is querying historical state on a pruned node.
error_patterns.resolution: Use an archive node (--state-pruning=archive) for full Ethereum RPC compatibility.

error_patterns.cause: Another node instance is using the same data directory.
error_patterns.resolution: Stop the existing process before starting a new one.

supplementary_context.description: Polkadot Hub RPC node setup with Ethereum RPC adapter for EVM compatibility.

pages.relevance: Primary source page for Polkadot Hub RPC node and eth-rpc adapter

skills.title: Run a Block-Producing Collator for Polkadot System Parachains
skills.objective: Set up a block-producing collator node for a Polkadot system parachain, generate session keys, and register on-chain for block production.

runtime: Ubuntu 22.04 LTS or similar Linux distribution
network: Public IP address
Stable 100+ Mbps connection
Port 30333 open for parachain P2P
Port 30334 open for relay chain P2P
tokens: Funded Polkadot account for on-chain transactions and potential bonding
wallet: Polkadot account with sr25519 keys (stash account)
hardware: Memory: 32 GB RAM minimum (64 GB recommended)
Storage: 200+ GB NVMe SSD with pruning enabled
Fast disk I/O critical for block production

steps.description:
Choose Docker or systemd deployment.

**Docker:**
```bash
docker pull parity/polkadot-parachain:latest
docker run --rm parity/polkadot-parachain:latest --version
```

**systemd (binary):**
```bash
wget https://github.com/paritytech/polkadot-sdk/releases/download/INSERT_VERSION/polkadot-parachain
chmod +x polkadot-parachain
sudo mv polkadot-parachain /usr/local/bin/
sudo chown root:root /usr/local/bin/polkadot-parachain
polkadot-parachain --version
```

NOTE: The developer must execute on their server.
steps.expected_output: polkadot-parachain binary or Docker image available with version output.

steps.description:
A stable node key provides a consistent peer ID across restarts.

```bash
sudo mkdir -p /var/lib/polkadot-collator
docker run -it parity/subkey:latest generate-node-key > /var/lib/polkadot-collator/node.key
```

The command outputs a peer ID like:
```
12D3KooWExcVYu7Mvjd4kxPVLwN2ZPnZ5NyLZ5ft477wqzfP2q6E
```

IMPORTANT: Save the peer ID for future reference. The node key file must be kept secure.
steps.expected_output: Node key file created and peer ID displayed.

steps.description:
Download the chain spec for your target system parachain.

**From Chainspec Collection (recommended):**
```bash
# Example for Asset Hub (Polkadot Hub)
curl -sL -o chain-spec.json \
  https://paritytech.github.io/chainspecs/polkadot/parachain/asset-hub/chainspec.json
```

System parachain Para IDs:
- Polkadot Hub (Asset Hub): 1000
- Bridge Hub: 1002
- People Chain: 1004
- Coretime Chain: 1005

Find URLs at https://paritytech.github.io/chainspecs/ under List of Chainspecs.
steps.expected_output: Chain specification JSON file downloaded.

steps.description:
Start the collator. The developer must execute on their server as this is a long-running process.

**Docker:**
```bash
mkdir -p collator-data
cp chain-spec.json collator-data/
cp /var/lib/polkadot-collator/node.key collator-data/

docker run -d --name polkadot-collator --restart unless-stopped \
  -p 30333:30333 -p 30334:30334 -p 9944:9944 -p 9615:9615 \
  -v $(pwd)/collator-data:/data \
  -v $(pwd)/chain-spec.json:/chain-spec.json \
  parity/polkadot-parachain:latest \
  --collator \
  --chain=/chain-spec.json --base-path=/data \
  --port=30333 --rpc-port=9944 \
  --prometheus-port=9615 --prometheus-external \
  --node-key-file=/data/node.key \
  --name="INSERT_YOUR_COLLATOR_NAME" \
  --blocks-pruning=256 --state-pruning=256 --database=paritydb \
  -- \
  --chain=polkadot --port=30334 --sync=fast \
  --blocks-pruning=256 --state-pruning=256 --database=paritydb \
  --pool-limit=0 --rpc-port=0
```

**systemd:**
1. Prepare directories:
```bash
sudo useradd -r -s /bin/bash polkadot
sudo cp chain-spec.json /var/lib/polkadot-collator/
sudo chown -R polkadot:polkadot /var/lib/polkadot-collator
```

2. Create `/etc/systemd/system/polkadot-collator.service`:
```ini
[Unit]
Description=Polkadot System Parachain Collator
After=network.target

[Service]
Type=simple
User=polkadot
Group=polkadot
WorkingDirectory=/var/lib/polkadot-collator
ExecStart=/usr/local/bin/polkadot-parachain \
  --collator \
  --chain=/var/lib/polkadot-collator/chain-spec.json \
  --base-path=/var/lib/polkadot-collator \
  --port=30333 --rpc-port=9944 \
  --prometheus-port=9615 \
  --node-key-file=/var/lib/polkadot-collator/node.key \
  --name="INSERT_YOUR_COLLATOR_NAME" \
  --blocks-pruning=256 --state-pruning=256 --database=paritydb \
  -- \
  --chain=polkadot --port=30334 --sync=fast \
  --blocks-pruning=256 --state-pruning=256 --database=paritydb \
  --pool-limit=0 --rpc-port=0
Restart=always
RestartSec=10
LimitNOFILE=65536

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
```

3. Start:
```bash
sudo systemctl daemon-reload
sudo systemctl enable polkadot-collator
sudo systemctl start polkadot-collator
```

Key flags:
- `--collator`: Enables block production mode
- `--database=paritydb`: Better performance
- `--sync=fast`: Fast sync for relay chain
- `--pool-limit=0`: Disables transaction pool on relay chain

Monitor logs:
- Docker: `docker logs -f polkadot-collator`
- systemd: `sudo journalctl -u polkadot-collator -f`

NOTE: Both relay chain and parachain must fully sync before block production. Do NOT proceed to key registration until synced.
steps.expected_output: Collator starts syncing relay chain (fast sync) and parachain.

steps.description:
Once the node is fully synced, generate session keys via RPC:
```bash
curl -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"id":1, "jsonrpc":"2.0", "method": "author_rotateKeys", "params":[]}' \
  http://localhost:9944
```

This returns session keys as a hex string. IMPORTANT: Save the session keys output - you need them for on-chain registration. If you wipe the database, you must regenerate keys.
steps.expected_output: JSON response containing hex-encoded session keys in the result field.

steps.description:
System parachains use governance-based invulnerables lists for collator selection.

1. Check existing collators in Polkadot.js Apps:
   - Connect to your system parachain
   - Go to Developer > Chain State
   - Query collatorSelection.invulnerables()

2. For invulnerables-based selection (most system parachains):
   - Draft a governance proposal explaining why you should be added
   - Include session keys, account ID, hardware specs, monitoring setup, and experience
   - Submit to relevant governance channels

3. Once approved, register session keys:
   - In Polkadot.js Apps > Developer > Extrinsics
   - Select your account
   - Choose session.setKeys extrinsic
   - Enter keys (from author_rotateKeys) and proof (0x00)
   - Submit and sign the transaction

4. For non-system parachains with on-chain bonding:
   - Select collatorSelection.registerAsCandidate
   - The CandidacyBond is automatically reserved

5. Verify collator is active by monitoring logs for:
   - 'Prepared block for proposing'
   - 'Imported #123'

NOTE: This step requires manual interaction with Polkadot.js Apps in a browser.
steps.expected_output: Session keys registered on-chain and collator producing blocks after governance approval.

error_patterns.cause: Node is not fully synced or RPC is not accessible.
error_patterns.resolution: Wait for both relay chain and parachain to fully sync before generating session keys.

error_patterns.pattern: Bootnode with peer id .* is on a different chain
error_patterns.cause: Wrong chain specification file.
error_patterns.resolution: Download the correct chain spec for your target system parachain.

error_patterns.pattern: Node is a collator but no collator service is running
error_patterns.cause: The --collator flag was not passed or the node is not registered.
error_patterns.resolution: Ensure --collator is in the startup command and session keys are registered on-chain.

supplementary_context.description: Collator setup for Polkadot system parachains including registration and session key management.

pages.relevance: Primary source page for collator node setup and registration

skills.title: Set Up a Polkadot Validator Node
skills.objective: Set up, configure, and activate a Polkadot validator node from initial system preparation through bonding DOT and joining the active validator set.

runtime: Linux-based OS with Kernel 5.16 or later
network: Symmetric 500 Mbit/s networking
Port 30333 open for P2P libp2p
Public IP address or properly configured NAT
tokens: Minimum self-stake of 10,000 DOT on stash account (as of March 2026 runtime upgrade)
Sufficient DOT to meet minimum validator bond (check current requirements - was ~1.16M DOT backing in era 1632)
Additional DOT for transaction fees
wallet: Polkadot stash account for bonding DOT
hardware: CPU: x86-64, 8 physical cores @ 3.4 GHz (Intel Ice Lake+ or AMD Zen3+)
Storage: 2 TB+ NVMe SSD (low latency prioritized)
SMT/Hyper-Threading should be disabled for optimal single-threaded performance

steps.description:
Configure the server for validator operations. The developer must execute on their server.

**Verify NTP synchronization:**
```bash
timedatectl
```
Should show 'System clock synchronized: yes'. If not:
```bash
sudo apt-get install ntp
sudo ntpq -p
```

**Verify Landlock is activated:**
```bash
dmesg | grep landlock || journalctl -kg landlock
```
If no output, Landlock may need to be enabled in kernel config.

**Verify seccomp (for Secure-Validator mode):**
```bash
cat /boot/config-$(uname -r) | grep CONFIG_SECCOMP=
```
Should return `CONFIG_SECCOMP=y`.
steps.expected_output: NTP synchronized, Landlock active, seccomp enabled.

steps.description:
Install three required binaries: polkadot, polkadot-prepare-worker, and polkadot-execute-worker. All must be in the same directory.

**Option A - Official releases (recommended):**
```bash
# Download and verify each binary
curl -LO https://github.com/paritytech/polkadot-sdk/releases/download/INSERT_VERSION/polkadot
curl -LO https://github.com/paritytech/polkadot-sdk/releases/download/INSERT_VERSION/polkadot.asc
curl -LO https://github.com/paritytech/polkadot-sdk/releases/download/INSERT_VERSION/polkadot-prepare-worker
curl -LO https://github.com/paritytech/polkadot-sdk/releases/download/INSERT_VERSION/polkadot-prepare-worker.asc
curl -LO https://github.com/paritytech/polkadot-sdk/releases/download/INSERT_VERSION/polkadot-execute-worker
curl -LO https://github.com/paritytech/polkadot-sdk/releases/download/INSERT_VERSION/polkadot-execute-worker.asc

# Verify signatures
gpg --keyserver hkps://keyserver.ubuntu.com --receive-keys 90BD75EBBB8E95CB3DA6078F94A4029AB4B35DAE
gpg --verify polkadot.asc
gpg --verify polkadot-prepare-worker.asc
gpg --verify polkadot-execute-worker.asc

chmod +x polkadot polkadot-prepare-worker polkadot-execute-worker
sudo mv polkadot polkadot-prepare-worker polkadot-execute-worker /usr/local/bin/
```

**Option B - APT package manager (Debian/Ubuntu):**
```bash
gpg --keyserver hkps://keyserver.ubuntu.com --receive-keys 90BD75EBBB8E95CB3DA6078F94A4029AB4B35DAE
gpg --export 90BD75EBBB8E95CB3DA6078F94A4029AB4B35DAE > /usr/share/keyrings/parity.gpg
echo 'deb [signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/parity.gpg] https://releases.parity.io/deb release main' > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/parity.list
apt update
apt install parity-keyring
apt install polkadot
```

**Option C - Docker:**
```bash
docker pull parity/polkadot:INSERT_VERSION
```

Verify installation:
```bash
polkadot --version
polkadot-execute-worker --version
polkadot-prepare-worker --version
```
All versions must match.

IMPORTANT: Signature verification with 'Good signature' confirms binary authenticity. Any other output signals a security risk.
steps.expected_output: All three binaries report matching version numbers.

steps.action: Generate and configure the node key
steps.description:
Validators need a static node key for stable peer identity across restarts.

```bash
polkadot key generate-node-key --file /path/to/node.key
```
Or use default path:
```bash
polkadot key generate-node-key --default-base-path
```

Save the file path - you will add `--node-key-file /path/to/node.key` to your startup command.

IMPORTANT: Without a stable node key, validators on Polkadot 1.11+ will get a startup error about ephemeral network keys.
steps.expected_output: Node key file generated and peer ID displayed.

steps.description:
Sync the node before activating validator mode. The developer must run this on their server.

**Full sync (default):**
```bash
polkadot --chain polkadot
```

**Warp sync (faster initial sync):**
```bash
polkadot --sync warp
```

For TestNets:
```bash
polkadot --chain=kusama  # or --chain=westend
```

Optional: Use database snapshots from https://snapshots.polkadot.io/ to speed up sync (though syncing from scratch is recommended for security).

Monitor progress by watching logs - compare your block height with Telemetry (https://telemetry.polkadot.io/) or Polkadot.js Apps Explorer.

If you see `peers=0`, ensure port 30333 is open.

NOTE: This is a long-running process. The developer must execute on their server and wait for full sync.
steps.expected_output: Node fully synced - node name appears white on Polkadot Telemetry.

steps.description:
Session keys are required for consensus participation. As of runtime 2.2.0, use author_rotateKeysWithOwner which returns both keys and an ownership proof.

```bash
curl -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"id":1, "jsonrpc":"2.0", "method": "author_rotateKeysWithOwner", "params":["INSERT_STASH_ACCOUNT_ID"]}' \
  http://localhost:9944
```

The response contains:
- `keys`: hex-encoded session keys
- `proof`: ownership proof

Example response:
```json
{
  "jsonrpc": "2.0",
  "result": {
    "keys": "0xda3861a45e0197f3ca145c2c209f9126e5053fas503e459af4255cf8011d51010",
    "proof": "0x1a2b3c4d5e6f..."
  },
  "id": 1
}
```

IMPORTANT: Save BOTH the keys and proof values. You need them for on-chain registration. The old author_rotateKeys and Subkey approach are no longer supported for new key generation.
steps.expected_output: JSON response with keys and proof hex strings.

steps.description:
Submit session keys and proof on-chain. Two paths available:

**Polkadot Hub (Recommended):**
1. Connect Polkadot.js Apps to Polkadot Hub (Asset Hub)
2. Navigate to Developer > Extrinsics
3. Select your stash account
4. Choose stakingRcClient > setKeys
5. Enter: keys (hex string), proof (hex string), maxDeliveryAndRemoteExecutionFee (None)
6. Submit and sign

NOTE: Setting keys on Polkadot Hub requires ~60 DOT deposit. Released only via stakingRcClient.purgeKeys on Hub.

**Relay Chain (Legacy - will be deprecated):**
1. Connect to relay chain
2. Developer > Extrinsics
3. Choose session > setKeys
4. Enter keys and proof
5. Submit and sign

Verify keys are set:
- Use hasSessionKeys RPC call or check via Polkadot.js Apps RPC interface

NOTE: This step requires browser-based interaction with Polkadot.js Apps.
steps.expected_output: Session keys registered on-chain with ExtrinsicSuccess confirmation.

steps.description:
Bond DOT tokens and signal intent to validate.

**Bond DOT:**
1. Go to Polkadot.js Apps > Network > Staking > Accounts
2. Click + Stash
3. Select stash account, enter bond amount, set payment destination
4. Click Bond and sign

Unbonding period: 28 days for Polkadot (expected to reduce to ~2 days for nominators per RFC-0097, validator unbonding may differ).

**Activate validator:**
1. In Staking > Accounts, click + Validator
2. Set bond amount, click Next
3. Paste session keys and proof from author_rotateKeysWithOwner
4. Set commission (minimum 10% required as of March 2026)
5. Click Bond & Validate and sign

Minimum commission of 10% is enforced on-chain. Validators below 10% are permissionlessly chill-able.
Minimum self-stake of 10,000 DOT required.

**Set up as systemd service for production:**
Create `/etc/systemd/system/polkadot-validator.service` with the validator startup command including `--validator` and `--node-key-file` flags. Include `RestartSec` delay to prevent equivocation on crash recovery.

```bash
systemctl enable polkadot-validator.service
systemctl start polkadot-validator.service
systemctl status polkadot-validator.service
journalctl -f -u polkadot-validator
```

Monitor on Telemetry - your node may initially be in the waiting queue. The validator set refreshes each era (24 hours on Polkadot). Increasing stake or gaining nominators improves selection chances.

NOTE: This step requires browser interaction with Polkadot.js Apps and systemd commands on the server.
steps.expected_output: Validator bonded, session keys set, and node either active or in the waiting queue for the next era.

error_patterns.pattern: Detected that the weights file was not generated by the native runtime
error_patterns.cause: Binary version mismatch between polkadot and worker binaries.
error_patterns.resolution: Ensure polkadot, polkadot-prepare-worker, and polkadot-execute-worker are all the same version and in the same directory.

error_patterns.pattern: Network key file.*was not found.*refusing to start with an ephemeral network key
error_patterns.cause: No stable node key configured (required since Polkadot 1.11).
error_patterns.resolution: Generate a node key with 'polkadot key generate-node-key --file /path/to/node.key' and add --node-key-file flag.

error_patterns.cause: NTP is not running, causing clock drift.
error_patterns.resolution: Install and start NTP: sudo apt-get install ntp

error_patterns.cause: Validator was chilled because commission is below 10% or self-stake below 10,000 DOT.
error_patterns.resolution: Increase commission to at least 10% and ensure minimum 10,000 DOT self-stake, then re-validate.

error_patterns.cause: No network peers found.
error_patterns.resolution: Ensure libp2p port 30333 is open and accessible through firewall.

supplementary_context.description: Comprehensive validator setup spanning system preparation, binary installation, key management, and activation.
