skills.title: Deploy Ephemeral Test Networks with Zombienet
skills.objective: Install Zombienet, configure a test network with relay chain validators and parachain collators, spawn it, and write automated tests using the Zombienet DSL.

runtime: Node binaries (polkadot, polkadot-parachain) for native provider
network: Internet connection to download Zombienet and node binaries

steps.action: Download and install the Zombienet binary
steps.description: Download the Zombienet executable for your platform from the GitHub releases page. On macOS, you may need to remove the quarantine attribute.
steps.expected_output: The zombienet binary is installed and available in PATH.

steps.action: Verify the installation and set up required binaries
steps.description: Verify Zombienet is installed, then use its setup command to download the polkadot and polkadot-parachain binaries needed for the native provider.
steps.expected_output: Zombienet version is printed. The polkadot and polkadot-parachain binaries are downloaded and placed in the current directory.

steps.description: Add the downloaded binaries to PATH so Zombienet can find them.
steps.expected_output: The polkadot and polkadot-parachain binaries are accessible from PATH.

steps.description: Write a TOML configuration file defining the relay chain with validators and a parachain with a collator. Download the Paseo Asset Hub chain spec for the parachain.
commands:
cat > network.toml << 'TOMLEOF'
[settings]
timeout = 1000
provider = "native"

[relaychain]
chain = "paseo"
default_command = "polkadot"

[[relaychain.nodes]]
name = "alice"
validator = true

[[relaychain.nodes]]
name = "bob"
validator = true

[[parachains]]
id = 1000
chain_spec_path = "./paseo-asset-hub.json"

[parachains.collator]
name = "collator-01"
command = "polkadot-parachain"
TOMLEOF
steps.expected_output: A network.toml file and paseo-asset-hub.json chain spec are created.

steps.description: Use Zombienet to spawn the configured test network. This is a long-running foreground process that starts validators, registers the parachain, and starts the collator.
steps.expected_output: Zombienet displays connection endpoints for all nodes. Relay chain and parachain are producing blocks. Press Ctrl+C to stop.

steps.description: Create a .zndsl test file that uses Zombienet's Domain Specific Language to assert nodes are running and the parachain is producing blocks.
commands:
cat > test.zndsl << 'DSLEOF'
Description: Basic network health test
Network: ./network.toml
Creds: config

alice: is up
bob: is up
alice: parachain 1000 is registered within 200 seconds
alice: parachain 1000 block height is at least 5 within 300 seconds
DSLEOF
steps.expected_output: A test.zndsl file is created with assertions for network health and parachain block production.

steps.description: Execute the Zombienet test. Zombienet will spawn the network, run all assertions, and report pass/fail for each test.
steps.expected_output: Each assertion is evaluated and reported as PASS or FAIL. The network is cleaned up after the test completes.

error_patterns.cause: Zombienet binary is not installed or not in PATH.
error_patterns.resolution: Download Zombienet from https://github.com/paritytech/zombienet/releases and move it to /usr/local/bin/ or add its directory to PATH.

error_patterns.cause: macOS Gatekeeper blocks the Zombienet binary.
error_patterns.resolution: Run: xattr -d com.apple.quarantine zombienet

error_patterns.cause: Required node binaries are not in PATH.
error_patterns.resolution: Run 'zombienet setup polkadot polkadot-parachain' and add the resulting binaries to PATH.

error_patterns.cause: Network startup exceeded the configured timeout.
error_patterns.resolution: Increase the timeout value in [settings] of network.toml. Ensure all binaries are compatible versions.

error_patterns.cause: The parachain was not registered within the assertion timeout.
error_patterns.resolution: Increase the timeout in the test assertion. Verify the parachain configuration and collator binary are correct.

supplementary_context.description: Zombienet supports multiple providers, extensive configuration, and a test DSL for automated network testing.

pages.relevance: Step-by-step tutorial for building a parachain binary and running it with Zombienet, complementing this reference guide.

skills.title: Interact with the XCM Precompile via Remix
skills.objective: Use Remix IDE to connect to the XCM precompile on Polkadot Hub, weigh XCM messages, and execute cross-chain instructions via the IXcm Solidity interface.

network: RPC endpoint: https://services.polkadothub-rpc.com/testnet (Polkadot Hub TestNet, chain ID 420420417)
Base fee: 1000 gwei
tokens: Testnet PAS tokens from https://faucet.polkadot.io/ — needed for executing XCM messages
wallet: MetaMask or compatible EVM wallet connected to Polkadot Hub TestNet and funded with PAS tokens

steps.action: Open Remix IDE and create the IXcm interface file
steps.description:
Navigate to https://remix.ethereum.org/. Create a new file called IXcm.sol. Paste the IXcm interface source code from the Polkadot SDK:
https://raw.githubusercontent.com/paritytech/polkadot-sdk/cb629d46ebf00aa65624013a61f9c69ebf02b0b4/polkadot/xcm/pallet-xcm/src/precompiles/IXcm.sol

The interface defines:
- A Weight struct with refTime and proofSize components
- execute(bytes calldata message, Weight calldata maxWeight) - for local XCM execution
- send(bytes calldata dest, bytes calldata message) - for cross-chain message transmission
- weighMessage(bytes calldata message) - for cost estimation

All XCM messages must be SCALE-encoded.

Non-interactive alternative: Not applicable — the XCM precompile interaction requires Remix browser UI.
steps.expected_output: IXcm.sol file created in Remix with the precompile interface.

steps.description: In the Solidity Compiler tab, compile IXcm.sol by clicking the 'Compile IXcm.sol' button or pressing Ctrl+S.
steps.expected_output: Green checkmark on the compiler icon. No errors.

steps.action: Connect to the XCM precompile at its fixed address
steps.description:
In the 'Deploy & Run Transactions' tab:
1. Ensure MetaMask is connected to Polkadot Hub TestNet (Injected Provider - MetaMask)
2. Select 'IXcm' from the contract dropdown
3. Enter the precompile address in the 'At Address' field: 0x00000000000000000000000000000000000a0000
4. Click the 'At Address' button

This connects Remix to the existing precompile at that fixed address — it does NOT deploy a new contract.
steps.expected_output: The IXcm interface appears in the 'Deployed Contracts' section with execute, send, and weighMessage functions visible.

steps.description:
Expand the deployed IXcm contract. Call the weighMessage function with a SCALE-encoded XCM message. For testing, use this example encoded message:

0x050c000401000003008c86471301000003008c8647000d010101000000010100368e8759910dab756d344995f1d3c79374ca8f70066d3a709e48029f6bf0ee7e

This message encodes three XCM instructions:
- WithdrawAsset: Removes assets from the caller's account
- BuyExecution: Purchases execution time on the destination chain
- DepositAsset: Deposits remaining assets into a specified beneficiary account

Paste the hex bytes into the 'message' input field and click the function button.
steps.expected_output: The function returns a Weight struct with refTime and proofSize values indicating the estimated computational cost.

steps.description:
Call the execute function with:
- message: the same SCALE-encoded XCM message bytes from step 4
- maxWeight: the Weight struct (refTime, proofSize) returned by weighMessage in step 4

Click the 'Transact' button. Approve the transaction in MetaMask.

Note: The execute function is the main entrypoint for cross-chain interactions. Always call weighMessage first to determine the required weight parameters.
steps.expected_output: Transaction succeeds. Remix terminal shows the transaction receipt. Verify by checking that the beneficiary account received the expected funds.

steps.description:
The send function transmits an XCM message to another chain. Call it with:
- dest: SCALE-encoded destination location (XCM location format specifying the target parachain)
- message: SCALE-encoded XCM instructions to execute on the destination

Unlike execute, send does not require a weight parameter — the destination chain handles execution costs.

Use case: send is necessary for operations like opening HRMP channels. For most cross-chain operations, execute is preferred.

More XCM examples available at: https://gist.github.com/franciscoaguirre/a6dea0c55e81faba65bedf700033a1a2
steps.expected_output: Transaction succeeds if the destination and message are correctly encoded.

error_patterns.cause: The XCM message is malformed, the SCALE encoding is invalid, or the account has insufficient funds for the XCM operation.
error_patterns.resolution: Verify the XCM message is correctly SCALE-encoded. Ensure the account has enough PAS tokens. Use weighMessage to validate the message before executing.

error_patterns.cause: The precompile address is incorrect or the connected network does not support this precompile.
error_patterns.resolution: Ensure the address is exactly 0x00000000000000000000000000000000000a0000 and that MetaMask is connected to Polkadot Hub TestNet (chain ID 420420417).

error_patterns.cause: The XCM message weight exceeds available block weight or the message cannot be processed.
error_patterns.resolution: Call weighMessage first to check the cost. Ensure the Weight values are within acceptable limits. Try a simpler XCM message.

supplementary_context.description: Background context for using the XCM precompile on Polkadot Hub.

pages.relevance: Primary source guide for the XCM precompile interface, functions, and usage examples.
General Remix IDE setup for connecting to Polkadot Hub.
XCM cross-consensus messaging overview and concepts.

skills.title: Replay and Dry Run XCMs Using Chopsticks
skills.objective: Replay and dry-run cross-chain messages (XCMs) locally using Chopsticks chain forks to debug message flow, trace execution, and diagnose failures without modifying live state.

runtime: Rust toolchain with cargo (for building Wasm runtime with logging)
network: Access to public RPC endpoints for the chains being forked (e.g., wss://polkadot-asset-hub-rpc.polkadot.io)
Knowledge of the block number or hash where the XCM was sent
wallet: A funded Polkadot account if replaying signed transactions (dev accounts available via Chopsticks)

env_vars.name: POLKADOT_BLOCK_NUMBER
env_vars.description: Block number to fork the Polkadot relay chain at (just before the XCM was sent)

env_vars.name: POLKADOT_HUB_BLOCK_NUMBER
env_vars.description: Block number to fork Polkadot Hub at

env_vars.name: ACALA_BLOCK_NUMBER
env_vars.description: Block number to fork the destination parachain at (e.g. Acala)

steps.description: Create a workspace directory, initialize a Node project, and install Chopsticks, TypeScript tooling, and Polkadot API packages.
commands: npm init -y && npm pkg set type=module
steps.expected_output: Project initialized with package.json (type=module), TypeScript configured, all dependencies installed

steps.description: Create a .env file with the block heights for each chain. These should be just before the XCM is sent to allow a full replay.
steps.expected_output: .env file created with block numbers for Polkadot, Polkadot Hub, and Acala

steps.action: Build Wasm runtime with logging enabled (optional but recommended)
steps.description: Clone the polkadot-fellows/runtimes repository and build the Polkadot Hub runtime with the release profile to enable full execution logs in Chopsticks. This step is required for detailed runtime log output.
steps.expected_output: Compiled Wasm runtime copied to replay-xcm-tests/wasms/ directory

steps.action: Create Chopsticks config with Wasm override and logging
steps.description: Download the default Polkadot Asset Hub Chopsticks config and modify it to enable runtime-log-level 5 and Wasm override for detailed XCM tracing.
steps.expected_output: Config file saved to configs/polkadot-asset-hub-override.yaml. Edit it to add: runtime-log-level: 5 and wasm-override: wasms/asset_hub_polkadot_runtime.compact.compressed.wasm

steps.action: Start forked chains with Chopsticks XCM mode
steps.description: Launch Chopsticks in XCM mode, forking the relay chain and parachains locally with full runtime logs enabled.
steps.expected_output: Three local RPC endpoints: Polkadot Hub on ws://localhost:8000, Acala on ws://localhost:8001, Polkadot relay on ws://localhost:8002

steps.action: Add Polkadot Hub descriptor for PAPI
steps.description: Register the Polkadot Hub chain descriptor with PAPI for type-safe API interactions.
steps.expected_output: Descriptor added for polkadotHub chain

steps.action: Create and run the XCM replay script
steps.description: Create a TypeScript script (replay-xcm.ts) that connects to the local Chopsticks fork, constructs the XCM transaction from call data, signs it with a dev account, and submits it to observe execution events and runtime logs.
steps.expected_output: Transaction events showing XCM execution results, including any emitted events, weight consumption, and success/failure status

steps.action: Create and run the dry-run script
steps.description: Create a TypeScript script (dry-run-call.ts) that uses the dry_run_call runtime API to simulate the XCM without state changes. This confirms whether the XCM would execute successfully.
steps.expected_output: Dry run result showing either success confirmation or detailed error information including error type and weight consumed

error_patterns.cause: Chopsticks cannot fetch the specified block from the chain's RPC endpoint
error_patterns.resolution: Verify the block number in .env exists on-chain. Ensure the RPC endpoint is reachable and supports archive queries.

error_patterns.cause: The Wasm override file path in the Chopsticks config is incorrect
error_patterns.resolution: Verify the wasms/ directory contains the compiled runtime and the config YAML points to the correct filename.

error_patterns.cause: The runtime does not support the DryRunApi or the method is not available at the forked block
error_patterns.resolution: Ensure the forked block uses a runtime version that includes xcm_runtime_apis::dry_run::DryRunApi. Try a more recent block.

error_patterns.cause: Rust compilation failure when building the Wasm runtime
error_patterns.resolution: Ensure a compatible Rust nightly toolchain is installed. Run 'rustup update' and 'rustup target add wasm32-unknown-unknown'. Check the runtimes repo README for version requirements.

supplementary_context.description: XCM debugging, Chopsticks forking, and cross-chain message flow documentation

pages.relevance: Primary source — full tutorial for replaying and dry-running XCMs with Chopsticks
Chopsticks documentation for forking parachains
XCM asset transfer basics for cross-chain communication context

skills.title: Set Up Secure WebSocket for a Polkadot Node
skills.objective: Guide the developer through securing a Polkadot node's WebSocket port with SSL using nginx or Apache2 as a reverse proxy, then verify connectivity via Polkadot.js Apps.

runtime: A running Polkadot node with WebSocket RPC enabled (default port 9944)
nginx or Apache2 installed on the server
Certbot installed for LetsEncrypt SSL certificates (recommended)
network: VPS or dedicated server with a public IP
DNS A record pointing to the server
Port 443 open in firewall for WSS traffic

env_vars.name: NODE_DOMAIN
env_vars.description: DNS domain name for the node (e.g., node.example.com)

env_vars.name: WS_PORT
env_vars.description: Internal WebSocket port the Polkadot node listens on (default: 9944)

steps.description: Obtain an SSL certificate for your domain using Certbot (LetsEncrypt). If you already have a certificate, skip to step 2. Alternatively, generate a self-signed certificate with OpenSSL (not recommended for production).
commands:
# Alternative self-signed (not recommended for production):
# openssl req -x509 -nodes -days 365 -newkey rsa:2048 -keyout /etc/ssl/private/selfsigned.key -out /etc/ssl/certs/selfsigned.crt
steps.expected_output: Certbot outputs 'Successfully received certificate' with paths to fullchain.pem and privkey.pem.

steps.description: Install nginx (if not already installed) and create a virtual host configuration that proxies WSS connections to the node's internal WS port. The developer writes this config on their server.
steps.expected_output: Nginx installed and config file created.

steps.description: Optionally add rate limiting to the nginx configuration to protect the node from abuse. Add a limit_req_zone directive in the http block of /etc/nginx/nginx.conf and a limit_req directive in the server block.
steps.expected_output: Rate limiting directives added to nginx configuration.

steps.description: Test and reload the nginx configuration.
commands: sudo nginx -t && sudo systemctl reload nginx
steps.expected_output: nginx -t reports 'syntax is ok' and 'test is successful'.

steps.description: (Alternative) If using Apache2 instead of nginx: install Apache2 with the WebSocket tunnel module and configure the SSL virtual host. Skip this step if you already configured nginx.
commands:
# Write to /etc/apache2/sites-enabled/polkadot-wss.conf:
# <VirtualHost *:443>
#     ServerName INSERT_NODE_DOMAIN
#     SSLEngine on
#     SSLCertificateFile /etc/letsencrypt/live/INSERT_NODE_DOMAIN/fullchain.pem
#     SSLCertificateKeyFile /etc/letsencrypt/live/INSERT_NODE_DOMAIN/privkey.pem
#     ProxyRequests off
#     ProxyPass / ws://127.0.0.1:9944/
#     ProxyPassReverse / ws://127.0.0.1:9944/
# </VirtualHost>
steps.expected_output: Apache2 configured and reloaded with WebSocket proxy and SSL.

steps.description: Verify the secure WebSocket connection by connecting via Polkadot.js Apps: open https://polkadot.js.org/apps, click the logo in the top left, enable the Development toggle, and enter your WSS endpoint.
commands:
# Open Polkadot.js Apps and connect to:
# wss://INSERT_NODE_DOMAIN:443
steps.expected_output: Polkadot.js Apps connects successfully and shows the chain syncing or synced state.

error_patterns.cause: The proxy is not correctly forwarding WebSocket upgrade requests, or the node is not running.
error_patterns.resolution: Verify the node is running and listening on port 9944. Check the proxy config includes Upgrade and Connection headers for WebSocket support.

error_patterns.cause: Using a self-signed SSL certificate that the browser does not trust.
error_patterns.resolution: Use a LetsEncrypt certificate via Certbot instead of a self-signed cert. If self-signed is required, add the certificate to the browser's trust store.

error_patterns.cause: Nginx cannot reach the backend Polkadot node on the configured port.
error_patterns.resolution: Verify the Polkadot node is running and the proxy_pass port matches the node's WS RPC port (default 9944).

supplementary_context.description: Related documentation for running Polkadot nodes and bootnode WSS configuration.

pages.relevance: Bootnode setup including nginx WSS proxy for P2P WebSocket connections
Full node setup which is a prerequisite for this guide

skills.title: Manage Polkadot Validator Session and Node Keys
skills.objective: Guide the developer through generating session keys with ownership proof, submitting them on-chain via Polkadot Hub or the relay chain, and configuring a stable node key for their validator.

runtime: polkadot binary installed and available in PATH
A running and synced Polkadot validator node with RPC accessible on localhost:9944
network: Access to Polkadot.js Apps (https://polkadot.js.org/apps)
tokens: Approximately 60 DOT deposit required when setting session keys via Polkadot Hub (stakingRcClient.setKeys)
wallet: Validator stash account with bonded DOT
Staking proxy account (if applicable)

env_vars.name: STASH_ACCOUNT_ID
env_vars.description: The SS58 or hex account ID of the validator's stash account

env_vars.name: NODE_RPC_URL
env_vars.description: RPC endpoint of the validator node (default: http://localhost:9944)

env_vars.name: NODE_KEY_PATH
env_vars.description: File path to store the generated node key (e.g., /home/polkadot/node-key)

steps.description: Generate new session keys with an ownership proof by calling the author_rotateKeysWithOwner RPC on your validator node. Replace INSERT_STASH_ACCOUNT_ID with your stash account ID. This must be run on the validator server itself (or via SSH).
steps.expected_output: JSON response with result containing 'keys' (hex-encoded session keys) and 'proof' (hex-encoded ownership proof). Save both values.

steps.description: (Recommended) Submit session keys via Polkadot Hub (Asset Hub). In Polkadot.js Apps: connect to Polkadot Hub, navigate to Developer > Extrinsics, select your stash account, choose the stakingRcClient pallet and setKeys extrinsic, paste the 'keys' and 'proof' values from step 1, and submit. This path is required for pure proxy stash accounts and Staking Operator proxies.
commands:
# Via Polkadot.js Apps UI:
# 1. Connect to Polkadot Hub (Asset Hub)
# 2. Developer > Extrinsics
# 3. Select stash account (or submit via proxy)
# 4. Pallet: stakingRcClient, Extrinsic: setKeys
# 5. keys: INSERT_SESSION_KEYS_HEX
# 6. proof: INSERT_PROOF_HEX
# 7. maxDeliveryAndRemoteExecutionFee: None
# 8. Submit and sign
steps.expected_output: Transaction included in a block. Polkadot Hub validates the proof and forwards keys to the relay chain via XCM. Approximately 60 DOT deposit is held.

steps.description: (Alternative / Legacy) Submit session keys directly on the relay chain using session.setKeys. This path will be deprecated in a future release. In Polkadot.js Apps: connect to the relay chain, Developer > Extrinsics, select stash account, choose session pallet and setKeys extrinsic, paste keys and proof.
commands:
# Via Polkadot.js Apps UI on relay chain:
# 1. Connect to Polkadot relay chain
# 2. Developer > Extrinsics
# 3. Select stash account
# 4. Pallet: session, Extrinsic: setKeys
# 5. keys: INSERT_SESSION_KEYS_HEX
# 6. proof: INSERT_PROOF_HEX
# 7. Submit and sign
steps.expected_output: Transaction included in a block. Session keys registered on-chain.

steps.description: Verify that session keys are correctly set by querying the node via RPC. Use the hasSessionKeys RPC to confirm the node holds the keys.
commands:
# In Polkadot.js Apps: Developer > RPC Calls
# Select author > hasSessionKeys
# Paste the full session keys hex string
# Execute -- should return true
steps.expected_output: RPC returns true, confirming the node has the session keys in its keystore.

steps.description: Generate a persistent node key to maintain a stable peer ID across restarts. Starting with Polkadot v1.11, validators without a stable network key will see an error on startup.
steps.expected_output: Node key saved to the specified file path. The corresponding peer ID is printed to stderr.

steps.description: Add the --node-key-file flag to your validator's startup command so it uses the persistent node key. Update your systemd service file or startup script accordingly.
steps.expected_output: Node starts with the specified node key and logs a consistent peer ID matching the generated key.

error_patterns.cause: The node does not support author_rotateKeysWithOwner, likely running a version before runtime 2.2.0.
error_patterns.resolution: Upgrade your Polkadot binary to a version that includes runtime 2.2.0 or later. The older author_rotateKeys is no longer supported for new key generation.

error_patterns.cause: Validator started without a persistent node key file (Polkadot v1.11+).
error_patterns.resolution: Generate a node key with 'polkadot key generate-node-key --file PATH' and add '--node-key-file PATH' to the startup command.

error_patterns.cause: Session keys were generated on a different node or the keystore was cleared.
error_patterns.resolution: Re-generate session keys using author_rotateKeysWithOwner on the correct validator node. There is no RPC to generate proof for existing keys.

error_patterns.cause: When submitting via proxy, the force_proxy_type was set incorrectly (e.g., Some(Staking) instead of Some(StakingOperator)).
error_patterns.resolution: Set force_proxy_type to Some(StakingOperator) or None when using a Staking Operator proxy.

supplementary_context.description: Related documentation for validator setup, staking operator proxy, and key security practices.

pages.relevance: Prerequisite validator setup guide that precedes key management
Staking Operator proxy setup which uses session key submission via proxy
Session key rotation procedure during validator node upgrades

skills.title: Set Up a Staking Operator Proxy on Polkadot
skills.objective: Guide the developer through creating a Staking Operator proxy on Polkadot Hub that enables non-custodial validator operations, separating fund management from node operations.

runtime: A running and synced Polkadot validator node (for session key generation)
network: Connection to Polkadot Hub (Asset Hub) via Polkadot.js Apps
tokens: Bonded DOT on the stash account for staking
Approximately 60 DOT deposit for session key registration via stakingRcClient.setKeys
Small amount of DOT for transaction fees
wallet: Stash account (capital provider / staker) with bonded DOT
Operator account (node runner) -- a fresh, dedicated, non-validator account is recommended

env_vars.name: STASH_ACCOUNT
env_vars.description: SS58 address of the staker's stash account that holds bonded DOT

env_vars.name: OPERATOR_ACCOUNT
env_vars.description: SS58 address of the operator account to be granted Staking Operator proxy permissions

env_vars.name: COMMISSION_PERCENT
env_vars.description: Initial validator commission rate in percent (e.g., 5 for 5%)

steps.description: Ensure DOT is bonded on the stash account. If not already bonded, the staker must bond DOT first. This step is performed by the staker (capital provider) on Polkadot Hub. Refer to the Start Validating guide for detailed bonding instructions.
commands:
# Via Polkadot.js Apps on Polkadot Hub:
# 1. Connect to Polkadot Hub (Asset Hub)
# 2. Developer > Extrinsics
# 3. Select stash account
# 4. Pallet: staking, Extrinsic: bond
# 5. Set value (amount to bond) and payee
# 6. Submit and sign
steps.expected_output: Transaction included in a block. DOT is now bonded on the stash account.

steps.description: The staker creates a Staking Operator proxy, delegating operational permissions to the operator account. Use proxy.addProxy on Polkadot Hub. A non-zero delay is recommended for added security -- it gives the staker a window to review and cancel proxy calls.
commands:
# Via Polkadot.js Apps on Polkadot Hub:
# 1. Developer > Extrinsics
# 2. Select stash account (or Any/Staking proxy)
# 3. Pallet: proxy, Extrinsic: addProxy
# 4. delegate: INSERT_OPERATOR_ACCOUNT
# 5. proxy_type: StakingOperator
# 6. delay: 0 (or higher for time-delay security)
# 7. Submit and sign
steps.expected_output: Transaction included in a block. The operator account is now a Staking Operator proxy for the stash.

steps.description: The operator registers intent to validate by calling staking.validate wrapped in proxy.proxy. This must be done BEFORE setting session keys. The operator sets the initial commission rate.
commands:
# Via Polkadot.js Apps on Polkadot Hub:
# 1. Developer > Extrinsics
# 2. Select OPERATOR account
# 3. Pallet: proxy, Extrinsic: proxy
# 4. real: INSERT_STASH_ACCOUNT
# 5. force_proxy_type: None (or Some(StakingOperator))
# 6. call: staking.validate(prefs: { commission: INSERT_COMMISSION })
# 7. Submit and sign
steps.expected_output: Transaction included in a block. The validator is now registered with the specified commission rate.

steps.description: On the validator server, generate session keys with ownership proof using the stash account ID. This is a server-side command run via SSH or terminal on the validator machine.
steps.expected_output: JSON response with 'keys' and 'proof' fields. Save both hex values.

steps.description: The operator submits session keys via a proxy call wrapping stakingRcClient.setKeys on Polkadot Hub. This requires a ~60 DOT deposit on the validator account.
commands:
# Via Polkadot.js Apps on Polkadot Hub:
# 1. Developer > Extrinsics
# 2. Select OPERATOR account
# 3. Pallet: proxy, Extrinsic: proxy
# 4. real: INSERT_STASH_ACCOUNT
# 5. force_proxy_type: None (or Some(StakingOperator))
# 6. call: stakingRcClient.setKeys(keys: INSERT_KEYS_HEX, proof: INSERT_PROOF_HEX)
# 7. Submit and sign
steps.expected_output: Transaction included in a block. Session keys registered on-chain via XCM to relay chain. ~60 DOT deposit held.

steps.description: Verify the proxy is working correctly by checking the proxy list for the stash account and confirming the session keys are set.
commands:
# Via Polkadot.js Apps on Polkadot Hub:
# 1. Developer > Chain State
# 2. Pallet: proxy, Query: proxies
# 3. Enter stash account
# 4. Verify the StakingOperator proxy entry exists
#
# Also verify session keys:
# Developer > RPC Calls > author > hasSessionKeys
steps.expected_output: Proxy list shows the operator account with StakingOperator type. hasSessionKeys returns true.

error_patterns.cause: The force_proxy_type was set to Some(Staking) instead of Some(StakingOperator) or None. The proxy pallet requires an exact match.
error_patterns.resolution: Set force_proxy_type to Some(StakingOperator) or None when submitting proxy calls.

error_patterns.pattern: Cannot set keys before calling validate
error_patterns.cause: stakingRcClient.setKeys was called before staking.validate. The order of operations matters.
error_patterns.resolution: Call staking.validate (via proxy.proxy) first to register validator intent, then set session keys.

error_patterns.cause: The validator account does not have the ~60 DOT deposit required for session key registration via Polkadot Hub.
error_patterns.resolution: Ensure the stash account has at least 60 DOT available beyond the bonded amount to cover the session key deposit.

error_patterns.cause: The same operator account was assigned to multiple validators, causing session key conflicts.
error_patterns.resolution: Use a fresh, dedicated, non-validator account as the StakingOperator for each validator. The relationship should be 1:1.

supplementary_context.description: Related documentation for key management, validator onboarding, and proxy operations.

pages.relevance: Detailed session key generation and submission procedures including author_rotateKeysWithOwner

skills.title: Upgrade a Polkadot Validator Node
skills.objective: Guide the developer through a zero-downtime validator node upgrade using a backup node (A/B swap) with proper session key rotation to avoid slashing.

runtime: polkadot binary (current version) installed on Validator A
polkadot binary (new version) available for Validator B
Sufficient VPS capacity to run two validator instances temporarily
network: Both Validator A and Validator B servers accessible via SSH
Access to Polkadot.js Apps (https://polkadot.js.org/apps)
tokens: Approximately 60 DOT deposit if rotating session keys via Polkadot Hub
wallet: Validator stash account with active validator status
Staking proxy account (if applicable)

env_vars.name: STASH_ACCOUNT_ID
env_vars.description: The SS58 or hex account ID of the validator's stash account

env_vars.name: VALIDATOR_B_RPC
env_vars.description: RPC endpoint of Validator B (default: http://localhost:9944 on Validator B's server)

steps.description: Launch Validator B (the backup node) with the new polkadot binary and wait until it is fully synced with the network. Start it with the --validator flag. This is a long-running process on the second server.
steps.expected_output: Validator B starts syncing and eventually reaches the chain head. Logs show 'Idle' with current block height matching the network.

steps.description: Once Validator B is fully synced, generate new session keys with ownership proof on Validator B. Run this on Validator B's server.
steps.expected_output: JSON response with 'keys' and 'proof' fields. Save both hex values.

steps.description: Submit Validator B's session keys on-chain using your staking proxy account. Use stakingRcClient.setKeys on Polkadot Hub (recommended) or session.setKeys on the relay chain. Record the current session number.
commands:
# Via Polkadot.js Apps:
# 1. Connect to Polkadot Hub (or relay chain)
# 2. Developer > Extrinsics
# 3. Select stash/proxy account
# 4. stakingRcClient.setKeys (or session.setKeys)
# 5. keys: INSERT_VALIDATOR_B_KEYS_HEX
# 6. proof: INSERT_VALIDATOR_B_PROOF_HEX
# 7. Submit and sign
# 8. Note the current session number (N)
steps.expected_output: Transaction included in a block. Record session number N.

steps.description: CRITICAL: Keep Validator A running during this entire waiting period. Session key changes take effect after the current session ends plus two additional full sessions (session N+3). Turning off Validator A early may result in chilling or slashing. Monitor session progress in Polkadot.js Apps under Network > Staking.
commands:
# Monitor session progress:
# Polkadot.js Apps > Network > Staking > Overview
# Wait until session N+3 begins
# DO NOT stop Validator A until session N+3
steps.expected_output: Session N+3 begins. Validator B is now the active validator for your stash.

steps.description: Once session N+3 has started, Validator B is active. Safely stop Validator A and perform the required maintenance (upgrade binary, server migration, etc.).
commands:
# On Validator A's server:
sudo systemctl stop polkadot
steps.expected_output: Validator A stops. Validator B continues producing blocks.

steps.description: When maintenance is complete, start the upgraded Validator A, wait for full sync, generate new session keys on Validator A, and submit them on-chain. Then wait another 3 sessions before stopping Validator B.
commands:
# On Validator A:
polkadot --chain polkadot --validator --name validator-a --node-key-file /home/polkadot/node-key-a

# After sync, generate new keys on Validator A:
curl -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"id":1, "jsonrpc":"2.0", "method": "author_rotateKeysWithOwner", "params":["INSERT_STASH_ACCOUNT_ID"]}' http://localhost:9944

# Submit new Validator A keys on-chain (same process as step 3)
# Wait for session change (current session + 2 more)
# Then stop Validator B
steps.expected_output: Validator A resumes as active validator after session key rotation completes. Validator B can be safely stopped.

error_patterns.cause: Both Validator A and Validator B have active session keys and are signing blocks simultaneously with duplicated keystores.
error_patterns.resolution: NEVER copy the keystore folder between validators. Always generate fresh session keys on each node. Ensure only one validator has active session keys at any time.

error_patterns.cause: Validator A was stopped before session N+3, leaving no active validator for the stash during the transition period.
error_patterns.resolution: Always wait for the full session key activation period (current session + 2 additional sessions) before stopping the old validator.

error_patterns.cause: Session keys were submitted before Validator B finished syncing, causing missed blocks.
error_patterns.resolution: Wait for Validator B to be fully synced (logs show 'Idle' at current block height) before generating and submitting session keys.

Equivocation slashing details critical for understanding the risks of key duplication
Hardware and software requirements for running validator nodes

skills.title: Stop Validating on Polkadot
skills.objective: Guide the developer through safely stopping a Polkadot validator by chilling the node, purging session keys, and unbonding staked tokens.

network: Connection to Polkadot relay chain or Polkadot Hub via Polkadot.js Apps
tokens: Small amount of DOT for transaction fees
wallet: Validator stash account or staking proxy account used to set session keys

steps.description: Chill the validator to signal that it should not be considered for the next era's validator set. This does not unbond tokens -- it only stops active validation. Use staking.chill via Polkadot.js Apps. You may also claim any pending staking rewards at this point.
commands:
# Via Polkadot.js Apps:
# 1. Connect to Polkadot relay chain (or Polkadot Hub)
# 2. Developer > Extrinsics
# 3. Select stash account (or staking proxy)
# 4. Pallet: staking, Extrinsic: chill
# 5. Submit and sign
steps.expected_output: Transaction included in a block. Validator status changes to 'chilled' and will not be selected for the next era.

steps.description: Purge the validator's session keys to remove the association between your stash account and the validator's keys. IMPORTANT: Use the same account that originally set the keys (stash or staking proxy). If keys were set via stakingRcClient.setKeys on Polkadot Hub, purge via stakingRcClient.purgeKeys on Polkadot Hub to release the ~60 DOT deposit. Purging via relay chain session.purgeKeys does NOT release the Polkadot Hub deposit.
commands:
# Via Polkadot.js Apps:
# Option A - Polkadot Hub (if keys were set via stakingRcClient):
# 1. Connect to Polkadot Hub
# 2. Developer > Extrinsics
# 3. Select same account that set keys
# 4. Pallet: stakingRcClient, Extrinsic: purgeKeys
# 5. Submit and sign
#
# Option B - Relay chain (legacy):
# 1. Connect to relay chain
# 2. Developer > Extrinsics
# 3. Select same account that set keys
# 4. Pallet: session, Extrinsic: purgeKeys
# 5. Submit and sign
steps.expected_output: Transaction included in a block. Session keys removed. If purged via Polkadot Hub, the ~60 DOT deposit is released.

steps.description: Unbond your staked DOT tokens. This starts the unbonding period (28 days on Polkadot, 7 days on Kusama). After this period, tokens become transferable. You can unbond via the Polkadot.js Apps UI or via the staking.unbond extrinsic.
commands:
# Via Polkadot.js Apps UI:
# 1. Navigate to Network > Staking > Account Actions
# 2. Select stash account
# 3. Click dropdown menu > Unbond Funds
# 4. Enter amount to unbond
# 5. Submit and sign
#
# Alternative via extrinsic:
# Developer > Extrinsics
# Pallet: staking, Extrinsic: unbond
# value: INSERT_AMOUNT_TO_UNBOND
# Submit and sign
steps.expected_output: Transaction included in a block. Unbonding period begins (28 days for Polkadot).

steps.description: After the unbonding period (28 days) completes, withdraw the unbonded tokens to make them fully transferable. Use staking.withdrawUnbonded.
commands:
# Via Polkadot.js Apps:
# 1. Developer > Extrinsics
# 2. Select stash account
# 3. Pallet: staking, Extrinsic: withdrawUnbonded
# 4. num_slashing_spans: 0 (or check current value)
# 5. Submit and sign
steps.expected_output: Transaction included in a block. Tokens are now fully transferable.

error_patterns.pattern: Cannot reap account with active session keys
error_patterns.cause: Attempted to transfer all tokens or reap the stash account without purging session keys first.
error_patterns.resolution: Purge session keys using session.purgeKeys (relay chain) or stakingRcClient.purgeKeys (Polkadot Hub) before attempting to empty the account. Use the same account that originally set the keys.

error_patterns.cause: Attempted to withdraw tokens before the 28-day unbonding period finished.
error_patterns.resolution: Wait for the full unbonding period to complete. Check the remaining time in Polkadot.js Apps under Network > Staking > Account Actions.

error_patterns.cause: Session keys were purged via relay chain session.purgeKeys but were originally set via stakingRcClient.setKeys on Polkadot Hub.
error_patterns.resolution: Purge keys via stakingRcClient.purgeKeys on Polkadot Hub to release the ~60 DOT deposit. The relay chain purge does not release the Hub deposit.

skills.title: Open HRMP Channels Between Parachains
skills.objective: Establish a unidirectional HRMP channel between two parachains by submitting channel request and acceptance XCM messages through the relay chain using Polkadot.js Apps.

runtime: polkadotXcm.send extrinsic available on both parachains
sudo pallet or governance mechanism for dispatching privileged calls
network: Relay chain (e.g., Rococo Local) running
At least two parachains connected (e.g., parachain 2500 and parachain 2600)
tokens: Sufficient relay chain native tokens in sovereign accounts of both parachains to cover XCM transact fees
wallet: Account with funds on the relay chain for initial sovereign account funding
Sudo or governance access on both parachains

env_vars.name: SENDER_PARA_ID
env_vars.description: Parachain ID of the channel initiator (e.g., 2500)

env_vars.name: RECEIVER_PARA_ID
env_vars.description: Parachain ID of the channel acceptor (e.g., 2600)

env_vars.name: RELAY_CHAIN_WS
env_vars.description: WebSocket endpoint of the relay chain (e.g., ws://127.0.0.1:9944)

steps.action: Fund sender parachain sovereign account on relay chain
steps.description: Connect to the relay chain in Polkadot.js Apps. Transfer relay chain native tokens to the sender parachain's sovereign account. For parachain 2500, the sovereign account is 5Ec4AhPSY2GEE4VoHUVheqv5wwq2C1HMKa7c9fVJ1WKivX1Y (derived from prefix 0x70617261 + SCALE-encoded para ID c4090000). Use the 'Para ID to Address' tool at Substrate Utilities (shawntabrizi.com) to calculate for other IDs.
steps.expected_output: Transfer event confirmed on relay chain; sovereign account balance is non-zero.

steps.description: In Polkadot.js Apps connected to the relay chain, navigate to Developer > Extrinsics. Select the hrmp pallet and the hrmpInitOpenChannel extrinsic. Set parameters: recipient = RECEIVER_PARA_ID (e.g., 2600), proposedMaxCapacity = 8, proposedMaxMessageSize = 4096. Copy the encoded call data (e.g., 0x3c00280a00000800000000001000 for parachain 2600).
steps.expected_output: Encoded call data string starting with 0x3c00.

steps.action: Submit XCM message from sender parachain to initiate channel
steps.description: Connect to the sender parachain in Polkadot.js Apps. Submit a sudo.sudo(polkadotXcm.send) extrinsic targeting the relay chain (dest: parents=1, interior=Here). The XCM message must contain: (1) WithdrawAsset - withdraw relay tokens from sovereign account, (2) BuyExecution - pay for execution, (3) Transact - execute the hrmpInitOpenChannel encoded call, (4) RefundSurplus - reclaim unused fees, (5) DepositAsset - return remaining funds to sender sovereign account.
steps.expected_output: Transaction finalized on sender parachain; sudo.Sudid event emitted.

steps.action: Verify channel request on relay chain
steps.description: In Polkadot.js Apps connected to the relay chain, navigate to Developer > Chain State. Query hrmp > hrmpOpenChannelRequests. Look for an entry with your sender and receiver parachain IDs confirming the pending channel request.
steps.expected_output: hrmpOpenChannelRequests shows entry for [SENDER_PARA_ID, RECEIVER_PARA_ID] with confirmed=false.

steps.action: Fund receiver parachain sovereign account on relay chain
steps.description: Transfer relay chain native tokens to the receiver parachain's sovereign account, following the same process as step 1 but for the receiver parachain ID.
steps.expected_output: Transfer event confirmed; receiver sovereign account has sufficient balance.

steps.description: In Polkadot.js Apps connected to the relay chain, navigate to Developer > Extrinsics. Select hrmp pallet and hrmpAcceptOpenChannel extrinsic. Set sender = SENDER_PARA_ID (e.g., 2500). Copy the encoded call data (e.g., 0x3c01c4090000 for accepting from parachain 2500).
steps.expected_output: Encoded call data string starting with 0x3c01.

steps.action: Submit XCM message from receiver parachain to accept channel
steps.description: Connect to the receiver parachain in Polkadot.js Apps. Submit a sudo.sudo(polkadotXcm.send) extrinsic targeting the relay chain. The XCM message structure is the same as step 3 but uses the hrmpAcceptOpenChannel encoded call data and sets the DepositAsset beneficiary to the receiver parachain's sovereign account.
steps.expected_output: Transaction finalized on receiver parachain; sudo.Sudid event emitted.

steps.action: Verify channel establishment on relay chain
steps.description: Wait for the next session on the relay chain. In Polkadot.js Apps, navigate to Developer > Chain State. Query hrmp > hrmpChannels. Verify the unidirectional channel from SENDER_PARA_ID to RECEIVER_PARA_ID exists with the configured capacity and message size.
steps.expected_output: hrmpChannels query shows channel entry [SENDER_PARA_ID, RECEIVER_PARA_ID] with maxCapacity and maxMessageSize values.

error_patterns.cause: A channel request from the sender to the receiver already exists and is pending acceptance.
error_patterns.resolution: Check hrmpOpenChannelRequests to see if the request is already pending. If so, proceed to the acceptance step.

error_patterns.cause: The parachain sovereign account on the relay chain does not have enough tokens to cover XCM transact fees.
error_patterns.resolution: Transfer additional relay chain native tokens to the parachain's sovereign account before retrying.

error_patterns.cause: Attempting to accept a channel that was never requested or has already been accepted.
error_patterns.resolution: Verify the channel request exists by querying hrmpOpenChannelRequests on the relay chain.

pages.relevance: Simplified single-message approach for system chains; useful contrast to the two-step regular parachain process.
Overview of XCM and cross-chain messaging concepts used in channel establishment.

skills.title: Open HRMP Channel with System Parachains
skills.objective: Establish a bidirectional HRMP channel between a parachain and a system chain (e.g., Asset Hub) using a single XCM message via the establish_channel_with_system extrinsic.

runtime: polkadotXcm.send extrinsic available on the parachain
sudo pallet or governance mechanism for dispatching privileged calls
network: Relay chain (e.g., Rococo Local) running
At least one parachain connected (e.g., parachain 2500)
Asset Hub system chain running (parachain 1000)
tokens: Sufficient relay chain native tokens in the parachain sovereign account to cover XCM transact fees
wallet: Account with funds on the relay chain for sovereign account funding
Sudo or governance access on the parachain

env_vars.name: PARA_ID
env_vars.description: Parachain ID that will open a channel with the system chain (e.g., 2500)

env_vars.name: SYSTEM_PARA_ID
env_vars.description: System chain parachain ID (e.g., 1000 for Asset Hub)

env_vars.name: RELAY_CHAIN_WS
env_vars.description: WebSocket endpoint of the relay chain

steps.action: Fund parachain sovereign account on relay chain
steps.description: Connect to the relay chain in Polkadot.js Apps. Transfer relay chain native tokens to your parachain's sovereign account. For parachain 2500, the sovereign account is 5Ec4AhPSY2GEE4VoHUVheqv5wwq2C1HMKa7c9fVJ1WKivX1Y (prefix 0x70617261 + SCALE-encoded para ID). Use the 'Para ID to Address' tool at Substrate Utilities for other IDs.
steps.expected_output: Transfer event confirmed; sovereign account balance is sufficient for XCM fees.

steps.description: In Polkadot.js Apps connected to the relay chain, navigate to Developer > Extrinsics. Select the hrmp pallet and the establishChannelWithSystem extrinsic. Set target_system_chain = SYSTEM_PARA_ID (e.g., 1000). Copy the encoded call data (e.g., 0x3c0ae8030000 for system parachain 1000).
steps.expected_output: Encoded call data string starting with 0x3c0a.

steps.action: Submit XCM message from parachain to relay chain
steps.description: Connect to your parachain in Polkadot.js Apps. Submit a sudo.sudo(polkadotXcm.send) extrinsic targeting the relay chain (dest: parents=1, interior=Here). The XCM message must contain: (1) WithdrawAsset - withdraw relay tokens from sovereign account, (2) BuyExecution - pay for execution with Unlimited weight, (3) Transact - execute the establish_channel_with_system encoded call, (4) RefundSurplus, (5) DepositAsset - return remaining funds to sovereign account.
steps.expected_output: Transaction finalized; sudo.Sudid event emitted on the parachain.

steps.description: After submission, the relay chain emits two HrmpSystemChannelOpened events. Wait for the next session, then connect to the relay chain in Polkadot.js Apps, navigate to Developer > Chain State, and query hrmp > hrmpChannels. Verify two channels exist: one from SYSTEM_PARA_ID to PARA_ID and one from PARA_ID to SYSTEM_PARA_ID.
steps.expected_output: Two HRMP channel entries visible: [1000, 2500] and [2500, 1000], each with maxCapacity and maxMessageSize configured.

error_patterns.cause: A channel between the parachain and system chain already exists.
error_patterns.resolution: Query hrmpChannels to verify existing channels. If both directions exist, no action is needed.

error_patterns.cause: The parachain sovereign account lacks funds for XCM execution fees.
error_patterns.resolution: Transfer additional relay chain tokens to the sovereign account.

error_patterns.cause: The extrinsic was not dispatched with the required root/sudo origin.
error_patterns.resolution: Ensure the polkadotXcm.send call is wrapped in sudo.sudo or submitted through governance.

supplementary_context.description: Related guides for regular parachain channels and XCM fundamentals.

pages.relevance: Two-step process for opening channels between regular parachains; useful for understanding the full HRMP lifecycle.
Introduction to XCM and cross-chain messaging used in channel setup.

skills.title: Renew Coretime for Parachain Operation
skills.objective: Renew coretime allocation for a parachain either manually via broker.renew on the Coretime chain or automatically by enabling auto-renewal through an XCM message from the parachain.

runtime: polkadotXcm.send extrinsic available on the parachain
sudo pallet or governance mechanism for dispatching privileged calls
network: Relay chain running with coretime sales active
Coretime system chain (parachain 1005) running
Parachain with an existing bulk coretime purchase
tokens: Sufficient DOT on the Coretime chain account (for manual renewal) or in the parachain sovereign account on the Coretime chain (for auto-renewal)
Sufficient relay chain tokens in the parachain sovereign account for XCM fees (auto-renewal path)
wallet: Account with funds on the Coretime chain for manual renewal
Sudo or governance access on the parachain for auto-renewal setup
HRMP channel between parachain and Coretime chain established (required for auto-renewal)

env_vars.name: PARA_ID
env_vars.description: Your parachain ID (e.g., 2000)

env_vars.name: CORE_INDEX
env_vars.description: The core number assigned to your parachain (query broker.workload or broker.workplan)

env_vars.name: CORETIME_CHAIN_WS
env_vars.description: WebSocket endpoint of the Coretime system chain

steps.action: Identify your assigned core and renewal timing
steps.description: Connect to the Coretime chain in Polkadot.js Apps. Query broker > workplan to find your parachain's future core assignment. If not in workplan, query broker > workload for the current assignment. Also query broker > saleInfo to determine the current sale phase. Renewals should ideally be completed during the interlude phase to avoid losing access to the core.
steps.expected_output: Core index identified (e.g., 48 for task 2000); saleInfo showing current phase (interlude, lead-in, or fixed price).

steps.description: For manual renewal, connect to the Coretime chain in Polkadot.js Apps. Navigate to Developer > Extrinsics. Select the broker pallet and the renew extrinsic. Set the core parameter to your assigned core index. Submit the transaction from an account with sufficient DOT. This must be done during a sale period, ideally during the interlude phase.
steps.expected_output: broker.Renewed event emitted confirming the core has been renewed for the next period.

steps.action: Auto-renewal: establish HRMP channel with Coretime chain
steps.description: If not already established, open an HRMP channel between your parachain and the Coretime system chain (parachain 1005) following the system chain channel procedure: construct hrmp.establishChannelWithSystem(1005) on the relay chain and submit via XCM from your parachain. See the 'open-hrmp-channel-with-system-chain' skill for detailed steps.
steps.expected_output: Bidirectional HRMP channels between your parachain and the Coretime chain confirmed via hrmpChannels query.

steps.action: Auto-renewal: fund parachain sovereign account on Coretime chain
steps.description: Calculate your parachain's sibling sovereign account on the Coretime chain using prefix 0x7369626c + SCALE-encoded para ID. For parachain 2000, this is 5Eg2fntJ27qsari4FGrGhrMqKFDRnkNSR6UshkZYBGXmSuC8. Transfer sufficient DOT to this account to cover both XCM fees and recurring coretime renewal payments.
steps.expected_output: Sovereign account balance on Coretime chain is sufficient for renewal costs.

steps.description: Query broker > saleInfo on the Coretime chain. If your coretime expires in the current sale period, use the regionBegin value. If already renewed (expires next period), use regionEnd. If using a lease, query broker > leases and use the 'until' value for your task.
steps.expected_output: workload_end_hint value determined (e.g., 322845 from regionBegin or 327885 from regionEnd).

steps.action: Auto-renewal: construct and encode enable_auto_renew extrinsic
steps.description: In Polkadot.js Apps connected to the Coretime chain, navigate to Developer > Extrinsics. Select the broker pallet and the enableAutoRenew extrinsic. Set parameters: core = CORE_INDEX, task = PARA_ID, workload_end_hint = the value from step 5. Copy the encoded call data (e.g., 0x32153000d007000001cd000500 for core 48, task 2000, hint 327885). Also query transactionPaymentCallApi.queryCallInfo with the encoded data to get the weight.
steps.expected_output: Encoded call data and transaction weight (refTime and proofSize) obtained.

steps.action: Auto-renewal: submit XCM from parachain to Coretime chain
steps.description: Connect to your parachain in Polkadot.js Apps. Submit a sudo.sudo(polkadotXcm.send) extrinsic targeting the Coretime chain (dest: parents=1, interior=X1(Parachain: 1005)). Use XCM v5 instructions: (1) WithdrawAsset - DOT from sovereign account, (2) PayFees - pay execution fees, (3) Transact with originKind=SovereignAccount and the encoded enable_auto_renew call data, (4) RefundSurplus, (5) DepositAsset to sovereign account.
steps.expected_output: broker.AutoRenewalEnabled event emitted on the Coretime chain with your core and task values.

error_patterns.cause: All available cores have been sold during the current sale period; renewal was attempted too late.
error_patterns.resolution: Renew during the interlude phase at the start of each sale period to guarantee availability.

error_patterns.cause: The sovereign account on the Coretime chain has insufficient funds to cover the renewal payment.
error_patterns.resolution: Top up the parachain sovereign account on the Coretime chain with enough DOT before the next sale period begins.

error_patterns.cause: The extrinsic origin is not the sovereign account of the task being configured.
error_patterns.resolution: Ensure the XCM originKind is SovereignAccount so the Coretime chain recognizes the parachain's sovereign account as the caller.

error_patterns.cause: Not enough funds to cover the broker.renew cost or XCM execution fees.
error_patterns.resolution: Fund the relevant account (Coretime chain account for manual renewal, sovereign account for auto-renewal).

Unlocking a parachain after migrating from legacy leases to coretime.

skills.title: Implement Storage Migrations for Runtime Upgrades
skills.objective: Implement a versioned storage migration using the OnRuntimeUpgrade trait and VersionedMigration wrapper to safely transform on-chain storage during a Substrate runtime upgrade.

steps.description: Determine whether your runtime upgrade changes storage layout. Migration IS required when: reordering/mutating struct fields, changing data types (e.g., u32 to u64), changing enum variant ordering or inserting variants, changing storage keys, or removing pallets/storage items. Migration is NOT required when: adding new storage items, adding/removing extrinsics, or appending new enum variants at the end.
steps.expected_output: Clear identification of which storage items need migration and the old vs new data formats.

steps.description:
Organize migrations in a dedicated module within your pallet. Create the following file structure:

```
my-pallet/
  src/
    lib.rs          # Main pallet implementation
    migrations/     # All migration-related code
      mod.rs        # Migrations module definition
      v1.rs         # V0 -> V1 migration
```

In mod.rs, declare submodules for each version migration.
steps.expected_output: Migration directory and module files created within the pallet source tree.
UncheckedOnRuntimeUpgrade implementation compiles without errors.

steps.description:
Use VersionedMigration to wrap your UncheckedOnRuntimeUpgrade implementation. This handles storage version checking automatically and prevents re-execution on subsequent upgrades. Define a type alias:

```rust
use frame_support::migrations::VersionedMigration;

pub type MigrateV0ToV1<T> = VersionedMigration<
    0,                          // From version
    1,                          // To version
    InnerMigrateV0ToV1<T>,      // The migration implementation
    pallet::Pallet<T>,          // The pallet being upgraded
    <T as frame_system::Config>::DbWeight,  // Runtime weight info
>;
```

VersionedMigration checks the on-chain StorageVersion before executing and updates it after successful migration.
steps.expected_output: Type alias defined and compiles; VersionedMigration wraps the inner migration.

steps.action: Implement pre_upgrade and post_upgrade test hooks
steps.expected_output: Test hooks compile under try-runtime feature flag.

steps.action: Register migration in the runtime Executive pallet
steps.description:
Add the migration to your runtime configuration in runtime/src/lib.rs. The Executive pallet accepts a tuple of migrations:

```rust
pub type Migrations = (
    pallet_my_pallet::migrations::v1::MigrateV0ToV1<Runtime>,
    // Add additional migrations here in order
);

pub type Executive = frame_executive::Executive<
    Runtime,
    Block,
    frame_system::ChainContext<Runtime>,
    Runtime,
    AllPalletsWithSystem,
    Migrations,  // Pass migrations tuple here
>;
```

Migrations execute in the order they appear in the tuple, before any on_initialize hooks.
steps.expected_output: Runtime compiles with the migration registered in the Executive type.

steps.description: Use the try-runtime CLI to test your migration against live chain state. Build the runtime with the try-runtime feature enabled and run the on-runtime-upgrade command against a live or forked chain snapshot. This executes pre_upgrade, on_runtime_upgrade, and post_upgrade in sequence to validate the migration end-to-end. For single-block migrations, ensure the total weight stays within block limits. For parachains, exceeding block weight limits will brick the chain.
steps.expected_output: try-runtime reports successful pre_upgrade, on_runtime_upgrade, and post_upgrade execution with no panics or assertion failures.

error_patterns.cause: The on-chain storage version does not match the expected 'From' version in VersionedMigration.
error_patterns.resolution: Verify the current on-chain StorageVersion for your pallet. Ensure your migration From version matches the actual on-chain version.

error_patterns.cause: The migration attempted to decode storage using the wrong data format (old format instead of new or vice versa).
error_patterns.resolution: Use frame_support::storage::unhashed::get with the OLD type to read existing storage before transformation. Never read old storage with the new type.

error_patterns.cause: A single-block migration consumed more weight than the maximum allowed per block, bricking a parachain.
error_patterns.resolution: Split the migration into a multi-block migration using the MBM (Multi-Block Migration) framework. Test weight consumption thoroughly with try-runtime before deploying.

error_patterns.cause: The runtime upgrade was applied but the migration was not registered in the Executive pallet Migrations tuple.
error_patterns.resolution: Add the migration type to the Migrations tuple in runtime/src/lib.rs and rebuild the runtime.

supplementary_context.description: Polkadot SDK documentation on migration patterns, the single-block and multi-block migration examples.

pages.relevance: Primary source covering OnRuntimeUpgrade trait, VersionedMigration, testing hooks, and migration scenarios.

skills.title: Unlock a Parachain via XCM
skills.objective: Unlock a locked parachain by sending an XCM message containing the registrar.removeLock extrinsic from the parachain to the relay chain.

runtime: polkadotXcm.send extrinsic available on the parachain
sudo pallet enabled on the parachain (or governance mechanism)
network: Relay chain running (e.g., Polkadot, Kusama, or local testnet)
Parachain deployed and producing blocks
Parachain currently in locked state
tokens: Sufficient relay chain native tokens (DOT/KSM) in the parachain sovereign account to cover XCM transact fees
wallet: Sudo access on the parachain or ability to submit governance proposals
Account with relay chain tokens for funding the sovereign account

env_vars.name: PARA_ID
env_vars.description: The parachain ID to unlock (e.g., 2006)

env_vars.name: RELAY_CHAIN_WS
env_vars.description: WebSocket endpoint of the relay chain

env_vars.name: PARACHAIN_WS
env_vars.description: WebSocket endpoint of the parachain

steps.description: In Polkadot.js Apps connected to the relay chain, navigate to Developer > Chain State. Select the registrar pallet and the paras query. Input your parachain ID (e.g., 2006) and execute. The result shows manager, deposit, and locked fields. Confirm locked is true before proceeding.
steps.expected_output: Query result shows locked: true for the specified parachain ID.

steps.description: In Polkadot.js Apps connected to the relay chain, navigate to Developer > Extrinsics. Select the registrar pallet and the removeLock extrinsic. Set the para parameter to your parachain ID (e.g., 2006). Copy the encoded call data (e.g., 0x4604d6070000 for parachain 2006). Verify by decoding the call data in Polkadot.js Apps.
steps.expected_output: Encoded call data string (e.g., 0x4604d6070000) confirmed as a valid registrar.removeLock call.

steps.description: In Polkadot.js Apps connected to the relay chain, navigate to Developer > Runtime calls. Execute the transactionPaymentCallApi.queryCallInfo runtime call with the encoded call data from step 2. Note the refTime and proofSize weight values for use in the XCM Transact instruction.
steps.expected_output: Weight object with refTime and proofSize values (e.g., refTime: 123456789, proofSize: 4567).

steps.action: Fund parachain sovereign account on relay chain
steps.description: Calculate the parachain's sovereign account using prefix 0x70617261 (child/parent) + SCALE-encoded para ID. For parachain 2006: 0x70617261d6070000... = 5Ec4AhPW97z4ZyYkd3mYkJrSeZWcwVv4wiANES2QrJi1x17F. Alternatively, use the locationToAccountApi.convertLocation runtime API or Substrate Utilities. Transfer sufficient DOT/KSM to this account to cover XCM fees.
steps.expected_output: Sovereign account funded with sufficient relay chain tokens.

steps.action: Craft and submit XCM message from parachain
steps.description: Connect to the parachain in Polkadot.js Apps. Submit a sudo.sudo(polkadotXcm.send) extrinsic with dest set to the relay chain (parents=1, interior=Here). Construct the XCM message with these instructions: (1) WithdrawAsset - withdraw DOT from sovereign account (id: parents=1, interior=Here, amount in Fungible), (2) BuyExecution - fees matching the withdrawn amount with weightLimit=Unlimited, (3) Transact - originKind=Native, requireWeightAtMost from step 3, call=encoded data from step 2, (4) RefundSurplus - reclaim unused fees, (5) DepositAsset - assets=Wild(All), beneficiary=parachain sovereign account (parents=0, interior=X1(Parachain: PARA_ID)). Submit the transaction.
steps.expected_output: Transaction finalized on the parachain; sudo.Sudid event emitted successfully.

steps.description: After the transaction is finalized and the XCM is processed on the relay chain, repeat the query from step 1. In Polkadot.js Apps connected to the relay chain, query registrar > paras with your parachain ID. The locked field should now be false.
steps.expected_output: Query result shows locked: false, confirming the parachain has been successfully unlocked.

error_patterns.cause: The parachain is already unlocked; the removeLock extrinsic is unnecessary.
error_patterns.resolution: Query registrar.paras to confirm the current lock status before proceeding.

error_patterns.cause: The parachain sovereign account on the relay chain lacks funds for XCM execution.
error_patterns.resolution: Transfer additional relay chain tokens to the sovereign account. Calculate the required amount using the XCM Payment API.

error_patterns.cause: The XCM was not sent with a valid parachain origin, or the polkadotXcm.send call was not dispatched with root privileges.
error_patterns.resolution: Ensure the call is wrapped in sudo.sudo or submitted through the parachain's governance mechanism.

error_patterns.cause: The relay chain XCM barrier rejected the message, possibly due to incorrect fee asset or weight configuration.
error_patterns.resolution: Verify WithdrawAsset uses the correct relay chain native token location and that BuyExecution specifies sufficient fees.

supplementary_context.description: Context on parachain locking mechanisms, XCM construction, and related maintenance operations.

pages.relevance: After unlocking, parachains migrating from legacy leases may need to set up coretime renewal.
XCM fundamentals used in constructing the unlock message.

skills.title: Interact with Polkadot Chains Using Dedot JavaScript Client
skills.objective: Install the Dedot JavaScript client, initialize a connection to a Polkadot SDK-based chain, read on-chain data (storage, constants, runtime APIs), and sign and send transactions.

network: WebSocket RPC endpoint for a Polkadot SDK-based chain (e.g., wss://rpc.polkadot.io for Polkadot, or wss://services.polkadothub-rpc.com/testnet for Polkadot Hub TestNet)
tokens: Tokens on the target chain if submitting transactions (e.g., DOT on Polkadot, or PAS from https://faucet.polkadot.io/ for TestNet)

env_vars.name: WS_ENDPOINT
env_vars.description: WebSocket RPC endpoint URL for the target Polkadot SDK-based chain

steps.description: Create a new Node.js project directory and install the Dedot client library along with the optional chaintypes package for TypeScript autocompletion.
commands: mkdir dedot-demo && cd dedot-demo && npm init -y
steps.expected_output: Dependencies installed successfully without errors.
Client connects and is ready for queries.

steps.description:
Use the client to read chain state via multiple patterns:

- Runtime constants: `client.consts.<pallet>.<constantName>`
- Storage queries: `client.query.<pallet>.<storageEntry>` (e.g., `client.query.system.account('ADDRESS')`)
- Runtime APIs: `client.call.<runtimeApi>.<methodName>`
- Events: `client.events.<pallet>.<eventName>`

Example storage query:
```typescript
const balance = await client.query.system.account('INSERT_ADDRESS');
console.log('Balance:', balance.data.free);
```
steps.expected_output: Chain data values printed to console (balance, constants, etc.).
Transaction status updates printed, ending with 'Finalized' and a block hash.

error_patterns.cause: Dedot package not installed in the project
error_patterns.resolution: Run `npm i dedot` in the project directory.

error_patterns.cause: Incorrect or unreachable RPC endpoint
error_patterns.resolution: Verify the WebSocket URL is correct and the node is running. Try a public endpoint like wss://rpc.polkadot.io.

error_patterns.cause: @dedot/chaintypes not installed as dev dependency
error_patterns.resolution: Run `npm i -D @dedot/chaintypes` or generate types with `npx dedot chaintypes -w <WS_URL>`.

supplementary_context.description: Dedot is a next-gen JavaScript client for Polkadot SDK chains, lightweight and tree-shakable with full TypeScript support. It supports both new and legacy JSON-RPC APIs and light client (Smoldot) connectivity.

pages.relevance: Primary source page with full API examples for client init, storage queries, constants, runtime APIs, events, and transactions

skills.title: Interact with Polkadot Chains Using Python Substrate Interface
skills.objective: Install the Python Substrate Interface library, connect to a Polkadot SDK-based node, query on-chain storage state, and submit signed transactions.

network: WebSocket RPC endpoint for a Polkadot SDK-based chain (e.g., wss://rpc.polkadot.io for Polkadot, or ws://127.0.0.1:9944 for a local node)
tokens: Tokens on the target chain if submitting transactions (e.g., DOT on Polkadot, or PAS from https://faucet.polkadot.io/ for TestNet)

env_vars.name: WS_ENDPOINT
env_vars.description: WebSocket RPC endpoint URL for the target Polkadot SDK-based chain

steps.description: Install the substrate-interface package using pip. It is recommended to use a virtual environment.
steps.expected_output: Package installed successfully.

steps.description:
Create a Python script that establishes a WebSocket connection to a Polkadot SDK-based node using SubstrateInterface.

```python
from substrateinterface import SubstrateInterface

substrate = SubstrateInterface(
    url="wss://rpc.polkadot.io"  # Or ws://127.0.0.1:9944 for local
)

print(f"Connected to chain: {substrate.chain}")
```
steps.expected_output: Prints the connected chain name (e.g., 'Polkadot').

steps.description:
Query on-chain storage using the substrate.query() method. Specify the pallet name, storage function, and any required parameters.

```python
account_info = substrate.query(
    module="System",
    storage_function="Account",
    params=["INSERT_SS58_ADDRESS"],
)

free_balance = account_info.value["data"]["free"]
reserved = account_info.value["data"]["reserved"]
nonce = account_info.value["nonce"]

print(f"Free Balance: {free_balance}")
print(f"Reserved: {reserved}")
print(f"Nonce: {nonce}")
```
steps.expected_output: Account balance, reserved amount, and nonce printed to console.
Transaction receipt with extrinsic hash and block hash on success.

error_patterns.cause: substrate-interface package not installed
error_patterns.resolution: Run `pip install substrate-interface`.

error_patterns.cause: Node is not running or WebSocket endpoint is incorrect
error_patterns.resolution: Verify the node is running and the WebSocket URL is correct (e.g., ws://127.0.0.1:9944 for local).

error_patterns.cause: Transaction failed on-chain, typically due to insufficient balance or invalid parameters
error_patterns.resolution: Check the account has sufficient balance to cover the transfer amount plus fees. Verify the recipient address and amount.

supplementary_context.description: The Python Substrate Interface enables interaction with Polkadot SDK-based chains from Python, supporting storage queries, extrinsic composition, SCALE codec, and metadata interaction.

skills.title: Set Up and Use Substrate Sidecar REST API
skills.objective: Install the Substrate API Sidecar REST service, connect it to a Polkadot SDK-based node, and query chain data through standardized REST endpoints.

network: A running Polkadot SDK-based node — either a local node (default ws://127.0.0.1:9944) or a remote node with a WebSocket RPC endpoint (e.g., wss://polkadot-asset-hub-rpc.polkadot.io)

env_vars.name: SAS_SUBSTRATE_URL
env_vars.description: WebSocket URL of the Polkadot SDK-based node for Sidecar to connect to. Defaults to ws://127.0.0.1:9944 if not set.

steps.description: Install Sidecar globally so the `substrate-api-sidecar` command is available system-wide.
steps.expected_output: Installation completes. Verify with `substrate-api-sidecar --version`.

steps.action: Start Sidecar connected to a node
steps.description:
Start Sidecar, connecting to either a local node (default) or a remote node via the SAS_SUBSTRATE_URL environment variable.

For a local node:
```bash
substrate-api-sidecar
```

For a remote node (e.g., Polkadot Asset Hub):
```bash
SAS_SUBSTRATE_URL=wss://polkadot-asset-hub-rpc.polkadot.io substrate-api-sidecar
```

Sidecar starts an Express server on http://127.0.0.1:8080 by default.
steps.expected_output: Log output showing configuration summary and 'Listening on http://127.0.0.1:8080/' with the connected chain name.

steps.description:
With Sidecar running, query the REST endpoints using curl, Postman, or a browser.

Example — get node version:
```bash
curl -X 'GET' 'http://127.0.0.1:8080/node/version' -H 'accept: application/json'
```

Other useful endpoints include:
- `/blocks/{blockNumber}` — get block details
- `/accounts/{accountId}/balance-info` — get account balance
- `/transaction/material` — get transaction construction material
- `/pallets/{palletId}/storage` — query pallet storage
- `/` — list all available endpoints

Full endpoint documentation: https://paritytech.github.io/substrate-api-sidecar/dist/
steps.expected_output: JSON response with node version information (e.g., clientVersion, clientImplName, chain).

error_patterns.cause: Sidecar not installed globally
error_patterns.resolution: Run `npm install -g @substrate/api-sidecar`.

error_patterns.cause: No Polkadot SDK node running at the default or specified WebSocket endpoint
error_patterns.resolution: Ensure a node is running and accessible. For remote nodes, set the SAS_SUBSTRATE_URL environment variable to the correct WebSocket URL.

error_patterns.cause: Sidecar is not running when attempting to query endpoints
error_patterns.resolution: Start Sidecar first with `substrate-api-sidecar` before making REST API requests.

supplementary_context.description: Substrate API Sidecar provides a REST interface for Polkadot SDK chains, acting as a caching layer between applications and nodes with standardized endpoints for blocks, accounts, transactions, and more.

skills.title: Interact with Polkadot Chains Using Polkadart Dart SDK

steps.description:
Create a new Dart project and add the Polkadart packages. This SDK is Dart-native and designed for Dart/Flutter developers.

Core packages:
```bash
dart pub add polkadart polkadart_cli polkadart_keyring
```

All packages (includes SCALE codec, crypto, SS58, and more):
```bash
dart pub add polkadart polkadart_cli polkadart_keyring polkadart_scale_codec secp256k1_ecdsa sr25519 ss58 substrate_bip39 substrate_metadata
```
commands: cd polkadart_demo && dart pub add polkadart polkadart_cli polkadart_keyring

steps.description:
Add a polkadart configuration block to pubspec.yaml to specify which chains to generate types for:

```yaml
polkadart:
  output_dir: lib/generated
  chains:
    polkadot: wss://rpc.polkadot.io
```

Then run the code generator:
```bash
dart run polkadart_cli:generate -v
```

This generates type-safe Dart bindings in lib/generated/ from the chain's runtime metadata.
steps.expected_output: Type definitions generated in lib/generated/ directory.

error_patterns.resolution: Add the polkadart config block to pubspec.yaml with output_dir and chains entries, then run `dart run polkadart_cli:generate -v`.

error_patterns.resolution: Verify the RPC endpoint URL is correct and accessible.
